title: scSidekick - Getting Started: PrepObject, ShowColors, GetColors
text: scSidekick built around one idea: configure project , every downstream step inherits configuration automatically - just colors layouts, full reproducibility trail. single setup call, following come free byproducts ordinary plotting analysis: Consistent, publication-ready figures - one palette factor ordering shared across every plot, single-cell spatial alike, repeated scale_color_manual() group.arguments. plain-text .legend sidecar written next every saved figure, describing exactly plotted - never reverse-engineer figure paper time (see Reporting & Utilities). draft methods paragraph reconstructed Seurat command history ExtractMethods(). recoverable session log - start_session_logger() captures commands figures session interrupted analysis can rebuilt, summarize_r_session() turns clean report runnable R script. one-call slide deck - create_analysis_pptx() assembles saved figures sectioned PowerPoint next lab meeting. PrepObject() engine makes possible. Call clustering stores color palette, factor-level ordering, output directory, default plotting parameters directly inside Seurat object seurat_object@misc$nk_settings. Every downstream scSidekick function reads slot arguments left NULL - set project’s look behavior single time, never repeat . Learn PrepObject() ; rest package builds . short: run PrepObject() -> consistent figures, auto-written legends, methods paragraph, recoverable session log, slide deck follow automatically.

title: scSidekick - Getting Started: PrepObject, ShowColors, GetColors
text: use bmcite, bone marrow CITE-seq dataset SeuratData. small (~30 k cells) already comes rich metadata - lane, donor, celltype.l1, celltype.l2 - can demonstrate multi-variable workflows without manufacturing fake columns.
code: if (!require("devtools", quietly = TRUE)) install.packages("devtools") devtools::install_github("nourabdelfattah/scSidekick")

title: scSidekick - Getting Started: PrepObject, ShowColors, GetColors
text: PrepObject() registers one metadata variables assigns level distinct color scSidekick’s built-palette. Pass character vector handle grouping columns . call bmcite@misc$nk_settings holds color map variable. variable gets independent palette levels stored ordered factors, axes legends always appear consistent order. Inspect ShowColors() GetColors() (see ).
code: bmcite <- PrepObject(bmcite,   variables = c("lane", "donor", "celltype.l1", "celltype.l2"))

title: scSidekick - Getting Started: PrepObject, ShowColors, GetColors
text: group., split., output_dir, object_name, subset_name store package-wide defaults every plotting function inherits arguments left NULL. stored, bare PlotDimPlots(bmcite) group cell type, split donor, save specified folder - repeated arguments.
code: bmcite <- PrepObject(bmcite,   variables   = c("lane", "donor", "celltype.l1", "celltype.l2"),   group.by    = "celltype.l1",   split.by    = "donor",   output_dir  = "./bmcite_Analysis/Figures",   object_name = "bmcite",   subset_name = "AllCells")

title: scSidekick - Getting Started: PrepObject, ShowColors, GetColors
text: can add new variables (like seurat_clusters) PrepObject later, running clustering pipeline. process object use Determine_nDims() pick number dimensions automatically. Determine_nDims() applies two heuristics PCA variance explained returns conservative (lower) two. Blue numbers selected dimensions; orange discarded.
code: result <- Determine_nDims(bmcite, reduction = "pca") result$plot

title: scSidekick - Getting Started: PrepObject, ShowColors, GetColors
text: ShowColors() renders swatch grid every variable stored nk_settings - one panel per variable, levels y-axis. fastest sanity check generating figures.
code: ShowColors(bmcite)

title: scSidekick - Getting Started: PrepObject, ShowColors, GetColors
text: Override variable’s palette named vector. level omit falls back auto-palette, specify colors care .
code: my_cols <- c("CD4 T" = "#E41A1C", "CD8 T" = "#377EB8", "NK" = "#4DAF4A",              "B"     = "#984EA3", "Mono"  = "#FF7F00")  bmcite <- PrepObject(bmcite,   variables     = "celltype.l1",   custom_colors = list(celltype.l1 = my_cols))

title: scSidekick - Getting Started: PrepObject, ShowColors, GetColors
text: var_levels sets display order variable, controlling legend entries, axis ticks, split panels. Levels don’t list appended end.
code: bmcite <- PrepObject(bmcite,   variables  = c("celltype.l1", "donor"),   var_levels = list(     celltype.l1 = c("CD4 T", "CD8 T", "NK", "B", "Mono", "DC", "other T"),     donor       = c("d1", "d2", "d3", "d4", "d5", "d6", "d7", "d8")   ))

title: scSidekick - Getting Started: PrepObject, ShowColors, GetColors
text: GetColors() returns named color vector variable, ready pass scale_color_manual() scSidekick colors argument.
code: # Single variable ct_cols <- GetColors(bmcite, "celltype.l1") ct_cols ##           B cell          Mono/DC               NK Progenitor cells  ##        "#003F5C"        "#74599E"        "#E46388"        "#F77B38"  ##           T cell  ##        "#8DB032" # All registered variables at once all_cols <- GetColors(bmcite) names(all_cols) ## [1] "lane"            "donor"           "celltype.l1"     "celltype.l2"     ## [5] "seurat_clusters"

title: scSidekick - Getting Started: PrepObject, ShowColors, GetColors
text: PrepObject() run, subsequent calls skip re-computation unless pass force = TRUE. Use adding new metadata changing cluster assignments.
code: bmcite$Assignment <- dplyr::case_when(   bmcite$celltype.l1 == "CD4 T" ~ "Helper T",   bmcite$celltype.l1 == "CD8 T" ~ "Cytotoxic T",   TRUE ~ bmcite$celltype.l1 )  bmcite <- PrepObject(bmcite,   variables = c("celltype.l1", "celltype.l2", "seurat_clusters", "Assignment"),   force     = TRUE)

title: scSidekick - Getting Started: PrepObject, ShowColors, GetColors
text: reference workflow real multi-sample project. Every step uses scSidekick function; Seurat processing steps middle standard. Nothing runs - template copy adapt.
code: library(scSidekick) library(Seurat) library(harmony)  # ── 1. Load raw 10x data ───────────────────────────────────────────────────── SeuratObj <- LoadSamplesRNA(   sample_dirs  = list.files("path/to/10x_dirs", full.names = TRUE),   sample_names = c("HC1", "HC2", "AD1", "AD2"),   metadata     = data.frame(     Sample = c("HC1", "HC2", "AD1", "AD2"),     Group  = c("HC",  "HC",  "AD",  "AD"),     Batch  = c(1, 1, 2, 2)   ) )  # ── 2. QC ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── PlotQCMetrics(SeuratObj, group.by = "Sample", output_dir = "./Figures/QC") # Inspect the QC plots, then filter: SeuratObj <- subset(SeuratObj, subset = DoubletStatus == "singlet" & miQC.keep == "keep")  # ── 3. Normalize & reduce ───────────────────────────────────────────────────── SeuratObj <- NormalizeData(SeuratObj) %>%   FindVariableFeatures(nfeatures = 2000) %>%   ScaleData(vars.to.regress = c("S.Score", "G2M.Score", "percent.mt")) %>%   RunPCA(npcs = 50)  # ── 4. (Optional) Harmony batch correction ──────────────────────────────────── SeuratObj <- harmony::RunHarmony(SeuratObj, group.by.vars = c("Sample", "Batch"))  # ── 5. Choose dimensions ────────────────────────────────────────────────────── dims <- Determine_nDims(SeuratObj, reduction = "harmony")  # or "pca" dims$plot   # inspect the elbow  SeuratObj <- RunUMAP(SeuratObj,       dims = 1:dims$n_dims, reduction = "harmony") SeuratObj <- FindNeighbors(SeuratObj, dims = 1:dims$n_dims, reduction = "harmony") SeuratObj <- FindClusters(SeuratObj,  resolution = 0.5)  # ── 6. Register colors & defaults ───────────────────────────────────────────── SeuratObj <- PrepObject(SeuratObj,   variables   = c("Sample", "Group", "seurat_clusters"),   group.by    = "seurat_clusters",   split.by    = "Sample",   output_dir  = "./Figures",   object_name = "MyProject") ShowColors(SeuratObj)  # ── 7. Explore & visualize ──────────────────────────────────────────────────── PlotDimPlots(SeuratObj,   group.by   = c("seurat_clusters", "Sample", "Group"),   show_trend = TRUE)  # ── 8. Marker genes & annotation ────────────────────────────────────────────── GroupHeatmap(SeuratObj, group.by = "seurat_clusters")  markers_df <- data.frame(   Genes    = c("CD3E", "CD4", "CD8A", "NKG7", "CD79A", "LYZ", "S100A8"),   CellType = c("T",    "T",   "T",    "NK",   "B",     "Mono","Mono") ) SplitDotPlot(SeuratObj, markers_df = markers_df, group.by = "seurat_clusters")  de <- RunWilcoxAUC(SeuratObj, group.by = "seurat_clusters") SeuratObj <- CellTypeAssignmentHelper(SeuratObj,   markers        = de,   cluster_column = "seurat_clusters",   sctype_tissues = "Immune system",   output_dir     = "./Figures/Annotation")  # ── 9. Finalize labels, update PrepObject ───────────────────────────────────── SeuratObj$CellType <- dplyr::recode(SeuratObj$seurat_clusters,   `0` = "CD4 T", `1` = "Monocyte", `2` = "NK", `3` = "B", ...)  SeuratObj <- PrepObject(SeuratObj,   variables = c("seurat_clusters", "Sample", "Group", "CellType"),   group.by  = "CellType",   force     = TRUE)  PlotDimPlots(SeuratObj, group.by = c("CellType", "Sample", "Group"), show_trend = TRUE)  # ── 10. Feature maps ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── PlotFeaturePlots(SeuratObj,   features = c("CD3E", "CD4", "CD8A", "NKG7", "CD79A", "LYZ"),   split.by = "Sample")  # ── 11. Composition ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── PlotTrendLabeled(SeuratObj,   stat.by  = "CellType",   group.by = "Group",   split.by = "Sample")  # ── 12. Pathway analysis ────────────────────────────────────────────────────── RunGSEA(SeuratObj,   group.by   = "CellType",   split.by   = NULL,   output_dir = "./Figures/Pathways")  # ── 13. Cell-cell communication ─────────────────────────────────────────────── cc_results <- RunCellChat(SeuratObj,   cell.by    = "CellType",   group.by   = "Group",   groups     = c("HC", "AD"),   output_dir = "./Figures/CellChat")  CompareCellChat(   cellchat_list = cc_results,   output_dir    = "./Figures/CellChat/Comparison")  # ── 14. Assemble slide deck ─────────────────────────────────────────────────── create_analysis_pptx(output_dir = "./Figures", object_name = "MyProject")

title: scSidekick - Getting Started: PrepObject, ShowColors, GetColors
text: Vignette 02: Determine_nDims() context dimensionality reduction Vignette 03: PlotDimPlots() - UMAP visualization using colors PrepObject Vignette 07: Spatial Transcriptomics - settings drive combined single-cell spatial figures (PlotMasterMaps, GenerateMasterGeneMaps) Vignette 12: Reporting & Utilities - reproducibility layer full: ExtractMethods(), auto-written .legend sidecars, session logger, create_analysis_pptx(), Nour_pal() / Nour_cols() palette utilities

title: scSidekick - Dimensionality Reduction: Determine_nDims
text: Choosing many dimensions retain PCA (LSI, ICA, etc.) one consequential decisions single-cell pipeline. lose biological signal; many add noise. Determine_nDims() automates using two complementary heuristics returns conservative (lower) two - safe default can override plot suggests otherwise.

title: scSidekick - Dimensionality Reduction: Determine_nDims
text: console output shows heuristics final selection. plot displays every PC number - blue = selected, orange = discarded. Vertical lines mark two cutoffs.
code: dims <- Determine_nDims(bmcite, reduction = "pca") dims$plot

title: scSidekick - Dimensionality Reduction: Determine_nDims
text: dims$n_dims plain integer: pass dims = 1:dims$n_dims downstream Seurat calls guarantee consistency.
code: # Recommended number of dimensions dims$n_dims ## [1] 18 # Heuristic 1 - cumulative variance plateau dims$cum_thresh ## [1] 42 # Heuristic 2 - elbow dims$elbow_thresh ## [1] 18

title: scSidekick - Dimensionality Reduction: Determine_nDims
text: , bmcite UMAP embedding cluster assignments ready visualization functions vignettes 03-05.
code: bmcite <- RunUMAP(bmcite,       dims = 1:dims$n_dims) bmcite <- FindNeighbors(bmcite, dims = 1:dims$n_dims) bmcite <- FindClusters(bmcite,  resolution = 0.5) ## Modularity Optimizer version 1.3.0 by Ludo Waltman and Nees Jan van Eck ##  ## Number of nodes: 30672 ## Number of edges: 962789 ##  ## Running Louvain algorithm... ## Maximum modularity in 10 random starts: 0.9093 ## Number of communities: 15 ## Elapsed time: 3 seconds

title: scSidekick - Dimensionality Reduction: Determine_nDims
text: defaults (threshold_cum_pct = 90, threshold_pct = 5) work well datasets. Adjust elbow plot looks unusual.
code: # Stricter: require 85 % cumulative variance, per-dim contribution < 3 % dims_strict <- Determine_nDims(bmcite,   threshold_cum_pct = 85,   threshold_pct     = 3) dims_strict$n_dims  # More permissive: 95 % cumulative, 1 % marginal drop dims_loose <- Determine_nDims(bmcite,   threshold_cum_pct = 95,   threshold_pct     = 1) dims_loose$n_dims

title: scSidekick - Dimensionality Reduction: Determine_nDims
text: function works named reduction slot - PCA, LSI (ATAC), ICA, Harmony.
code: # After RunHarmony(): library(harmony) bmcite <- RunHarmony(bmcite, group.by.vars = "donor") dims_h <- Determine_nDims(bmcite, reduction = "harmony")

title: scSidekick - Dimensionality Reduction: Determine_nDims
text: dims$plot standard ggplot2 object.
code: ggplot2::ggsave(   filename = "bmcite_elbow.pdf",   plot     = dims$plot,   width    = 6, height = 4)

title: scSidekick - Dimensionality Reduction: Determine_nDims
text: Vignette 01: PrepObject - run clustering register colors Vignette 03: PlotDimPlots - UMAP visualization RunUMAP(dims = 1:dims$n_dims)

title: scSidekick - UMAP Visualization: PlotDimPlots
text: PlotDimPlots() generates grid UMAP (reduction) scatter plots. group.controls metadata column colors cell split.creates one column panels per level (one panel per donor, sample, condition, etc.) row.creates one row panels per level Pass vector group.scSidekick iterates, producing one complete figure per variable Colors come PrepObject automatically - need pass manually.

title: scSidekick - UMAP Visualization: PlotDimPlots
code: PlotDimPlots(bmcite, group.by = "celltype.l1",split.by = NULL,legendnrow = 1)

title: scSidekick - UMAP Visualization: PlotDimPlots
text: split.= "donor" creates one panel per donor. panel shows donor’s cells, colored cell type. legend shared.
code: PlotDimPlots(bmcite,   group.by = "celltype.l1",legendnrow = 1,   split.by = "donor")

title: scSidekick - UMAP Visualization: PlotDimPlots
text: show_trend = TRUE appends stacked bar chart left showing proportional cell-type composition per split group.
code: PlotDimPlots(bmcite,   group.by   = "celltype.l1",   split.by   = "donor",legendnrow = 1,   show_trend = TRUE)

title: scSidekick - UMAP Visualization: PlotDimPlots
text: show_labels = TRUE overlays level name centroid group, avoiding legend look-.
code: PlotDimPlots(bmcite,   group.by    = "celltype.l1",legendnrow = 1,   show_labels = TRUE)

what: 5. Multiple group.by - one figure per variable
title: scSidekick - UMAP Visualization: PlotDimPlots
text: Pass character vector produce one complete figure per grouping variable. primary way compare different annotations map onto embedding.
code: p=PlotDimPlots(bmcite,   group.by = c("celltype.l1", "celltype.l2")) patchwork::wrap_plots(p,ncol = 1)

title: scSidekick - UMAP Visualization: PlotDimPlots
text: row.creates 2D grid: rows = levels row., columns = levels split.. Use compare two categorical cuts simultaneously.
code: PlotDimPlots(bmcite,   group.by = "celltype.l1",   row.by = "donor",   split.by   = "lane")

title: scSidekick - UMAP Visualization: PlotDimPlots
text: Override PrepObject defaults passing named vector colors.
code: my_cols <- c(   "CD4 T"  = "#003f5c",   "CD8 T"  = "#227ed4",   "NK"     = "#a05195",   "B"      = "#d45087",   "Mono"   = "#ffa600",   "DC"     = "#42a665" )  PlotDimPlots(bmcite,   group.by = "celltype.l1",   colors   = my_cols)

title: scSidekick - UMAP Visualization: PlotDimPlots
text: split.col.levels enforces specific column order. Useful donors samples meaningful biological order (e.g. time points).
code: PlotDimPlots(bmcite,   group.by         = "celltype.l1",   split.by         = "donor",   split.col.levels = c("batch2", "batch1"))

title: scSidekick - UMAP Visualization: PlotDimPlots
text: Supply output_dir object_name auto-save every figure PDF. filename constructed object_name, group., split..
code: PlotDimPlots(bmcite,   group.by    = c("celltype.l1", "celltype.l2", "seurat_clusters"),   split.by    = "donor",   show_trend  = TRUE,   output_dir  = "./bmcite_Figures",   object_name = "bmcite",   subset_name = "AllCells")

title: scSidekick - UMAP Visualization: PlotDimPlots
text: Vignette 01: PrepObject - set default group., split., colors Vignette 02: Determine_nDims - choose dims running RunUMAP() Vignette 04: PlotFeaturePlots - gene expression overlaid layout Vignette 10: PlotTrendLabeled - standalone composition figure

title: scSidekick - Feature Expression: PlotFeaturePlots & PlotMultiFeature
text: Two functions visualizing gene expression UMAP (reduction): PlotFeaturePlots() - one panel per gene × split group. Saves disk convention; alias GenerateFeatureMaps(). PlotMultiFeature() - co-expression several genes single panel (gene mode, intersection mode, continuous scale mode).

title: scSidekick - Feature Expression: PlotFeaturePlots & PlotMultiFeature
code: PlotFeaturePlots(bmcite,   features = "CD3E")

what: 2. Multiple genes - one row per gene
title: scSidekick - Feature Expression: PlotFeaturePlots & PlotMultiFeature
code: PlotFeaturePlots(bmcite,   features = c("CD3E", "CD79A", "LYZ"))

what: 3. Split by donor - one column per donor
title: scSidekick - Feature Expression: PlotFeaturePlots & PlotMultiFeature
text: split.creates one column per level. Combined multiple features, produces gene × donor grid. Pain point fixed. Seurat’s FeaturePlot(..., split.=) renders panels colour legend - can’t tell colours mean, common & RestoreLegend() workaround attaches guide doesn’t match data. PlotFeaturePlots() keeps one shared, accurate colour scale across every panel, expression directly comparable across conditions.
code: PlotFeaturePlots(bmcite,   features = c("CD3E", "NKG7", "CD79A"),   split.by = "donor")

title: scSidekick - Feature Expression: PlotFeaturePlots & PlotMultiFeature
text: label = TRUE prints cell-type labels cluster centroids, can immediately connect expression hotspots identity.
code: PlotFeaturePlots(bmcite,   features = c("CD3E", "CD79A"),   label    = TRUE,   label.by = "celltype.l1")

title: scSidekick - Feature Expression: PlotFeaturePlots & PlotMultiFeature
text: Passing vector split.generates one complete figure per split variable.
code: PlotFeaturePlots(bmcite,   features = c("CD3E", "NKG7", "CD79A"),   split.by = c("lane"),row.by = "donor",layout_method = "metadata")

title: scSidekick - Feature Expression: PlotFeaturePlots & PlotMultiFeature
text: gene gets distinct color. Cells expressing gene threshold colored accordingly; cells expressing one gene get blended color. single UMAP panel shows lineages .
code: PlotMultiFeature(bmcite,   features = c("CD3E", "CD79A", "LYZ", "NKG7") )

title: scSidekick - Feature Expression: PlotFeaturePlots & PlotMultiFeature
text: Highlights cells co-express listed genes cutoff. Useful identifying rare dual-positive populations.
code: PlotMultiFeature(bmcite,   features           = c("CD3E", "CD8A"),   plot_mode          = "intersection",   pct_cutoff         = 0.5,   intersection_label = "CD3+CD8+ T cells")

title: scSidekick - Feature Expression: PlotFeaturePlots & PlotMultiFeature
text: Plots single gene (averaged signature) continuous gradient. Equivalent FeaturePlot() integrated scSidekick saving pipeline.
code: PlotMultiFeature(bmcite,   features  = "CD3E",   plot_mode = "scale")

title: scSidekick - Feature Expression: PlotFeaturePlots & PlotMultiFeature
text: Vignette 03: PlotDimPlots - cell-type UMAP without expression overlay Vignette 05: GroupHeatmap, FastDotPlot - expression across clusters Vignette 07: PlotSpatialFeaturePlots - concept spatial data

title: scSidekick - Heatmaps & Dot Plots: GroupHeatmap, SplitDotPlot, FastDotPlot
text: Three functions comparing gene expression across groups: GroupHeatmap() - ComplexHeatmap average expression per group, optional dot-size overlay, gene-category annotations. SplitDotPlot() - dot plot organized cell-type gene blocks; accepts markers_df data frame. FastDotPlot() - quick dot plot explicit gene list regex pattern, markers_df required.

title: scSidekick - Heatmaps & Dot Plots: GroupHeatmap, SplitDotPlot, FastDotPlot
text: column one cell type; row one gene. Color = scaled mean expression (z-score across groups). Column colors top come PrepObject.
code: GroupHeatmap(bmcite,   group.by = "celltype.l1",   features = bm_markers)

title: scSidekick - Heatmaps & Dot Plots: GroupHeatmap, SplitDotPlot, FastDotPlot
text: feature_split named vector (names = genes, values = category labels). Genes grouped labeled row blocks.
code: feature_split <- setNames(markers_df$CellType, markers_df$Genes)  GroupHeatmap(bmcite,   group.by      = "celltype.l1",   features      = names(feature_split),   feature_split = feature_split)

title: scSidekick - Heatmaps & Dot Plots: GroupHeatmap, SplitDotPlot, FastDotPlot
text: add_dot = TRUE overlays circles sized percent-expressed top color-encoded expression - dot-heatmap hybrid.
code: GroupHeatmap(bmcite,   group.by = "celltype.l1",   features = bm_markers,   add_dot  = TRUE,   add_bg   = TRUE)

title: scSidekick - Heatmaps & Dot Plots: GroupHeatmap, SplitDotPlot, FastDotPlot
code: GroupHeatmap(bmcite,   group.by = c("celltype.l1", "seurat_clusters"),   features = bm_markers)

title: scSidekick - Heatmaps & Dot Plots: GroupHeatmap, SplitDotPlot, FastDotPlot
code: GroupHeatmap(bmcite,   group.by      = "celltype.l1",   features      = names(feature_split),   feature_split = feature_split,   add_dot       = TRUE,   output_dir    = "./bmcite_Figures",   object_name   = "bmcite")

title: scSidekick - Heatmaps & Dot Plots: GroupHeatmap, SplitDotPlot, FastDotPlot
text: SplitDotPlot accepts markers_df data frame (gene + cell-type column) organizes genes annotated blocks along x-axis.

title: scSidekick - Heatmaps & Dot Plots: GroupHeatmap, SplitDotPlot, FastDotPlot
text: Clusters x-axis, grouped CellType block; genes y-axis. Dot color = scaled mean expression; dot size = percent-expressed.
code: SplitDotPlot(bmcite,   markers_df = markers_df,   group.by   = "celltype.l1")

title: scSidekick - Heatmaps & Dot Plots: GroupHeatmap, SplitDotPlot, FastDotPlot
text: Adding split.stacks one dot plot per level, enabling direct expression comparison across conditions batches.
code: SplitDotPlot(bmcite,   markers_df = markers_df,   group.by   = "celltype.l1",   split.by   = "donor")

title: scSidekick - Heatmaps & Dot Plots: GroupHeatmap, SplitDotPlot, FastDotPlot
text: SplitDotPlot2 adds hierarchical clustering within gene blocks.
code: SplitDotPlot2(bmcite,   markers_df    = markers_df,   group.by      = "celltype.l1",   ClusterGenes  = TRUE,   min.pct.exp   = 10)

title: scSidekick - Heatmaps & Dot Plots: GroupHeatmap, SplitDotPlot, FastDotPlot
text: FastDotPlot quick path don’t need cell-type block annotations.

title: scSidekick - Heatmaps & Dot Plots: GroupHeatmap, SplitDotPlot, FastDotPlot
code: FastDotPlot(bmcite,   features = c("CD3E", "CD8A", "NKG7", "CD79A", "LYZ", "CD14"),   group.by = "celltype.l1")

what: 2. Regex pattern - stop looking up exact gene names
title: scSidekick - Heatmaps & Dot Plots: GroupHeatmap, SplitDotPlot, FastDotPlot
text: Pain point fixed. rarely remember every gene symbol family. Instead hand-typing c("CD3D","CD3E","CD3G","CD4","CD8A", ...), give FastDotPlot() regex pattern pulls every matching gene straight rownames(). idea powers pathway keyword search Vignette 08 - describe want, exact name. pattern selects matching genes; k_genes keeps top k variable per cluster.  handy patterns: "^HLA-" (MHC genes), "^IFI|^ISG|^OAS" (interferon response), "^MT-" (mitochondrial). FastDotPlot2() goes - clusters matched genes k labelled “Pattern” groups can run enrichR , loose regex becomes annotated, organised dot plot.
code: FastDotPlot(bmcite,   pattern  = "^CD",          # every gene whose symbol starts with "CD"   group.by = "celltype.l1",   k_genes  = 3)

title: scSidekick - Heatmaps & Dot Plots: GroupHeatmap, SplitDotPlot, FastDotPlot
text: Vignette 04: PlotFeaturePlots - per-gene UMAP expression maps Vignette 06: CellTypeAssignmentHelper - uses markers annotation Vignette 08: RunGSEA - pathway-level analysis

title: scSidekick - Cell Annotation: CellTypeAssignmentHelper
text: CellTypeAssignmentHelper() runs three independent annotation methods single function call saves summary PDF : Running three simultaneously gives majority-vote-style sanity check: two three methods agree, annotation high-confidence; disagree, cluster warrants manual inspection.

title: scSidekick - Cell Annotation: CellTypeAssignmentHelper
code: library(scSidekick) library(Seurat) library(SeuratData) library(dplyr)  data("bmcite") bmcite <- UpdateSeuratObject(bmcite) bmcite <- bmcite %>%   NormalizeData() %>% FindVariableFeatures() %>% ScaleData() %>% RunPCA() dims <- Determine_nDims(bmcite, elbow_plot = FALSE) bmcite <- RunUMAP(bmcite, dims = 1:dims$n_dims) bmcite <- FindNeighbors(bmcite, dims = 1:dims$n_dims) bmcite <- FindClusters(bmcite, resolution = 0.5) bmcite <- PrepObject(bmcite,   variables   = c("lane", "donor", "celltype.l1", "celltype.l2", "seurat_clusters"),   group.by    = "celltype.l1",   split.by    = "donor",   output_dir  = tempdir(),   object_name = "bmcite")

title: scSidekick - Cell Annotation: CellTypeAssignmentHelper
text: CellTypeAssignmentHelper requires pre-computed markers table. fastest approach scSidekick’s RunWilcoxAUC(), BPCells- Seurat-v5-safe wrapper around presto::wilcoxauc() runs Wilcoxon rank-sum test clusters simultaneously. Alternatively, use Seurat’s FindAllMarkers(): CellTypeAssignmentHelper accepts either format; auto-detects column names. RunWilcoxAUC() strongly recommended large datasets runs seconds rather minutes works BPCells-backed Seurat v5 objects.
code: # Fast (recommended): scSidekick's presto wrapper markers <- RunWilcoxAUC(bmcite, group.by = "seurat_clusters")  # Check the structure head(markers) # feature | group | logFC | pct_in | pct_out | padj | ... markers_seurat <- Seurat::FindAllMarkers(bmcite,   only.pos      = TRUE,   min.pct       = 0.25,   logfc.threshold = 0.25)

title: scSidekick - Cell Annotation: CellTypeAssignmentHelper
text: function runs three methods, stores results Seurat object’s meta.data, saves one PDF summary figure per method output_dir. Progress messages printed console method.
code: bmcite <- CellTypeAssignmentHelper(bmcite,   markers          = markers,   cluster_column   = "seurat_clusters",   output_dir       = tempdir(),   sctype_tissues   = c("Immune system"),   run_sctype       = TRUE,   run_rcellmarker  = TRUE,   run_singler      = TRUE,   singler_ref      = "HumanPrimaryCellAtlasData")

title: scSidekick - Cell Annotation: CellTypeAssignmentHelper
text: sctype_tissues argument filters scType database tissues relevant sample. Providing correct tissue dramatically improves annotation accuracy. Pass character vector query multiple tissues simultaneously:
code: # For a brain tumor dataset: query both brain and immune compartments bmcite <- CellTypeAssignmentHelper(bmcite,   markers        = markers,   cluster_column = "seurat_clusters",   output_dir     = tempdir(),   sctype_tissues = c("Brain", "Immune system"))

title: scSidekick - Cell Annotation: CellTypeAssignmentHelper
text: SingleR requires reference expression dataset. scSidekick exposes commonly used references celldex package:
code: # For a detailed immune classification bmcite <- CellTypeAssignmentHelper(bmcite,   markers     = markers,   output_dir  = tempdir(),   run_sctype  = FALSE,   run_singler = TRUE,   singler_ref = "MonacoImmuneData")

title: scSidekick - Cell Annotation: CellTypeAssignmentHelper
text: Set run_* argument FALSE skip method. useful one method slow need quick single-reference annotation.
code: # scType only (fastest, no internet required) bmcite <- CellTypeAssignmentHelper(bmcite,   markers         = markers,   cluster_column  = "seurat_clusters",   output_dir      = tempdir(),   sctype_tissues  = "Immune system",   run_sctype      = TRUE,   run_rcellmarker = FALSE,   run_singler     = FALSE)

title: scSidekick - Cell Annotation: CellTypeAssignmentHelper
text: method produces PDF output_dir named, example: bmcite_scType_annotation.pdf, bmcite_rcellmarker_annotation.pdf, bmcite_SingleR_annotation.pdf. PDF contains: - UMAP colored predicted cell type label method - bar chart showing annotation confidence (scType score, enrichment score, SingleR delta) per cluster - table summarizing top predicted label runner-cluster Use confidence charts identify ambiguous clusters: low scores near-tie top two candidates warrant manual review.

what: 7. Accessing results in the Seurat object
title: scSidekick - Cell Annotation: CellTypeAssignmentHelper
text: function returns, three new columns added bmcite@meta.data: agreement table fast way spot discordant clusters committing final label.
code: # scType predictions head(bmcite$scType_CellType)  # rcellmarker predictions head(bmcite$rcellmarker_CellType)  # SingleR predictions head(bmcite$SingleRAssignment)  # Quick agreement check table(bmcite$scType_CellType, bmcite$SingleRAssignment)

title: scSidekick - Cell Annotation: CellTypeAssignmentHelper
text: reviewing three method outputs, assign final label new column re-run PrepObject() register . PrepObject() force = TRUE assigns distinct color every unique cell type Assignment stores alongside existing cluster colors. subsequent plots use group.= "Assignment" use palette automatically.
code: # Manually assign based on consensus + prior knowledge cluster_labels <- c(   "0" = "CD4 T cell",   "1" = "CD14 Monocyte",   "2" = "CD4 T cell",   "3" = "B cell",   "4" = "CD8 T cell",   "5" = "NK cell",   "6" = "CD16 Monocyte",   "7" = "Dendritic cell",   "8" = "Platelet" )  pbmc$Assignment <- cluster_labels[as.character(bmcite$seurat_clusters)]  # Register the new column - use force = TRUE to update nk_settings bmcite <- PrepObject(bmcite,   variables = c("seurat_clusters", "Sample", "Condition", "Assignment"),   force     = TRUE)  # Confirm ShowColors(bmcite)

title: scSidekick - Cell Annotation: CellTypeAssignmentHelper
text: Vignette 01: PrepObject - register Assignment column annotation Vignette 03: PlotDimPlots - visualize final Assignment coloring UMAP Vignette 05: GroupHeatmap - confirm marker expression aligns assignment

title: scSidekick - Cell Annotation: CellTypeAssignmentHelper
code: sessionInfo()

title: scSidekick - Spatial Transcriptomics: PlotSpatialDimPlots, PlotSpatialFeaturePlots, PlotMasterMaps
text: scSidekick’s three spatial plotting functions mirror scRNA-seq counterparts render expression metadata directly tissue image: PlotSpatialDimPlots() - color spots metadata variable (e.g. cluster, condition, spatial domain). Long-form alias: GenerateSpatialDimMaps(). PlotSpatialFeaturePlots() - color spots gene expression. Long-form alias: GenerateSpatialFeatureMaps(). PlotMasterMaps() - combined figure: tissue image + gene expression panels + cluster-colored UMAP side side. Long-form alias: GenerateMasterGeneMaps(). three functions accept group., split., layout_method, output_dir conventions scRNA-seq equivalents.

title: scSidekick - Spatial Transcriptomics: PlotSpatialDimPlots, PlotSpatialFeaturePlots, PlotMasterMaps
text: PrepObject(), cluster palette stored appear consistently across spatial figures.
code: library(scSidekick) library(Seurat) library(SeuratData)  InstallData("stxBrain") brain <- LoadData("stxBrain", type = "anterior1")  # Standard processing brain <- NormalizeData(brain) brain <- FindVariableFeatures(brain) brain <- ScaleData(brain) brain <- RunPCA(brain) brain <- RunUMAP(brain, dims = 1:30) brain <- FindNeighbors(brain, dims = 1:30) brain <- FindClusters(brain, resolution = 0.5)  # Fake condition column for split demos set.seed(42) brain$Condition <- sample(c("WT","KO"), ncol(brain), replace = TRUE)  # Register colors brain <- PrepObject(brain,   variables   = c("seurat_clusters", "Condition"),   group.by    = "seurat_clusters",   output_dir  = tempdir(),   object_name = "MouseBrain")

title: scSidekick - Spatial Transcriptomics: PlotSpatialDimPlots, PlotSpatialFeaturePlots, PlotMasterMaps
text: spot tissue section colored cluster assignment. tissue H&E image shown grey-scale background. legend maps colors cluster numbers. palette comes PrepObject.
code: PlotSpatialDimPlots(brain,   group.by = "seurat_clusters")

title: scSidekick - Spatial Transcriptomics: PlotSpatialDimPlots, PlotSpatialFeaturePlots, PlotMasterMaps
text: Passing vector group.produces one spatial map per variable. Two figures generated: one showing spatial cluster distribution, one showing spots annotated WT vs KO.
code: PlotSpatialDimPlots(brain,   group.by = c("seurat_clusters", "Condition"))

title: scSidekick - Spatial Transcriptomics: PlotSpatialDimPlots, PlotSpatialFeaturePlots, PlotMasterMaps
text: default Visium spot sizes scaled local spot density. Set uniform_size = TRUE use fixed, equal size spots - useful comparing two sections spot density differs.
code: PlotSpatialDimPlots(brain,   group.by     = "seurat_clusters",   uniform_size = TRUE)

title: scSidekick - Spatial Transcriptomics: PlotSpatialDimPlots, PlotSpatialFeaturePlots, PlotMasterMaps
text: layout_method = "metadata" uses row_var stack multiple spatial maps vertically, one row per level row_var. Useful multiple tissue sections ROIs stored object. row output corresponds one condition level. Columns correspond levels split.provided.
code: PlotSpatialDimPlots(brain,   group.by      = "seurat_clusters",   layout_method = "metadata",   row.by = "Condition")

title: scSidekick - Spatial Transcriptomics: PlotSpatialDimPlots, PlotSpatialFeaturePlots, PlotMasterMaps
code: # Colors from PrepObject (default) PlotSpatialDimPlots(brain,   group.by = "seurat_clusters")  # Override with a custom palette my_cols <- GetColors(brain, "seurat_clusters") my_cols["0"] <- "#FF0000"  # re-color cluster 0  PlotSpatialDimPlots(brain,   group.by = "seurat_clusters",   colors   = my_cols)

title: scSidekick - Spatial Transcriptomics: PlotSpatialDimPlots, PlotSpatialFeaturePlots, PlotMasterMaps
code: PlotSpatialDimPlots(brain,   group.by    = "seurat_clusters",   output_dir  = tempdir(),   object_name = "MouseBrain")

title: scSidekick - Spatial Transcriptomics: PlotSpatialDimPlots, PlotSpatialFeaturePlots, PlotMasterMaps
text: gene generates one spatial map. Spot color encodes normalized expression grey (zero) high-expression color (default: dark red). Hpca marks hippocampal neurons; Ttr marks choroid plexus - appear expected anatomical locations.
code: PlotSpatialFeaturePlots(brain,   features = c("Hpca", "Ttr"))

title: scSidekick - Spatial Transcriptomics: PlotSpatialDimPlots, PlotSpatialFeaturePlots, PlotMasterMaps
text: Rows correspond levels Condition. gene’s spatial pattern shown per condition. layout useful comparing expression biological groups group occupies distinct tissue section object.
code: PlotSpatialFeaturePlots(brain,   features      = c("Hpca", "Ttr"),   layout_method = "metadata",   row.by = "Condition")

title: scSidekick - Spatial Transcriptomics: PlotSpatialDimPlots, PlotSpatialFeaturePlots, PlotMasterMaps
text: Files named gene: e.g. MouseBrain_Anterior1_spatial_Hpca.pdf.
code: PlotSpatialFeaturePlots(brain,   features    = c("Hpca", "Ttr"),   output_dir  = tempdir(),   object_name = "MouseBrain",   subset_name = "Anterior1")

title: scSidekick - Spatial Transcriptomics: PlotSpatialDimPlots, PlotSpatialFeaturePlots, PlotMasterMaps
text: PlotMasterMaps composite figure function: gene assembles multi-panel page containing tissue spatial map, UMAP colored specified group., UMAP colored gene expression - one page, ready supplementary figure lab presentation.

title: scSidekick - Spatial Transcriptomics: PlotSpatialDimPlots, PlotSpatialFeaturePlots, PlotMasterMaps
text: gene produces PDF page three panels arranged horizontally: 1. Spatial expression map (spot colors = expression level) 2. UMAP colored group.(cluster) orientation 3. UMAP colored gene expression lets simultaneously see tissue gene expressed clusters drive expression.
code: PlotMasterMaps(brain,   features   = c("Hpca", "Ttr"),   group.by   = "seurat_clusters",   output_dir = tempdir())

title: scSidekick - Spatial Transcriptomics: PlotSpatialDimPlots, PlotSpatialFeaturePlots, PlotMasterMaps
text: combination gene × group.variable produces panel page. 2 genes 2 group.variables, get 4 pages.
code: PlotMasterMaps(brain,   features   = c("Hpca", "Ttr"),   group.by   = c("seurat_clusters", "Condition"),   output_dir = tempdir())

title: scSidekick - Spatial Transcriptomics: PlotSpatialDimPlots, PlotSpatialFeaturePlots, PlotMasterMaps
text: gene_colors two-element vector: [1] zero-expression color, [2] high-expression color. default grey -> dark red.
code: PlotMasterMaps(brain,   features    = "Hpca",   group.by    = "seurat_clusters",   gene_colors = c("lightgrey", "#006837"),   output_dir  = tempdir(),   object_name = "MouseBrain")

title: scSidekick - Spatial Transcriptomics: PlotSpatialDimPlots, PlotSpatialFeaturePlots, PlotMasterMaps
text: Vignette 01: PrepObject - register spatial cluster colors Vignette 03: PlotDimPlots - UMAP-equivalent scRNA-seq Vignette 04: PlotFeaturePlots - scRNA-seq gene expression UMAP

title: scSidekick - Spatial Transcriptomics: PlotSpatialDimPlots, PlotSpatialFeaturePlots, PlotMasterMaps
code: sessionInfo()

title: scSidekick - Pathway Analysis: RunGSEA
text: RunGSEA() performs gene set enrichment analysis Seurat object. handles full pipeline internally: Subset split.(e.g. one GSEA per broad cell type) Run Wilcoxon DE presto::wilcoxauc(), grouped group.Run fgsea one MSigDB collections Save lollipop plots (top/bottom 10 pathways per group) NES heatmaps pre-computed DE table needed - just pass Seurat object.

title: scSidekick - Pathway Analysis: RunGSEA
code: library(scSidekick) library(Seurat) library(SeuratData) library(dplyr)  data("bmcite") bmcite <- UpdateSeuratObject(bmcite) bmcite <- bmcite %>%   NormalizeData() %>% FindVariableFeatures() %>% ScaleData() %>% RunPCA() dims <- Determine_nDims(bmcite, elbow_plot = FALSE) bmcite <- RunUMAP(bmcite, dims = 1:dims$n_dims) bmcite <- FindNeighbors(bmcite, dims = 1:dims$n_dims) bmcite <- FindClusters(bmcite, resolution = 0.5) bmcite <- PrepObject(bmcite,   variables   = c("lane", "donor", "celltype.l1", "celltype.l2", "seurat_clusters"),   group.by    = "celltype.l1",   split.by    = "donor",   output_dir  = tempdir(),   object_name = "bmcite")

title: scSidekick - Pathway Analysis: RunGSEA
text: group.defines groups compared (e.g. cell types, clusters). split.= NULL runs single GSEA whole object. Set split.broad cell-type column (e.g. "celltype.l1") run GSEA separately within major lineage. default RunGSEA tests four MSigDB collections: Hallmark, KEGG, Reactome, WikiPathways.
code: res <- RunGSEA(bmcite,   group.by   = "celltype.l1",   split.by   = NULL,   output_dir = tempdir())

title: scSidekick - Pathway Analysis: RunGSEA
text: common use: compare detailed subtypes (celltype.l2) within major lineage (celltype.l1). produces one independent GSEA per level celltype.l1. , DE computed celltype.l2 groups pathways ranked NES.
code: res <- RunGSEA(bmcite,   group.by   = "celltype.l2",   split.by   = "celltype.l1",   output_dir = tempdir())

title: scSidekick - Pathway Analysis: RunGSEA
text: Supply combination MSigDB collections via pathway_sets. element needs category optional subcategory. Run msigdbr::msigdbr_collections() see available collections.
code: res <- RunGSEA(bmcite,   group.by     = "celltype.l1",   split.by     = NULL,   output_dir   = tempdir(),   pathway_sets = list(     Hallmark    = list(category = "H"),     ImmuneSigDB = list(category = "C7", subcategory = "IMMUNESIGDB"),     GO_BP       = list(category = "C5", subcategory = "GO:BP")   ))

title: scSidekick - Pathway Analysis: RunGSEA
code: res <- RunGSEA(brain,   group.by   = "seurat_clusters",   split.by   = NULL,   species    = "Mus musculus",   output_dir = tempdir())

title: scSidekick - Pathway Analysis: RunGSEA
text: padj_thresh logfc_thresh filter DE results ranking. top_n controls many pathways appear per group summary heatmaps.
code: res <- RunGSEA(bmcite,   group.by      = "celltype.l1",   split.by      = NULL,   padj_thresh   = 0.01,   logfc_thresh  = 0.25,   top_n         = 10L,   output_dir    = tempdir())

title: scSidekick - Pathway Analysis: RunGSEA
text: resume = TRUE skips subsets output files already exist output_dir. Essential restarting run interrupted.
code: res <- RunGSEA(bmcite,   group.by   = "celltype.l2",   split.by   = "celltype.l1",   output_dir = tempdir(),   resume     = TRUE)

title: scSidekick - Pathway Analysis: RunGSEA
text: RunGSEA returns nested list. top level split split.level ("" split.= NULL), pathway_sets name.
code: # NES heatmap for the Hallmark collection, whole object res$all$Hallmark$heatmap  # Full fgsea results table head(res$all$Hallmark$results)  # Top pathways per group res$all$Hallmark$top_pathways

title: scSidekick - Pathway Analysis: RunGSEA
text: GSEA run returns hundreds pathways long, exact MSigDB names. shouldn’t know apoptosis pathway called HALLMARK_APOPTOSIS. PlotGSEAEnrichment() auto-discovers result CSVs selects pathways keyword search instead. Pain point fixed. Describe biology, exact string. Pass character vector logic, list vectors -groups (’d) - case-insensitive regex.
code: # OR: any pathway mentioning "apoptosis" OR "cell_death" PlotGSEAEnrichment(   output_dir   = "./Figures/Pathways",   search_terms = c("apoptosis", "cell_death"))  # AND-group: pathways mentioning BOTH "interferon" AND "response" PlotGSEAEnrichment(   output_dir   = "./Figures/Pathways",   search_terms = list(c("interferon", "response")))  # Mixed: (T_cell AND activation) OR inflammation PlotGSEAEnrichment(   output_dir   = "./Figures/Pathways",   search_terms = list(c("t_cell", "activation"), "inflammation"))

what: 9. Which genes drive a pathway? - VisualizeLeadingEdge()
title: scSidekick - Pathway Analysis: RunGSEA
text: significant NES tells pathway enriched, genes responsible. VisualizeLeadingEdge() pulls leading-edge genes pathways select (keyword search), draws per-cell expression heatmap - , optionally, dotplot, stacked violins, boxplots, intersected DEGs. Pain point fixed. Go “pathway ” “specific genes driving , cells” - visually, one call. Supply DEG table via deg_df intersect leading-edge genes significant differentially expressed genes focused, defensible gene set.
code: VisualizeLeadingEdge(bmcite,   gsea_files   = "./Figures/Pathways",     # a dir of GSEA CSVs, or file paths   search_terms = c("interferon"),   group.by     = "celltype.l1",   split.by     = "donor",   output_dir   = "./Figures/LeadingEdge")

title: scSidekick - Pathway Analysis: RunGSEA
text: Vignette 05: GroupHeatmap - gene-level expression heatmap Vignette 06: CellTypeAssignmentHelper - annotate clusters pathway analysis Vignette 09: RunCellChat - pathway-level cell-cell communication

title: scSidekick - Pathway Analysis: RunGSEA
text: default NES heatmaps use 5-stop diverging palette centered 0 (blue - light blue - white - light orange - orange-red). Override circlize::colorRamp2 object via heatmap_colors.
code: # Classic blue-white-red (3-stop) col_bwr <- circlize::colorRamp2(c(-3, 0, 3), c("#2166ac", "white", "#d6604d"))  # Purple-green (PRGn) col_prg <- circlize::colorRamp2(   c(-3, -1.5, 0, 1.5, 3),   c("#762a83", "#c2a5cf", "white", "#7fbf7b", "#1b7837"))  # Tighter scale for subtle signals col_tight <- circlize::colorRamp2(c(-1.5, 0, 1.5),                                    c("#007dd1", "white", "#ab3000"))  res <- RunGSEA(bmcite,   group.by       = "celltype.l1",   split.by       = NULL,   output_dir     = tempdir(),   heatmap_colors = col_bwr)

Pain point fixed. Go “pathway ” “specific genes driving , cells” - visually, one call. Supply DEG table via deg_df intersect leading-edge genes significant differentially expressed genes focused, defensible gene set.
code: VisualizeLeadingEdge(bmcite,   gsea_files   = "./Figures/Pathways",     # a dir of GSEA CSVs, or file paths   search_terms = c("interferon"),   group.by     = "celltype.l1",   split.by     = "donor",   output_dir   = "./Figures/LeadingEdge")

title: scSidekick - Pathway Analysis: RunGSEA
text: Vignette 05: GroupHeatmap - gene-level expression heatmap Vignette 06: CellTypeAssignmentHelper - annotate clusters pathway analysis Vignette 09: RunGSEA_pseudobulk - sample-level pseudobulk GSEA limma-voom Vignette 10: RunSCssGSEA - per-cell pathway scoring; PlotPathwayButterfly - 4-pathway state hierarchy; RunEnrichment - ORA Vignette 11: RunCellChat - pathway-level cell-cell communication

title: scSidekick - Pathway Analysis: RunGSEA
code: sessionInfo()

title: scSidekick - Cell-Cell Communication: RunCellChat & CompareCellChat
text: scSidekick wraps CellChat provide streamlined, reproducible cell-cell communication workflow. Two functions cover full analysis: RunCellChat() - runs complete CellChat pipeline level grouping variable (e.g. one CellChat object per condition) saves summary figures. CompareCellChat() - takes list pre-computed CellChat objects generates comparative plots (interaction count differences, chord diagrams, pathway-level comparisons). functions designed multi-condition studies want ask “interactions gained lost Control Treated?”

title: scSidekick - Cell-Cell Communication: RunCellChat & CompareCellChat
text: Note: real analyses, “group” group.least hundred cells. fake Condition column , results illustrative .
code: library(scSidekick) library(Seurat) library(SeuratData) library(CellChat)  library(dplyr) data("bmcite") bmcite <- UpdateSeuratObject(bmcite) bmcite <- bmcite %>%   NormalizeData() %>% FindVariableFeatures() %>% ScaleData() %>% RunPCA() dims <- Determine_nDims(bmcite, elbow_plot = FALSE) bmcite <- RunUMAP(bmcite, dims = 1:dims$n_dims) bmcite <- FindNeighbors(bmcite, dims = 1:dims$n_dims) bmcite <- FindClusters(bmcite, resolution = 0.5) bmcite <- PrepObject(bmcite,   variables   = c("lane", "donor", "celltype.l1", "celltype.l2", "seurat_clusters"),   group.by    = "celltype.l1",   split.by    = "donor",   output_dir  = tempdir(),   object_name = "bmcite")

title: scSidekick - Cell-Cell Communication: RunCellChat & CompareCellChat
text: RunCellChat() splits Seurat object levels group., builds one CellChat object per level, runs full inference pipeline , saves summary figures. cell.defines cell-type identity used communication inference. group.defines groups (e.g. conditions) run separately. function returns named list CellChat objects, one per level group..
code: cc_results <- RunCellChat(bmcite,   cell.by    = "seurat_clusters",   # cell identity column   group.by   = "celltype.l1",       # one CellChat run per cell type   output_dir = tempdir())

title: scSidekick - Cell-Cell Communication: RunCellChat & CompareCellChat
text: Use groups run subset levels, fix order:
code: cc_results <- RunCellChat(bmcite,   cell.by    = "seurat_clusters",   group.by   = "Condition",   groups     = c("Control", "Treated"),   output_dir = tempdir())

title: scSidekick - Cell-Cell Communication: RunCellChat & CompareCellChat
text: Set species = "mouse" use CellChatDB.mouse ligand-receptor database:
code: # brain is the stxBrain object from vignette 07 cc_brain <- RunCellChat(brain,   cell.by    = "seurat_clusters",   group.by   = "Condition",   species    = "mouse",   output_dir = tempdir())

title: scSidekick - Cell-Cell Communication: RunCellChat & CompareCellChat
text: Pass named character vector colors override cluster palette used CellChat chord diagrams network plots. Colors keyed levels cell..
code: cluster_cols <- GetColors(bmcite, "seurat_clusters")  cc_results <- RunCellChat(bmcite,   cell.by    = "seurat_clusters",   group.by   = "Condition",   colors     = cluster_cols,   output_dir = tempdir())

title: scSidekick - Cell-Cell Communication: RunCellChat & CompareCellChat
text: resume = TRUE checks output_dir already-completed CellChat .rds files skips groups, resuming run stopped. Essential large datasets.
code: cc_results <- RunCellChat(bmcite,   cell.by    = "seurat_clusters",   group.by   = "Condition",   output_dir = tempdir(),   resume     = TRUE)

title: scSidekick - Cell-Cell Communication: RunCellChat & CompareCellChat
text: run.patterns = TRUE (default) additionally runs identifyCommunicationPatterns extract global signaling patterns (incoming outgoing) using NMF. Set FALSE skip step save time.
code: cc_results <- RunCellChat(bmcite,   cell.by      = "seurat_clusters",   group.by     = "Condition",   output_dir   = tempdir(),   run.patterns = FALSE)   # skip pattern analysis

title: scSidekick - Cell-Cell Communication: RunCellChat & CompareCellChat
text: level group., RunCellChat() saves following files output_dir:

title: scSidekick - Cell-Cell Communication: RunCellChat & CompareCellChat
text: CompareCellChat() takes named list CellChat objects (one per condition) generates comparative visualizations side side. Pain point fixed. Two things go wrong natively. (1) signalling pathway detected one cohort absent another, per-pathway plot errors cohorts lack , panels silently dropped absence (often whole point) becomes invisible. (2) object draws cell groups colours order, panels aren’t comparable position--position. CompareCellChat() puts every cohort one shared node layout colours group order every panel, every pathway present object - compare apples apples. Use thresh = 1 also compare pathways active ligand-receptor level didn’t reach pathway-level significance cohorts.

title: scSidekick - Cell-Cell Communication: RunCellChat & CompareCellChat
text: Pass list returned directly RunCellChat(). names list elements (e.g. "Control" "Treated") used labels plots.
code: # cc_results is the list returned by RunCellChat() CompareCellChat(   cellchat_list = cc_results,   output_dir = tempdir() )

title: scSidekick - Cell-Cell Communication: RunCellChat & CompareCellChat
text: previously ran RunCellChat() different session, reload .rds files build list manually:
code: cc_ctrl    <- readRDS(file.path(tempdir(), "Control_CellChat.rds")) cc_treated <- readRDS(file.path(tempdir(), "Treated_CellChat.rds"))  CompareCellChat(   cellchat_list = list(Control = cc_ctrl, Treated = cc_treated),   output_dir = tempdir() )

title: scSidekick - Cell-Cell Communication: RunCellChat & CompareCellChat
text: CompareCellChat() saves following files output_dir: differential heatmap scatter plot two commonly cited figures condition comparisons publications.

title: scSidekick - Cell-Cell Communication: RunCellChat & CompareCellChat
code: # Step 1: run CellChat for each condition cc_results <- RunCellChat(bmcite,   cell.by    = "seurat_clusters",   group.by   = "Condition",   groups     = c("Control", "Treated"),   colors     = GetColors(bmcite, "seurat_clusters"),   output_dir = file.path(tempdir(), "CellChat"),   resume     = TRUE)  # Step 2: compare CompareCellChat(   cellchat_list = cc_results,   output_dir = file.path(tempdir(), "CellChat", "Comparison"))

title: scSidekick - Cell-Cell Communication: RunCellChat & CompareCellChat
text: Vignette 01: PrepObject - register cell-type colors used chord diagrams Vignette 06: CellTypeAssignmentHelper - assign cell types CellChat Vignette 08: RunGSEA - pathway-level analysis complementary CellChat

title: scSidekick - Cell-Cell Communication: RunCellChat & CompareCellChat
code: sessionInfo()

title: scSidekick - Pathway Analysis: RunGSEA_pseudobulk
text: RunGSEA_pseudobulk() performs pseudobulk gene set enrichment analysis using sample-level limma-voom contrasts - statistically rigorous alternative cell-level Wilcoxon approach RunGSEA(). key idea: cells biological sample aggregated pseudobulk profile DE testing, biological replicate contributes one observation rather thousands. avoids pseudoreplication produces calibrated p-values. use approach: addition GSEA, RunGSEA_pseudobulk() optionally runs per-patient ssGSEA (set run.ssgsea = TRUE, default) produce individual-level pathway heatmaps showing patient--patient variability.

title: scSidekick - Pathway Analysis: RunGSEA_pseudobulk
code: library(scSidekick) library(Seurat) library(dplyr)  # Assumes a multi-sample Seurat object with: #   celltype  - annotated cell types #   sample    - unique biological sample ID (one per donor/timepoint/condition) #   condition - the comparison variable (e.g. "Control" vs "Disease")

title: scSidekick - Pathway Analysis: RunGSEA_pseudobulk
text: three required metadata columns group.(cell type), sample.(sample/donor ID), contrast.(condition compare). runs GSEA cell type group., comparing levels condition default limma-voom contrasts.
code: res <- RunGSEA_pseudobulk(   seurat_object = seu,   group.by      = "celltype",   sample.by     = "sample",   contrast.by   = "condition",   output_dir    = "./Figures/Pathways_PB" )

title: scSidekick - Pathway Analysis: RunGSEA_pseudobulk
text: default, RunGSEA_pseudobulk tests pairwise comparisons within contrast.. Supply contrasts explicitly control comparisons made, direction. name becomes label set output figures; value standard limma contrast expression.
code: res <- RunGSEA_pseudobulk(   seurat_object = seu,   group.by      = "celltype",   sample.by     = "sample",   contrast.by   = "condition",   contrasts     = list(     Disease_vs_Control = "Disease - Control",     Treatment_vs_Control = "Treatment - Control"   ),   output_dir    = "./Figures/Pathways_PB" )

title: scSidekick - Pathway Analysis: RunGSEA_pseudobulk
text: Correct batch effects sample-level metadata passing covariates. included additional terms limma-voom model.
code: res <- RunGSEA_pseudobulk(   seurat_object = seu,   group.by      = "celltype",   sample.by     = "sample",   contrast.by   = "condition",   covariates    = c("batch", "sex"),   output_dir    = "./Figures/Pathways_PB" )

title: scSidekick - Pathway Analysis: RunGSEA_pseudobulk
text: Use split.run full pipeline independently within major lineage. pseudobulk equivalent split.parameter RunGSEA.
code: res <- RunGSEA_pseudobulk(   seurat_object = seu,   group.by      = "celltype_fine",   sample.by     = "sample",   contrast.by   = "condition",   split.by      = "celltype_broad",   output_dir    = "./Figures/Pathways_PB" )

title: scSidekick - Pathway Analysis: RunGSEA_pseudobulk
text: syntax RunGSEA. Default: Hallmark, KEGG, Reactome, WikiPathways.
code: res <- RunGSEA_pseudobulk(   seurat_object = seu,   group.by      = "celltype",   sample.by     = "sample",   contrast.by   = "condition",   pathway_sets  = list(     Hallmark    = list(category = "H"),     ImmuneSigDB = list(category = "C7", subcategory = "IMMUNESIGDB")   ),   output_dir    = "./Figures/Pathways_PB" )

title: scSidekick - Pathway Analysis: RunGSEA_pseudobulk
text: already aggregated pseudobulk counts (e.g. ComputePseudobulk), pass directly via pseudobulk skip re-aggregation.
code: pb <- ComputePseudobulk(seu,   group.by  = "celltype",   sample.by = "sample")  res <- RunGSEA_pseudobulk(   pseudobulk = pb,   group.by   = "celltype",   sample.by  = "sample",   contrast.by = "condition",   output_dir = "./Figures/Pathways_PB" )

title: scSidekick - Pathway Analysis: RunGSEA_pseudobulk
text: run.ssgsea = TRUE (default) computes per-patient pathway activity via ssGSEA main GSEA, producing heatmaps columns individual samples. makes patient-level variability visible alongside group-level result. Use ssgsea.use.padj = TRUE (default) filter ssGSEA rows BH-adjusted p-value, ssgsea.use.padj = FALSE use raw p-values.
code: res <- RunGSEA_pseudobulk(   seurat_object  = seu,   group.by       = "celltype",   sample.by      = "sample",   contrast.by    = "condition",   run.ssgsea     = TRUE,   ssgsea.use.padj = TRUE,   top_n_ssgsea   = 20L,   output_dir     = "./Figures/Pathways_PB" )

title: scSidekick - Pathway Analysis: RunGSEA_pseudobulk
text: heatmap_colors parameter works RunGSEA. Pass circlize::colorRamp2 object override default 5-stop blue-white-orange-red palette.
code: col_custom <- circlize::colorRamp2(   c(-3, -1.5, 0, 1.5, 3),   c("#762a83", "#c2a5cf", "white", "#7fbf7b", "#1b7837") )  res <- RunGSEA_pseudobulk(   seurat_object  = seu,   group.by       = "celltype",   sample.by      = "sample",   contrast.by    = "condition",   heatmap_colors = col_custom,   output_dir     = "./Figures/Pathways_PB" )

title: scSidekick - Pathway Analysis: RunGSEA_pseudobulk
text: save.rds = TRUE (default) saves intermediate RDS files cell type. run interrupted, use redo_plots = TRUE reload saved RDS files regenerate PDFs without re-running DE analysis.
code: # Regenerate all PDFs from saved RDS (no re-computation) res <- RunGSEA_pseudobulk(   seurat_object = seu,   group.by      = "celltype",   sample.by     = "sample",   contrast.by   = "condition",   redo_plots    = TRUE,   output_dir    = "./Figures/Pathways_PB" )

title: scSidekick - Pathway Analysis: RunGSEA_pseudobulk
text: RunGSEA_pseudobulk returns nested list, one element per cell type. element contains:
code: # GSEA results table for one cell type res$`CD4 T`$gsea_results  # NES heatmap ggplot object res$`CD4 T`$nes_heatmap  # ssGSEA score matrix (pathways x samples) res$`CD4 T`$ssgsea_scores

title: scSidekick - Pathway Analysis: RunGSEA_pseudobulk
text: Vignette 08: RunGSEA - cell-level Wilcoxon-based GSEA (replicates needed) Vignette 10: RunSCssGSEA - per-cell pathway scoring; PlotPathwayButterfly - 4-pathway state hierarchy Vignette 11: RunCellChat - pathway-level cell-cell communication

title: scSidekick - Pathway Analysis: RunGSEA_pseudobulk
code: sessionInfo()

title: scSidekick - Pathway Analysis: Single-Cell Scoring & Enrichment
text: vignette covers three complementary approaches pathway analysis single-cell level: tools answer different questions: RunSCssGSEA: “pathways differentially active cell types, single-cell level?” PlotPathwayButterfly: “cell sit 4-pathway state space? cell types polarize toward specific states?” RunEnrichment: “Given list significant DEGs, pathways -represented?”

title: scSidekick - Pathway Analysis: Single-Cell Scoring & Enrichment
text: RunSCssGSEA() computes pathway activity score every cell using either GSVA’s ssGSEA algorithm UCell, tests significant differences groups using ANOVA Kruskal-Wallis. Results visualized mean z-score heatmap (pathways x groups) optional per-pathway boxplots. Gene-set input modes (pick one): Modes 3 4 can combined.

title: scSidekick - Pathway Analysis: Single-Cell Scoring & Enrichment
code: library(scSidekick) library(Seurat) library(SeuratData)  data("bmcite") bmcite <- UpdateSeuratObject(bmcite) bmcite <- PrepObject(bmcite,   variables   = c("celltype.l1", "celltype.l2", "donor"),   group.by    = "celltype.l1",   output_dir  = tempdir(),   object_name = "bmcite")

title: scSidekick - Pathway Analysis: Single-Cell Scoring & Enrichment
text: Pass named list gene vectors. element becomes one row heatmap.
code: RunSCssGSEA(bmcite,   group.by  = "celltype.l1",   gene_sets = list(     T_effector  = c("GZMB", "PRF1", "IFNG", "TNF"),     T_exhausted = c("PDCD1", "HAVCR2", "LAG3", "TIGIT"),     NK_cytotoxic = c("NCR1", "KLRB1", "GNLY", "NKG7")   ),   output_dir  = tempdir(),   object_name = "bmcite")

title: scSidekick - Pathway Analysis: Single-Cell Scoring & Enrichment
text: Build gene sets automatically DEG table. group’s top DEGs become one gene set. deg_group_column can single column (groups heatmap rows) two columns (first column used split rows heatmap).
code: # Get DEGs first markers <- RunWilcoxAUC(bmcite, group.by = "celltype.l1")  RunSCssGSEA(bmcite,   group.by        = "celltype.l1",   deg_df          = markers,   deg_gene_column = "feature",   deg_group_column = "group",   deg_fc_column   = "logFC",   deg_padj_column = "padj",   deg_padj_cutoff = 0.05,   deg_top_n       = 30L,   output_dir      = tempdir(),   object_name     = "bmcite")

title: scSidekick - Pathway Analysis: Single-Cell Scoring & Enrichment
text: Fetch entire MSigDB collection score cells . Large collections (e.g. GO:BP thousands sets) filtered search_terms min.size keep run tractable.
code: RunSCssGSEA(bmcite,   group.by         = "celltype.l1",   gene_set_library = "H",   # Hallmark   min.size         = 10L,   output_dir       = tempdir(),   object_name      = "bmcite")

title: scSidekick - Pathway Analysis: Single-Cell Scoring & Enrichment
text: Search gene-set names case-insensitive regex. Pass character vector logic, list vectors -groups.
code: # OR: any set mentioning "interferon" OR "apoptosis" RunSCssGSEA(bmcite,   group.by      = "celltype.l1",   search_terms  = c("interferon", "apoptosis"),   pathway_sets  = list(Hallmark = list(category = "H")),   output_dir    = tempdir(),   object_name   = "bmcite")  # AND-group: sets mentioning BOTH "T_CELL" AND "ACTIVATION" RunSCssGSEA(bmcite,   group.by     = "celltype.l1",   search_terms = list(c("T_CELL", "ACTIVATION")),   pathway_sets = list(     C7 = list(category = "C7", subcategory = "IMMUNESIGDB")),   output_dir   = tempdir(),   object_name  = "bmcite")

title: scSidekick - Pathway Analysis: Single-Cell Scoring & Enrichment
text: default scoring backend GSVA’s ssGSEA. Use method = "ucell" UCell rank-based algorithm, scales better large datasets avoids GSVA memory overhead.
code: RunSCssGSEA(bmcite,   group.by         = "celltype.l1",   gene_set_library = "H",   method           = "ucell",   ucell_max_rank   = 1500L,   output_dir       = tempdir(),   object_name      = "bmcite")

title: scSidekick - Pathway Analysis: Single-Cell Scoring & Enrichment
text: objects >50,000 cells, pass downsample cap number cells per group scoring.
code: RunSCssGSEA(large_obj,   group.by         = "celltype",   gene_set_library = "H",   downsample       = 5000L,   output_dir       = "./Figures/Pathways")

title: scSidekick - Pathway Analysis: Single-Cell Scoring & Enrichment
text: default 5-stop diverging palette centered 0 (z = -2 +2). Override circlize::colorRamp2 object.
code: # Classic blue-white-red col_bwr <- circlize::colorRamp2(c(-2, -1, 0, 1, 2),                                  c("#2166ac", "#92c5de", "white",                                    "#f4a582", "#d6604d"))  RunSCssGSEA(bmcite,   group.by         = "celltype.l1",   gene_set_library = "H",   heatmap_colors   = col_bwr,   output_dir       = tempdir(),   object_name      = "bmcite")

title: scSidekick - Pathway Analysis: Single-Cell Scoring & Enrichment
text: resume = TRUE reloads previously saved score CSV checkpoint, skipping re-scoring cells already processed.
code: RunSCssGSEA(bmcite,   group.by         = "celltype.l1",   gene_set_library = "H",   resume           = TRUE,   output_dir       = tempdir(),   object_name      = "bmcite")

title: scSidekick - Pathway Analysis: Single-Cell Scoring & Enrichment
text: PlotPathwayButterfly() maps every cell onto 2-D coordinate system defined exactly four pathways arranged quadrants. makes cell state polarization visually immediate: cells high top-left pathway cluster upper-left plot; cells sitting center transition. Quadrant layout: Y-axis separates dominant axis (top vs bottom pathways); X-axis gives left-right balance within whichever axis dominates cell. Output layout: one large overview panel (cells colored group.) flanked one small panel per group level (group highlighted color; remaining cells gray). visualization adapted scrabble R package (Suva Lab).
code: top-left | top-right               ---------|----------            bottom-left | bottom-right

what: 2.2 Basic call with MSigDB Hallmark pathways
title: scSidekick - Pathway Analysis: Single-Cell Scoring & Enrichment
text: Supply four pathway names appear MSigDB collection. pathway_sets argument specifies collection search.
code: PlotPathwayButterfly(   seurat_object = bmcite,   group.by      = "celltype.l1",   quadrant_order = c(     "HALLMARK_INTERFERON_GAMMA_RESPONSE",  # bottom-left     "HALLMARK_INFLAMMATORY_RESPONSE",      # bottom-right     "HALLMARK_G2M_CHECKPOINT",             # top-left     "HALLMARK_MYC_TARGETS_V1"             # top-right   ),   pathway_sets  = list(Hallmark = list(category = "H")),   output_dir    = tempdir() )

title: scSidekick - Pathway Analysis: Single-Cell Scoring & Enrichment
text: gene sets (MSigDB), pass directly via gene_sets. names must match quadrant_order. names quadrant_order map four quadrant positions: bottom-left, bottom-right, top-left, top-right.
code: PlotPathwayButterfly(   seurat_object  = bmcite,   group.by       = "celltype.l1",   gene_sets      = list(     Cytotoxic    = c("GZMB", "PRF1", "IFNG"),     NaiveLike    = c("CCR7", "SELL", "TCF7"),     Exhausted    = c("PDCD1", "LAG3", "HAVCR2"),     Regulatory   = c("FOXP3", "IL2RA", "CTLA4")   ),   quadrant_order = c("NaiveLike", "Cytotoxic", "Regulatory", "Exhausted"),   output_dir     = tempdir() )

title: scSidekick - Pathway Analysis: Single-Cell Scoring & Enrichment
text: default, corner labels auto-shortened pathway name (prefix underscores removed). Override quadrant_labels cleaner display.
code: PlotPathwayButterfly(   seurat_object  = bmcite,   group.by       = "celltype.l1",   gene_sets      = list(     Cytotoxic    = c("GZMB", "PRF1", "IFNG"),     NaiveLike    = c("CCR7", "SELL", "TCF7"),     Exhausted    = c("PDCD1", "LAG3", "HAVCR2"),     Regulatory   = c("FOXP3", "IL2RA", "CTLA4")   ),   quadrant_order  = c("NaiveLike", "Cytotoxic", "Regulatory", "Exhausted"),   quadrant_labels = c("Naive",  "Cytotoxic",    # bl, br                       "Treg",   "Exhausted"),    # tl, tr   output_dir      = tempdir() )

title: scSidekick - Pathway Analysis: Single-Cell Scoring & Enrichment
text: split.produces separate figure per split level, axis limits. Use compare butterfly distributions across donors conditions.
code: PlotPathwayButterfly(   seurat_object  = bmcite,   group.by       = "celltype.l1",   split.by       = "donor",   gene_sets      = list(     Cytotoxic    = c("GZMB", "PRF1", "IFNG"),     NaiveLike    = c("CCR7", "SELL", "TCF7"),     Exhausted    = c("PDCD1", "LAG3", "HAVCR2"),     Regulatory   = c("FOXP3", "IL2RA", "CTLA4")   ),   quadrant_order = c("NaiveLike", "Cytotoxic", "Regulatory", "Exhausted"),   output_dir     = tempdir() )

title: scSidekick - Pathway Analysis: Single-Cell Scoring & Enrichment
text: objects >50,000 cells, enable subsampling keep scoring fast: Without subsampling, function emits warning subsample_warn_at = 50000 cells (adjustable).
code: PlotPathwayButterfly(   seurat_object     = large_obj,   group.by          = "celltype",   quadrant_order    = c("HALLMARK_MYC_TARGETS_V1",                         "HALLMARK_E2F_TARGETS",                         "HALLMARK_INTERFERON_GAMMA_RESPONSE",                         "HALLMARK_INFLAMMATORY_RESPONSE"),   subsample         = TRUE,   n_cells           = 30000L,   output_dir        = "./Figures/Butterfly" )

title: scSidekick - Pathway Analysis: Single-Cell Scoring & Enrichment
text: PlotPathwayButterfly invisibly returns named list (one element per split level). element contains raw coordinates scores.
code: res <- PlotPathwayButterfly(   seurat_object  = bmcite,   group.by       = "celltype.l1",   gene_sets      = list(     Cytotoxic    = c("GZMB", "PRF1", "IFNG"),     NaiveLike    = c("CCR7", "SELL", "TCF7"),     Exhausted    = c("PDCD1", "LAG3", "HAVCR2"),     Regulatory   = c("FOXP3", "IL2RA", "CTLA4")   ),   quadrant_order = c("NaiveLike", "Cytotoxic", "Regulatory", "Exhausted") )  # Hierarchy coordinates (X, Y) for each cell head(res$All$hierarchy)  # Raw pathway scores (cells x pathways) head(res$All$scores)

title: scSidekick - Pathway Analysis: Single-Cell Scoring & Enrichment
text: RunEnrichment() runs -representation analysis (ORA) via enrichR gene list significant DEGs data frame. automatically calls PlotEnrichment() save dot plots enriched terms. ORA simpler GSEA answers different question: given gene list, pathways contain genes expected chance? ranking used - just presence absence.

title: scSidekick - Pathway Analysis: Single-Cell Scoring & Enrichment
text: Pass character vector gene symbols directly. Run enrichR::listEnrichrDbs() available database names.
code: RunEnrichment(   features   = c("GZMB", "PRF1", "IFNG", "TNF", "FASLG", "NKG7"),   databases  = c("MSigDB_Hallmark_2020",                  "GO_Biological_Process_2023",                  "KEGG_2021_Human"),   output_dir = tempdir() )

title: scSidekick - Pathway Analysis: Single-Cell Scoring & Enrichment
text: Pass DEG data frame; RunEnrichment filters significant DEGs testing. Columns auto-detected common RunWilcoxAUC/FindMarkers naming.
code: markers <- RunWilcoxAUC(bmcite, group.by = "celltype.l1")  RunEnrichment(   deg_df     = markers,   direction  = "up",         # "up", "down", or "both"   fc_cutoff  = 0.25,   padj_cutoff = 0.05,   databases  = c("MSigDB_Hallmark_2020", "GO_Biological_Process_2023"),   output_dir = tempdir(),   object_name = "bmcite" )

title: scSidekick - Pathway Analysis: Single-Cell Scoring & Enrichment
text: DEG table group_column, RunEnrichment tests group’s DEGs separately saves one plot per group.
code: RunEnrichment(   deg_df       = markers,   group_column = "group",   direction    = "up",   databases    = c("MSigDB_Hallmark_2020"),   top_n_plot   = 15L,   output_dir   = tempdir(),   object_name  = "bmcite" )

title: scSidekick - Pathway Analysis: Single-Cell Scoring & Enrichment
text: default databases MSigDB_Hallmark_2020, GO_Biological_Process_2023, KEGG_2021_Human. can test databases available enrichR.
code: # List all available enrichR databases enrichR::listEnrichrDbs()  RunEnrichment(   features  = c("GZMB", "PRF1", "IFNG"),   databases = c("Reactome_2022",                 "WikiPathway_2023_Human",                 "CellMarker_Augmented_2021"),   output_dir = tempdir() )

title: scSidekick - Pathway Analysis: Single-Cell Scoring & Enrichment
text: RunEnrichment returns nested list: [[group]][[database]] containing enrichR result data frames.
code: res <- RunEnrichment(   deg_df       = markers,   group_column = "group",   direction    = "up",   databases    = c("MSigDB_Hallmark_2020"),   output_dir   = tempdir() )  # Enrichment results for one group and database head(res$`CD4 T`$MSigDB_Hallmark_2020)

title: scSidekick - Pathway Analysis: Single-Cell Scoring & Enrichment
text: Vignette 08: RunGSEA - rank-based GSEA full Seurat object Vignette 09: RunGSEA_pseudobulk - sample-level pseudobulk GSEA limma-voom Vignette 05: GroupHeatmap / PlotDotPlot - gene-level expression visualizations Vignette 11: RunCellChat - pathway-level cell-cell communication

title: scSidekick - Pathway Analysis: Single-Cell Scoring & Enrichment
code: sessionInfo()

title: scSidekick - Reporting & Utilities
text: scSidekick built write-writes . analyse, package captures provenance ’ll need months later paper time: Auto-logged figure legends - every figure-saving function drops plain-text .legend sidecar next figure, describing exactly plotted. ExtractMethods() - turns Seurat command log ready--paste methods paragraph. Passive session logging - start_session_logger() records commands plots; summarize_r_session() turns messy session clean report tidy R script. create_analysis_pptx() - assembles saved figures sectioned PowerPoint deck one call. Plus everyday utilities: GetColors(), ShowColors(), theme_NourMin(), Nour_pal(), PlotTrendLabeled(), PlotComposition().

what: 1. ExtractMethods() - a methods paragraph for free
title: scSidekick - Reporting & Utilities
text: ExtractMethods() reads Seurat command log (bmcite@commands) reconstructs key analysis steps prose paragraph structured summary - reverse-engineering pipeline submission time. companion $summary structured list detected steps parameters, handy supplementary table.
code: methods_out <- ExtractMethods(bmcite, cite_seurat = TRUE) cat(methods_out$methods_text) ## Single-cell RNA-seq (scRNA-seq) data (30,672 cells, 17,009 genes; assay: RNA) were analyzed using Seurat. Gene expression counts were normalized using LogNormalize (scale factor 10,000) and 2,000 highly variable genes were identified using the vst method. PCA was performed (50 PCs computed). Cells were clustered using the Louvain algorithm at resolution 0.50, yielding 15 clusters. (Cite: Hao et al., Cell 2021 / Hao et al., Nature Methods 2024 as appropriate.)

title: scSidekick - Reporting & Utilities
text: Every scSidekick function saves figure also writes .legend text file beside , generated exact arguments used. never remember figure showed. can also log legends analysis parameters log_figure_legend() log_analysis_params() build custom figure outside package.
code: fig_dir <- file.path(tempdir(), "figs") dir.create(fig_dir, showWarnings = FALSE)  # Save a feature map; a PDF and a matching .legend are written: invisible(GenerateFeatureMaps(bmcite,   features    = "CD3E",   output_dir  = fig_dir,   object_name = "bmcite"))  list.files(fig_dir) ## [1] "CD3E featuremap bmcite.legend" "CD3E featuremap bmcite.pdf" # Read back the auto-generated legend: legend_file <- list.files(fig_dir, pattern = "\\.legend$", full.names = TRUE) cat(readLines(legend_file), sep = "\n") ## UMAP feature plot showing expression of CD3E in bmcite. Colour scale: low (dark blue) to high (dark red), capped at the per-gene maximum (3.89). Each panel represents one group level; legend shows the shared colour bar.

title: scSidekick - Reporting & Utilities
text: start_session_logger() installs lightweight hook records commands run plots draw rest session. Afterwards, summarize_r_session() distills log Markdown report clean, runnable R script - turning exploratory session reproducible output. logger_log_file() logger_plot_dir() tell active log captured plots live.
code: # At the top of an analysis session: start_session_logger()  # ... do your analysis (every command and plot is captured) ...  # At the end, generate a report + a tidy script: summarize_r_session(   output_md = "analysis_report.md",   output_r  = "analysis_clean.R")

title: scSidekick - Reporting & Utilities
text: create_analysis_pptx() scans output_dir saved PDF/PNG figures, groups sections filename keyword, writes .pptx - whole analysis deck, one call. Section grouping rules (keyword filename -> section label): Include exclude sections name:
code: create_analysis_pptx(   output_dir  = "./bmcite_Figures",   object_name = "bmcite") create_analysis_pptx(   output_dir       = "./bmcite_Figures",   object_name      = "bmcite",   include_sections = c("Dimensionality Reduction", "Gene Expression"))

title: scSidekick - Reporting & Utilities
text: GetColors() returns named color vector variable, ready pass scale_color_manual() scSidekick colors argument.
code: ct_cols <- GetColors(bmcite, "celltype.l1") ct_cols ##           B cell          Mono/DC               NK Progenitor cells  ##        "#003F5C"        "#74599E"        "#E46388"        "#F77B38"  ##           T cell  ##        "#8DB032" all_cols <- GetColors(bmcite) names(all_cols) ## [1] "lane"            "donor"           "celltype.l1"     "celltype.l2"     ## [5] "seurat_clusters"

title: scSidekick - Reporting & Utilities
code: ShowColors(bmcite)

title: scSidekick - Reporting & Utilities
text: PlotTrendLabeled() publication-ready composition figure percentage labels; PlotComposition() idea without directional labels.
code: PlotTrendLabeled(bmcite,   stat.by    = "celltype.l1",   group.by   = "lane",   split.by   = "donor",   label.side = "right") PlotComposition(bmcite,   group.by = "celltype.l1",   split.by = "donor")

title: scSidekick - Reporting & Utilities
text: scSidekick ships two hand-curated palettes unified accessor: Nour18 - 18 qualitative colors (blues, purples, pinks, reds, oranges, greens). Best datasets 18 cell types. Nour20 - 20 colors sweeping full visible spectrum. Better atlases many fine-grained subtypes. Nour_pal() - returns color-function (like scale_color_brewer internally), can pull number colors either palette name.   Nour_pal() pulls subset either palette name plugs straight scale_color_manual() / scale_fill_manual():  SelectColors() lets interactively pick subset colors name Nour18 - useful want hand-pick specific hues figure:
code: # Nour18 - 18 qualitative colors scales::show_col(Nour18, cex_label = 0.6) # Nour20 - full-spectrum 20-color palette scales::show_col(Nour20, cex_label = 0.6) df <- data.frame(x = 1:8, y = 1, grp = LETTERS[1:8]) ggplot(df, aes(x, y, color = grp)) +   geom_point(size = 6) +   scale_color_manual(values = Nour_pal("main")(8)) +   theme_NourMin() # Returns the hex codes for just the colors you name SelectColors("blue1", "teal", "pink2", "orange")

title: scSidekick - Reporting & Utilities
text: Seurat clusters come 0, 1, 2, .... RenameClusters() converts zero-padded, prefixed labels (C01, C02, …) axes sort correctly look publication-ready. Common usage right FindClusters():
code: # Simulate raw Seurat cluster IDs raw_clusters <- factor(c(0, 1, 2, 0, 3, 1, 2))  RenameClusters(raw_clusters, prefix = "C", start = 1) ##  0  1  2  0  3  1  2  ## C1 C2 C3 C1 C4 C2 C3  ## Levels: C1 C2 C3 C4 bmcite$Cluster <- RenameClusters(bmcite$seurat_clusters, prefix = "C", start = 1) # seurat cluster 0 -> "C01", cluster 1 -> "C02", etc. # factor levels are in cluster order, so ggplot axes always sort correctly.  bmcite <- PrepObject(bmcite, variables = "Cluster", group.by = "Cluster") PlotDimPlots(bmcite, group.by = "Cluster")

title: scSidekick - Reporting & Utilities
text: Vignette 01: PrepObject, ShowColors, GetColors - color management Vignette 11: PlotMetaSummary() composition chart family Every analysis vignette saves output_dir - feeds straight create_analysis_pptx() produces .legend sidecars automatically.
