title: YAP Project — scRNA-seq Analysis
code: library(easypackages) libraries(c(   "dplyr", "ggplot2", "ggpubr", "patchwork", "stringr",   "BPCells", "Seurat", "ComplexHeatmap", "SingleCellExperiment",   "RColorBrewer", "viridis", "BiocParallel" )) register(MulticoreParam(workers = 8, progressbar = TRUE)) library(scSidekick)  dirs <- GenerateDirectories(   base_dir       = "/dir/",   subset         = "AllClusters",   object_name    = "YAP",   resolution     = 1,   output_subdirs = c("Featuremaps", "DotPlots", "Pathways", "CellChat") )

title: YAP Project — scRNA-seq Analysis
code: basedir <- "/basedir/"  sample_meta <- data.frame(   sample_id = c("YSGm", "YSGf", "SGm", "SGf"),   Sample    = c("YSGm", "YSGf", "SGm", "SGf"),   run_id    = paste0("MYSC", 25:28),   Genotype  = c("YSG", "YSG", "SG", "SG"),   sex       = c("M", "F", "M", "F"),   batch     = 1L )  SeuratObj <- LoadSamplesRNA(   sample_ids       = sample_meta$Sample,   run_ids          = sample_meta$run_id,   metadata         = sample_meta,   input_type       = "10x_dir",   base_dir         = basedir,   robj_dir         = dirs$robj_dir,   merged_filename  = "unfilteredAllClusters.rds",   merge_samples    = TRUE,   modality         = "scRNA-seq",   mt_pattern       = "^mt-",   save_individual  = TRUE,   # cache per-sample .rds so you can resume if interrupted   resume           = TRUE,   output_dir       = dirs$output_dir )

title: YAP Project — scRNA-seq Analysis
code: PlotQCMetrics(SeuratObj,   sample_col = "Sample",   species    = "mouse",   output_dir = dirs$output_dir )

title: YAP Project — scRNA-seq Analysis

code: SeuratObj <- SeuratObj %>%   NormalizeData() %>%   FindVariableFeatures(nfeatures = 2000) %>%   ScaleData() %>%   RunPCA(npcs = 50)  SeuratObj <- harmony::RunHarmony(SeuratObj, group.by.vars = "Sample")  dims <- Determine_nDims(SeuratObj, reduction = "harmony")  SeuratObj <- SeuratObj %>%   RunUMAP(dims = 1:dims$n_dims, reduction = "harmony") %>%   FindNeighbors(dims = 1:dims$n_dims, reduction = "harmony") %>%   FindClusters(resolution = 0.5)  CellInfo <- SeuratObj@meta.data CellInfo$Cluster <- RenameClusters(SeuratObj$seurat_clusters) SeuratObj@meta.data <- CellInfo  saveRDS(SeuratObj, file.path(dirs$robj_dir, "clustered.rds"))

title: scSidekick - Single-Cell & Spatial Transcriptomics Toolkit
code: # install.packages("devtools") devtools::install_github("nourabdelfattah/scSidekick")

title: scSidekick - Single-Cell & Spatial Transcriptomics Toolkit
text: Every example reproducible public data (data-raw/make_readme_figures.R).

what: 1 · Shared, accurate colour scales across split feature panels
title: scSidekick - Single-Cell & Spatial Transcriptomics Toolkit
text: Splitting feature plot condition single common comparison single-cell work — default single legend placed ambiguously across panels, shared scale can lost custom palette supplied. PlotFeaturePlots() locks one shared, labelled colour scale across every panel comparison honest. per-call workarounds needed. Panel — split single variable: Panel B — split lane, arranged rows donor (metadata layout): samples cross two variables (e.g., lane × donor), row.adds second faceting dimension layout_method = "metadata" aligns panels actual metadata coordinates rather wrapping arbitrary order — grid tells story.
code: # Panel A: split by donor — one shared colour bar PlotFeaturePlots(bmcite, features = "LYZ", split.by = "donor") # Panel B: lane on columns, donor on rows, positionally aligned PlotFeaturePlots(bmcite, features = "LYZ",                  split.by = "lane", row.by = "donor",                  layout_method = "metadata")

previous_headings: ✨ What scSidekick Provides > 2 · Gene-expression dot plots, organized for comparison
what: SplitDotPlot() — expression first, organized by gene group
title: scSidekick - Single-Cell & Spatial Transcriptomics Toolkit
text: Standard Seurat split dot plots color dot split identity rather expression interleave rows, making hard read expression magnitude. SplitDotPlot() keeps colour = expression, size = % expressed, organizes genes labelled cell-type blocks, facets cleanly condition every cluster comparable across conditions glance.
code: SplitDotPlot(bmcite, markers_df = markers_df,              group.by = "celltype.l1", split.by = "donor")

previous_headings: ✨ What scSidekick Provides > 2 · Gene-expression dot plots, organized for comparison
what: FastDotPlot() — find gene programs without knowing gene names
title: scSidekick - Single-Cell & Spatial Transcriptomics Toolkit
text: FastDotPlot() adds two capabilities matter exploring large gene sets: regex pattern selects matching genes automatically (manual list required), k_genes slices gene dendrogram labelled programs co-regulated blocks become visible immediately.  Pair FastDotPlot2() add automatic EnrichR annotation header gene-pattern panel — pattern → pathway one call:
code: # All CD1x/CD2x genes, automatically split into 3 co-expression patterns FastDotPlot(bmcite,             pattern  = "^CD[1-2]",             group.by = "celltype.l1",             k_genes  = 3) FastDotPlot2(bmcite, pattern = "^CD[1-2]", group.by = "celltype.l1",              k_genes = 3, EnrichR_DB = "CellMarker_2024")

what: 3 · Multi-cohort cell-cell communication — keep every cohort visible
title: scSidekick - Single-Cell & Spatial Transcriptomics Toolkit
text: CompareCellChat() places every cohort one shared layout node colours group order every panel, compare presence absence side side. Cohorts pathway absent get blank panel rather silently dropped — absence often finding. Standard comparison — cohorts lacking pathway silently omitted CompareCellChat — every cohort shown; colours, order, absence visible
code: CompareCellChat(   list(SGm = "SGmCellChat.rds",  SGf = "SGfCellChat.rds",        YSGm = "YSGmCellChat.rds", YSGf = "YSGfCellChat.rds"),   output_dir = "./Figures/CellChat")

what: 4 · Atlas-scale metadata, summarized in one line
title: scSidekick - Single-Cell & Spatial Transcriptomics Toolkit
text: PlotMetaSummary() computes cell-type composition across multiple grouping levels — counts percentages — single expression:  function handles 1.78M-cell, 153-column clinical atlas: point data frame id_column = "Donor.ID" collapses cells donors lays clinical variables — manual aggregation:
code: PlotMetaSummary(obj, variables = c("celltype.l1", "celltype.l2"),                 fill_variable = "donor", count_unit = "cells") + PlotMetaSummary(obj, variables = c("celltype.l1", "celltype.l2"),                 fill_variable = "donor", count_unit = "cells", percent = TRUE) PlotMetaSummary(md, id_column = "Donor.ID",                 variables = c("Braak", "APOE.Genotype", "CERAD.score"),                 fill_variable = "Cognitive.Status", row_variable = "Sex")

what: 5 · Methods paragraph + figure legends, written as you go
title: scSidekick - Single-Cell & Spatial Transcriptomics Toolkit
text: Every figure-writing function drops plain-text legend sidecar (.legend) next figure file ’s saved. ExtractMethods() reads Seurat command history ready--paste methods paragraph. Nothing reconstruct months later.
code: # Auto-written next to every figure, e.g. "LYZ_donor featuremap bmcite.legend": #   UMAP feature plot showing expression of LYZ in bmcite, split by donor. #   Colour scale: low (dark blue) to high (dark red), capped at the per-gene #   maximum (6.46). Each panel represents one donor level; legend shows the #   shared colour bar.  ExtractMethods(obj)$methods_text #> Single-cell RNA-seq (scRNA-seq) data (30,672 cells, 17,009 genes; assay: RNA) #> were analyzed using Seurat. Gene expression counts were normalized using #> LogNormalize (scale factor 10,000) and 2,000 highly variable genes were #> identified using the vst method. PCA was performed (30 PCs computed). ...

what: 6 · Analysis to slides in one call
title: scSidekick - Single-Cell & Spatial Transcriptomics Toolkit
text: create_analysis_pptx() walks figures directory (including per-subfolder parameters) assembles PowerPoint deck — aspect-ratio-preserving figure placement, section ordering, works:
code: create_analysis_pptx(output_dir = "./Figures", object_name = "MyProject")

what: 7 · Gene-set enrichment, with keyword pathway search
title: scSidekick - Single-Cell & Spatial Transcriptomics Toolkit
text: One call runs ranking, GSEA Hallmark / KEGG / Reactome / WikiPathways, enrichment plots: Nested comparisons two arguments. split.runs independent GSEA per level — one per major lineage, comparing subtypes within : need know exact pathway names. Pull pathways keyword, see genes drive : sample-level robustness per-cell scores large object, RunGSEA_pseudobulk() RunSCssGSEA() stream BPCells / sketched assays.
code: RunGSEA(obj, group.by = "seurat_clusters", output_dir = "./Figures/Pathways") RunGSEA(obj,         group.by   = "celltype.l2",   # subtypes compared         split.by   = "celltype.l1",   # nested: one run per lineage         output_dir = "./Figures/Pathways") PlotGSEAEnrichment(output_dir = "./Figures/Pathways",                    search_terms = list(c("interferon", "response"), "apoptosis"))  VisualizeLeadingEdge(obj, gsea_files = "./Figures/Pathways",                      search_terms = "interferon", group.by = "celltype.l1")

title: scSidekick - Single-Cell & Spatial Transcriptomics Toolkit
text: three arguments work across every function — plots analyses: group.— defines group (colour, x-axis, comparison) split.— make one panel / one independent run per level row.— arrange panels along second dimension words whether drawing UMAP, dot plot, running GSEA CellChat. Learn grammar ; carries every step.
code: PlotDimPlots(obj,        group.by = "CellType", split.by = "Sample") PlotFeaturePlots(obj,    features = "CD3E",     split.by = "Condition") SplitDotPlot(obj,        markers_df = m, group.by = "CellType", split.by = "Condition") PlotMetaSummary(obj,     variables = "CellType", fill_variable = "Group", row_variable = "Sex") RunGSEA(obj,             group.by = "celltype.l2", split.by = "celltype.l1") RunCellChat(obj,         group.by = "CellType",    split.by = "Condition")

title: scSidekick - Single-Cell & Spatial Transcriptomics Toolkit
text: Honest--default plots — shared scales kept legends design, comparisons valid without per-call workarounds. Consistency free — register colours, factor levels, defaults PrepObject(); every downstream function reads , entire project shares one palette layout. Reproducibility captured passively — legend sidecar every figure, auto-drafted methods paragraph, one-call PPTX builder, session logger turns working session clean script. Scales big data — feature plots, pseudobulk, pathway scoring stream BPCells / -disk / Sketch assays; one-liners work 2M-cell atlas.

title: scSidekick - Single-Cell & Spatial Transcriptomics Toolkit
code: library(scSidekick) library(Seurat)  # Register colours, levels, and defaults once: obj <- PrepObject(obj,   variables   = c("Sample", "Group", "seurat_clusters"),   group.by    = "seurat_clusters",   split.by    = "Sample",   output_dir  = "./Figures",   object_name = "MyProject")  # Every downstream function reads those defaults automatically: PlotDimPlots(obj)                                   # UMAP split by Sample PlotMetaSummary(obj, variables = "seurat_clusters", # composition at a glance                 fill_variable = "Group", count_unit = "cells") PlotFeaturePlots(obj, features = c("CD3E", "CD79A", "LYZ")) GroupHeatmap(obj, features = marker_genes) RunGSEA(obj, group.by = "seurat_clusters") create_analysis_pptx(output_dir = "./Figures", object_name = "MyProject")

what: Annotate Seurat features with genomic metadata — AnnotateFeatures
title: Annotate Seurat features with genomic metadata — AnnotateFeatures
text: Adds chromosome location, gene biotype, Ensembl ID, genomic metadata columns feature-level metadata Seurat object. Annotation fetched (order): local cache → biomaRt → AnnotationHub/EnsDb → precomputed download → user-supplied file. Successful results cached ~/.cache/R/scSidekick/ network call made per species.

title: Annotate Seurat features with genomic metadata — AnnotateFeatures
code: AnnotateFeatures(   seurat_object,   species,   assay = NULL,   annotation_file = NULL,   force = FALSE )

title: Annotate Seurat features with genomic metadata — AnnotateFeatures
text: seurat_object Seurat object. species Character. One "mouse", "human", "zebrafish". assay Character. Assay annotate. Defaults DefaultAssay(). annotation_file Character NULL. Path user-supplied TSV/CSV (gene symbol rowname; recognized chromosome/start/end column names accepted). Bypasses automatic sources supplied. force Logical. Re-annotate even nk_chr column already present. Default FALSE.

title: Annotate Seurat features with genomic metadata — AnnotateFeatures
text: Seurat object new columns feature metadata: nk_chr, nk_start, nk_end, nk_strand, nk_biotype, nk_ensembl_id, nk_description.

what: Assign cell-type labels from a cluster map — AssignCellTypes
title: Assign cell-type labels from a cluster map — AssignCellTypes
text: Converts named list CellType -> cluster IDs new metadata column Seurat object. Clusters absent assignments receive NA.

title: Assign cell-type labels from a cluster map — AssignCellTypes
code: AssignCellTypes(   object,   assignments,   cluster_col = "Cluster",   new_col = "CellType",   other = NULL )

title: Assign cell-type labels from a cluster map — AssignCellTypes
text: object Seurat object. assignments Named list. Names cell-type labels; element character vector cluster IDs belonging type. E.g.: list(Excitatory_Neurons = c("C02","C03"), Astrocytes = "C05"). cluster_col Metadata column containing cluster IDs. Default "Cluster". new_col Name new metadata column create. Default "CellType". Character NULL. Label assigned cluster present assignments. NULL (default) leaves NA.

title: Assign cell-type labels from a cluster map — AssignCellTypes
text: Seurat object new_col added meta.data.

title: Assign cell-type labels from a cluster map — AssignCellTypes

what: Automated cell-type annotation with rcellmarker, scType, and SingleR — CellTypeAssignmentHelper
title: Automated cell-type annotation with rcellmarker, scType, and SingleR — CellTypeAssignmentHelper
text: Runs three automated cell-type annotation methods Seurat object, adds results new metadata columns, produces two summary PDFs: AutoAssignment umaps.pdf scType, rcellmarker, cluster UMAPs side--side. AutoAssignment umaps singleR.pdf + Assignment Helper.pdf combining annotations canonical-marker dotplots.

title: Automated cell-type annotation with rcellmarker, scType, and SingleR — CellTypeAssignmentHelper
code: CellTypeAssignmentHelper(   seurat_object,   markers,   cluster_column = "Cluster",   output_dir = NULL,   object_name = "",   subset_name = "",   reduction = "umap",   cluster_colors = NULL,   species = "human",   run_rcellmarker = TRUE,   run_sctype = TRUE,   sctype_tissues = c("Immune system", "Brain"),   sctype_assay = "RNA",   sctype_layer = "scale.data",   sctype_db = NULL,   run_singler = TRUE,   singler_ref = NULL,   singler_col = "SingleRAssignment",   markers_csv = NULL,   marker_groups = NULL,   top_n_per_cluster = 3L,   extra_dimplot_cols = NULL,   extra_col_colors = NULL,   col.min = 0,   col.max = 2,   scale.min = 0,   scale.max = 100,   caffeinate = FALSE )

title: Automated cell-type annotation with rcellmarker, scType, and SingleR — CellTypeAssignmentHelper
text: seurat_object Seurat object. markers Data frame DE results presto::wilcoxauc(). Required rcellmarker annotation top-marker dotplot. Expected columns: feature, group, avgExpr, avg_log2FC (logFC), auc, pval, padj, pct_in, pct_out. cluster_column Character. Metadata column holding cluster labels. Default "Cluster". output_dir Character. Directory PDFs. object_name Character. Prefix PDF filenames. subset_name Character. Second prefix component. reduction Character. UMAP reduction name. Default "umap". cluster_colors Named character vector colors cluster_column levels. NULL auto-generates Nour_pal(). species Character. "human" (default) "mouse". Controls: (1) gene column read markers CSV (Human vs Mouse), (2) rcellmarker species argument, (3) CellChatDB loaded downstream CellChat workflows. run_rcellmarker Logical. Run rcellmarker automated annotation? Requires rcellmarker package. Default TRUE. run_sctype Logical. Run scType automated annotation? Requires openxlsx. Default TRUE. sctype_tissues Character vector tissue type(s) query bundled ScTypeDB_full.xlsx. Combine multiple types broader coverage. valid values (bundled database): "Adrenal" - adrenal gland "Brain" - cerebral cortex, broad neuronal/glial types "Eye" - retinal eye-specific cell types "Heart" - cardiac cell types "Hippocampus" - hippocampal-specific subtypes (use "Brain" combined brain data) "Immune system" - broad immune cells: T, B, NK, myeloid, etc. "Intestine" - intestinal epithelial stromal types "Kidney" - renal cell types "Liver" - hepatocytes, Kupffer cells, stellate cells "Lung" - airway epithelium, alveolar cells, immune "Muscle" - skeletal smooth muscle "Pancreas" - islet exocrine types "Placenta" - trophoblast decidual types "Spleen" - splenic immune populations "Stomach" - gastric epithelial types "Thymus" - thymocyte thymic stromal types Recommended combinations: c("Brain", "Hippocampus") brain snRNAseq; c("Immune system", "Spleen") immune-rich tissues; c("Immune system", "Brain") neuroinflammation datasets. sctype_assay Character. Seurat assay use scoring. Must contain scale.data (scaled) layer. Common choices: "RNA" - standard full assay (default); use sctype_layer = "scale.data" "sketch" - BPCells sketch assay large datasets; use sctype_layer = "scale.data" ScaleData() sketch sctype_layer Character. Layer within sctype_assay holds scaled expression values. Default "scale.data". Must scaled (mean-centered) matrix - scType scores meaningful raw counts. sctype_db Character NULL. Path ScTypeDB_full.xlsx database file. NULL (default) uses copy bundled inst/extdata/ScTypeDB_full.xlsx; missing downloaded scType GitHub cached tools::R_user_dir("scSidekick", "cache"). run_singler Logical. Run SingleR cluster-level annotation? Requires SingleR, scuttle, celldex, SingleCellExperiment. Default TRUE. singler_ref reference SummarizedExperiment SingleR::SingleR(), NULL use celldex::BlueprintEncodeData() (default). Choose based tissue type species: Human references:  celldex::BlueprintEncodeData() Blueprint/ENCODE bulk RNA-seq. Broad cell types; good general-purpose starting point. Default. celldex::HumanPrimaryCellAtlasData() Human Primary Cell Atlas. broad coverage across many tissues; best highly heterogeneous datasets diverse cell populations. celldex::MonacoImmuneData() Monaco et al. Finest immune subtype resolution (29 populations). Best PBMCs, spleen, immune-focused study. celldex::DatabaseImmuneCellExpressionData() DICE database. Good alternative immune reference activated/resting states. celldex::NovershternHematopoieticData() Hematopoietic lineages. Best bone marrow stem-cell datasets. Mouse references:  celldex::ImmGenData() ImmGen consortium. comprehensive mouse immune cell reference (250 subtypes). celldex::MouseRNAseqData() Broad mouse tissue types bulk RNA-seq. Good non-immune mouse tissues. celldex references downloaded cached BiocFileCache; subsequent calls instant. singler_col Character. Name new metadata column storing numbered SingleR labels (e.g. "01.CD8+ T cells"). Default "SingleRAssignment". markers_csv Character NULL. Path canonical-marker CSV. NULL uses CSV bundled scSidekick (inst/extdata/Markers.csv). Required columns: Type, Cell types, plus Human /Mouse gene symbol columns. bundled CSV covers brain immune markers (see marker_groups full list Type values). marker_groups Named list controlling many dotplots produced Type values markers CSV appear . One SplitDotPlot() generated per list entry. NULL produces single dotplot markers combined. Type values bundled Markers.csv: "Brain Stroma" - Astrocytes, Oligodendrocytes, OPC, Microglia, Endothelial, Pericytes, Neurons (excitatory, inhibitory, dopaminergic, glutamatergic, serotonergic), VLMC, stem/progenitor types "Lymphocytes" - T cells (naive, cytotoxic, Th1/2/17, Treg, exhausted, MAIT, GDT), B cells, NK cells "Monocytes" - CD14/CD16 monocytes, MDSCs "Macrophages" - tissue macrophages "Dendritic cells" - cDC1, cDC2, pDCs, LAMP3+ DCs "Granulocytes" - neutrophils, basophils, mast cells "Bone Marrow Derived" - BMD immune cells "" - proliferating cells (Prol) "Tumor" - generic tumor marker Example brain snRNAseq separate immune panel:   top_n_per_cluster Integer. Number top DE genes per cluster shown cluster-faceted dotplot (uses markers data frame). Default 3. extra_dimplot_cols Character vector additional metadata columns include panels multi-UMAP summary figure (e.g. "Subclass" SEAAD reference labels "Donor.ID" QC). Colors auto-generated Nour_pal() unless supplied via extra_col_colors. extra_col_colors Named list color vectors extra_dimplot_cols. Names must match extra_dimplot_cols. Example: list(Subclass = subclass_colors). col.min Numeric. Minimum scaled expression value dotplot color scale. Default 0. col.max Numeric. Maximum scaled expression value dotplot color scale. Default 2. scale.min Numeric. Minimum percentage expressing cells dotplot size scale. Default 0. scale.max Numeric. Maximum percentage expressing cells dotplot size scale. Default 100. caffeinate Logical. TRUE, prevent macOS system sleep duration function call using caffeinate. Default FALSE.
code: marker_groups = list(   "Brain"  = "Brain Stroma",   "Immune" = c("Lymphocytes", "Monocytes", "Macrophages",                "Dendritic cells", "Granulocytes",                "Bone Marrow Derived", "All") )

title: Automated cell-type annotation with rcellmarker, scType, and SingleR — CellTypeAssignmentHelper
text: modified Seurat object (returned invisibly), : New metadata columns scType_CellType, rcellmarker_CellType, /column named singler_col, depending methods ran. seurat_object@misc$annotation_colors Named list color vectors annotation column: rcellmarker, sctype, singler, plus extra_dimplot_cols colors. Retrieve seurat_object@misc$annotation_colors$sctype etc.

what: Open the Chart Builder app pre-loaded with an R data frame — ChartBuilder
title: Open the Chart Builder app pre-loaded with an R data frame — ChartBuilder

title: Open the Chart Builder app pre-loaded with an R data frame — ChartBuilder

title: Open the Chart Builder app pre-loaded with an R data frame — ChartBuilder
text: data data.frame (object coercible .data.frame()), Seurat object - case meta.data slot used. app_dir Character. Path Chart Builder app directory (folder containing chart_builder.html). Defaults bundled copy inside scSidekick package (inst/Chart_Builder), getOption("nourkit.chart_builder_dir") set. filename Character. Name shown app loaded dataset. Defaults deparsed name data .csv suffix. max_rows Integer NULL. set data rows, random sample max_rows rows sent (keeps payload small). NULL (default) sends everything; app samples large data plotting. warning issued large frames. launch Logical. Open browser? Default TRUE. FALSE builds session directory returns path without opening (useful tests).

what: Verify sample sex annotation using sex-specific gene expression — CheckSex
title: Verify sample sex annotation using sex-specific gene expression — CheckSex
text: Compares sample's annotated sex pseudobulk expression sex-specific genes. Sex called sample level (averaged across cells donor), per cell. avoids false positives single-cell dropout: female cell may fail capture XIST given sequencing run, truly male sample zero XIST across cells.

title: Verify sample sex annotation using sex-specific gene expression — CheckSex
code: CheckSex(   seurat_object,   sex_col = "sex",   sample_col = "Sample",   cell_type_col = NULL,   species = NULL,   female_genes = NULL,   male_genes = NULL,   assay = NULL,   layer = "data",   noise_floor = 0.05,   output_dir = NULL,   plot = TRUE )

title: Verify sample sex annotation using sex-specific gene expression — CheckSex
text: seurat_object Seurat object. sex_col Character. Metadata column containing annotated sex. Accepted values: "F"/"M", "Female"/"Male", "female"/"male", "1"/"2". Default "sex". sample_col Character. Metadata column identifying donors/samples. Used per-donor pseudobulk aggregation. NULL skips sample-level analysis returns per-cell scores visualisation. Default "Sample". cell_type_col Character NULL. supplied, per-cell-type pseudobulk table computed addition sample-level table — useful distinguishing BMT/chimerism (cell-type-specific mismatch) labeling errors (cell types mismatched). Default NULL. species Character. "mouse", "human", "zebrafish". Auto- detected AnnotateFeatures() previously run. Zebrafish universal sex-gene defaults — supply female_genes male_genes. female_genes Character vector NULL. Override default female marker genes. NULL package defaults used (XIST/Tsix automatic X-paralog fallback). male_genes Character vector NULL. Override default male marker genes. NULL package defaults used. assay Character NULL. Assay pull expression . Defaults DefaultAssay(). layer Character. Layer within assay. Default "data" (log-normalized counts). Falls back "counts" automatically "data" present (BPCells objects). ~4–8 sex genes materialised memory — near-instantaneous even million-cell objects. noise_floor Numeric. Pseudobulk mean expression threshold separates "completely absent" "present" sample level. Default 0.05. applied sample mean (averaged hundreds–thousands cells), alignment-artifact signal X–Y paralog multi-mapping (typically 0.001–0.01 pseudobulk space) well threshold, true sex-gene expression (0.2–2.0) well . Raise 0.1 see "Undetermined" low-depth samples; lower 0.02 high-depth data. output_dir Character NULL. Directory saved figures CSV summary tables. Walks PrepObject() NULL. plot Logical. Generate save figures. Default TRUE.

title: Verify sample sex annotation using sex-specific gene expression — CheckSex
text: input Seurat object following metadata columns added: predicted_sex Sample-level sex call ("Female" / "Male" / "Ambiguous" / "Undetermined"), propagated identically cells within sample. sex_score_f Per-cell mean female-marker expression (visualisation; used mismatch decision). sex_score_m Per-cell mean male-marker expression. sex_ratio Per-cell log2(female_score + 0.01) / (male_score + 0.01). Positive = female signal, negative = male signal. Used continuous UMAP ratio plot. sex_mismatch Sample-level mismatch flag (TRUE/FALSE/NA), propagated cells sample. TRUE expected markers completely absent opposite markers present.

title: Verify sample sex annotation using sex-specific gene expression — CheckSex
text: sample flagged following hold: expected sex marker completely absent pseudobulk level (bulk mean ≤ noise_floor). opposite-sex marker clearly present pseudobulk level (bulk mean > noise_floor). strict two-condition rule avoids flags : Dropout (expected marker absent opposite signal either). X–Y paralog multi-mapping artifacts: DDX3X/DDX3Y, KDM6A/UTY, KDM5C/KDM5D share 70–85\ spurious Y-gene signal misaligned X reads. pseudobulk level artifact tiny (scattered reads cells) stays well noise_floor. Female-scoring modes (auto-selected): Primary (XIST-based): uses XIST/Xist, X-inactivation signal. specific available. Fallback (X-paralog dosage): uses DDX3X, KDM6A, KDM5C (mouse equivalents). Triggered XIST/Tsix absent panel (common lncRNAs filtered targeted panels used). mode mismatch called using sample-level prediction rather noise_floor threshold, X-paralogs expressed males lower (zero) levels. cell_type_col supplied, per-cell-type pseudobulk breakdown also computed. sample flagged specific cell types (e.g., immune cells) suggests BMT/chimerism; one flagged across cell types suggests labeling error.

Cell identities include. NULL uses shared identities. group.merged Named character vector NULL. Maps identity names broader categories chord group aggregation. Names must match groups.show. colors Named character vector mapping identity names colors. species Character. "human" "mouse". Default "human". thresh Numeric. Set thresh = 1 display predicted interactions regardless statistical significance - essential comparing pathways active conditions reach pathway-level significance others (.e. pathway cc@net$prob cc@netP$pathways). title: Compute pseudobulk (donor-level mean) expression — ComputePseudobulk

title: Compute pseudobulk (donor-level mean) expression — ComputePseudobulk

title: Compute pseudobulk (donor-level mean) expression — ComputePseudobulk
text: seurat_object Seurat object. group.Character vector.  Metadata columns together define one pseudobulk sample.  donor-level plots pass c("Donor.ID").  donor x cell-type plots pass c("Donor.ID", "CellType"). genes One : "variable" (default) Use variable features stored FindVariableFeatures(). NULL genes assay (can slow). Character vector Specific gene names. assay Character.  Seurat assay.  Default "RNA". layer Character.  Assay layer / slot.  Default "data" (log-normalized). min_cells Integer.  Drop pseudobulk groups fewer many cells.  Default 0L (keep groups). force Logical.  TRUE, discard existing cache group.key recompute scratch.  Use see "cache may stale" warning.  Default FALSE. caffeinate Logical.  TRUE, calls .nk_caffeinate() prevent system sleeping computation.  Useful large BPCells datasets take many minutes aggregate.  Default FALSE. verbose Logical.  Print progress messages.  Default TRUE.

title: Compute pseudobulk (donor-level mean) expression — ComputePseudobulk
text: seurat_object pseudobulk data frame stored seurat_object@misc$pseudobulk[[cache_key]].  data frame one row per unique group combination columns: group.columns, n_cells, one column per gene.

title: Compute pseudobulk (donor-level mean) expression — ComputePseudobulk
text: Aggregation uses single sparse matrix multiplication (genes x cells . cells x groups), making efficient BPCells / -disk assays: matrix streamed . called group.different (additional) genes, newly requested genes computed appended existing cache - already-cached genes never recomputed.

title: Compute pseudobulk (donor-level mean) expression — ComputePseudobulk
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Per-donor pseudobulk (variable genes) SeuratObj <- ComputePseudobulk(SeuratObj,   group.by = "Donor.ID")  # Per-donor x per-cell-type (specific genes) SeuratObj <- ComputePseudobulk(SeuratObj,   group.by = c("Donor.ID", "CellType"),   genes    = c("STAT3", "APOE", "CD3E")) } # }

what: Correlate one gene against all others within each cell type — CorrelateGene
title: Correlate one gene against all others within each cell type — CorrelateGene
text: Computes Spearman (Pearson) correlation gene interest (GOI) every gene, separately within cell type. Three resolution levels supported:

title: Correlate one gene against all others within each cell type — CorrelateGene
code: CorrelateGene(   seurat_object,   gene,   group_by,   level = c("sc", "pseudobulk", "group"),   compare_by = NULL,   sample_column = NULL,   group_column = NULL,   assay = NULL,   layer = "data",   method = c("spearman", "pearson"),   expressed_only = TRUE,   min_pct = 10,   min_cells = 20L,   min_donors = 5L,   min_groups = 3L )

title: Correlate one gene against all others within each cell type — CorrelateGene
text: seurat_object Seurat object. gene Character. gene interest (GOI). group_by Character. Cell-type identity column. level Character. "sc" (default), "pseudobulk", "group". compare_by Character NULL. Optional metadata column whose levels define independent comparison groups (e.g. "Sex", "Diagnosis"). supplied, correlation computed separately within level can ask genes correlated GOI group group B. sample_column Character. Donor/sample ID. Required "pseudobulk". group_column Character. Condition column. Required "group". assay Character NULL. Assay use. layer Character. Layer/slot SC group levels. Default "data". method Character. "spearman" (default) "pearson". expressed_only Logical. SC level: restrict GOI > 0 cells? Default TRUE. min_pct Numeric. SC level: minimum % (GOI-expressing) cells must express tested gene. Default 10. min_cells Integer. SC level: minimum cells per cell type. Default 20. min_donors Integer. Pseudobulk level: minimum donors. Default 5. min_groups Integer. Group level: minimum groups. Default 3.

title: Correlate one gene against all others within each cell type — CorrelateGene
text: data frame: gene, cell_type, r, p, padj, n, goi, level, method, (compare_by set) compare_group. Sorted cell type descending |r|.

title: Correlate one gene against all others within each cell type — CorrelateGene
text: "sc": cell level. Restricts cells GOI > 0 (expressed_only = TRUE) default avoid co-absence artifacts dropout. Exploratory; validate hits pseudobulk level. "pseudobulk": donor level. Aggregates raw counts per donor x cell type, converts edgeR log-CPM, runs Spearman across donors. Statistically valid. "group": averages log-normalized expression within group_column x cell_type combination, correlates across groups. compare_by supplied, analysis run separately within level variable (e.g. compare_by = "Sex" produces independent correlation tables males females). Results gain compare_group column PlotCorrelation() lay groups side side.

what: Determine the optimal number of PCs for downstream analysis — Determine_PCs
title: Determine the optimal number of PCs for downstream analysis — Determine_PCs
text: Uses two complementary heuristics select number principal components use UMAP, clustering, neighbor-graph construction:

title: Determine the optimal number of PCs for downstream analysis — Determine_PCs
code: Determine_PCs(   seurat_object,   threshold_pct = 5,   reduction = "pca",   threshold_cum_pct = 90,   threshold_diff_pct = 0.1,   elbow_plot = TRUE,   red = NULL )

title: Determine the optimal number of PCs for downstream analysis — Determine_PCs
text: seurat_object Seurat object PCA (linear) reduction already computed. threshold_pct Numeric. Maximum per-PC variance (%) selected PC may contribute. Default 5. threshold_cum_pct Numeric. Cumulative variance (%) threshold first heuristic. Default 90. threshold_diff_pct Numeric. Minimum absolute drop per-PC variance consecutive PCs elbow heuristic. Default 0.1. elbow_plot Logical. TRUE (default), returns annotated elbow plot fourth element output list. red Character. Name reduction inspect. Default "pca".

title: Determine the optimal number of PCs for downstream analysis — Determine_PCs
text: named list four elements: pcs Recommended number PCs (integer). cum_thresh PC index cumulative-variance heuristic. elbow_thresh PC index elbow heuristic. plot ggplot2 elbow plot (NULL elbow_plot = FALSE).

title: Determine the optimal number of PCs for downstream analysis — Determine_PCs
text: Cumulative variance threshold: first PC cumulative variance explained exceeds threshold_cum_pct per-PC variance drops threshold_pct. Elbow heuristic: last PC absolute drop per-PC variance consecutive PCs exceeds threshold_diff_pct. recommended number PCs minimum two thresholds, conservative (safer) choice.

what: Determine the optimal number of dimensions for downstream analysis — Determine_nDims
title: Determine the optimal number of dimensions for downstream analysis — Determine_nDims
text: Uses two complementary heuristics choose many dimensions linear reduction (PCA, LSI, etc.) pass UMAP, neighbor graphs, clustering.

title: Determine the optimal number of dimensions for downstream analysis — Determine_nDims
code: Determine_nDims(   seurat_object,   threshold_pct = 5,   reduction = "pca",   threshold_cum_pct = 90,   threshold_diff_pct = 0.1,   elbow_plot = TRUE )

title: Determine the optimal number of dimensions for downstream analysis — Determine_nDims
text: seurat_object Seurat object target reduction already computed (e.g. via RunPCA() RunLSI()). threshold_pct Numeric. selected dimension must contribute less percentage total variance. Default 5. reduction Character. Name reduction slot inspect. Default "pca". threshold_cum_pct Numeric. Cumulative variance (%) must reached Heuristic 1 can trigger. Default 90. threshold_diff_pct Numeric. Minimum drop per-dimension variance consecutive dimensions Heuristic 2. Default 0.1. elbow_plot Logical. Return annotated elbow plot? Default TRUE.

title: Determine the optimal number of dimensions for downstream analysis — Determine_nDims
text: named list: n_dims Recommended number dimensions (integer). cum_thresh Dimension selected Heuristic 1. elbow_thresh Dimension selected Heuristic 2. plot ggplot2 elbow plot, NULL elbow_plot = FALSE.

title: Determine the optimal number of dimensions for downstream analysis — Determine_nDims
text: Heuristic 1 - cumulative variance plateau: finds first dimension cumulative variance explained exceeds threshold_cum_pct per-dimension contribution already dropped threshold_pct. marks point diminishing returns. Heuristic 2 - elbow: finds last dimension drop per-dimension variance successive dimensions still larger threshold_diff_pct. Beyond point curve flattened. function returns minimum two, conservative choice.

title: Enrich TCGA metadata with GDC clinical and molecular subtype columns — EnrichTCGAMeta
text: Adds comprehensive clinical data TCGA marker-paper molecular subtype columns existing metadata data frame — without re-downloading expression matrix.  fastest way add paper_IDH.status, paper_Subtype, paper_CIMP.status, tumour stage, treatment history, GDC variables dataset previously loaded LoadTCGA.

title: Enrich TCGA metadata with GDC clinical and molecular subtype columns — EnrichTCGAMeta
text: tcga_data list returned LoadTCGA, named list least $meta data frame $project string. project Character scalar.  TCGA project ID (e.g. "TCGA-GBM"). Required tcga_data carry $project element (e.g. passing raw metadata data frame).

title: Enrich TCGA metadata with GDC clinical and molecular subtype columns — EnrichTCGAMeta
text: input list $meta replaced enriched data frame.  original columns preserved; new columns appended. metadata data frame supplied, data frame returned.

title: Enrich TCGA metadata with GDC clinical and molecular subtype columns — EnrichTCGAMeta
text: Internally calls two fast GDC REST API endpoints via TCGAbiolinks (per-patient file downloads): TCGAbiolinks::GDCquery_clinic() — comprehensive clinical table (age diagnosis, pathologic stage, race, vital status, treatment, etc.) TCGAbiolinks::TCGAquery_subtype() — TCGA marker-paper molecular subtypes (paper_* columns), available major cancer types. Requires TCGAbiolinks: BiocManager::install("TCGAbiolinks").

title: Enrich TCGA metadata with GDC clinical and molecular subtype columns — EnrichTCGAMeta
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Typical workflow after first LoadTCGA call gbm <- LoadTCGA("TCGA-GBM") gbm <- EnrichTCGAMeta(gbm) SurvMetaSummary(gbm$meta)   # now shows paper_IDH.status, stage, etc.  # Or enrich a bare metadata data frame meta_enriched <- EnrichTCGAMeta(gbm$meta, project = "TCGA-GBM") } # }

what: Create a standard project directory tree — GenerateDirectories

text: Builds conventional scSidekick analysis folder layout base_dir returns every created path named list.  Replaces usual pile paste0() + dir.create() lines top analysis script. tree created :
code: base_dir/   R_Objects/                  # saved Seurat objects (.rds)   R_Code/                     # analysis scripts   Output/                     # all figures / tables     <subset>/                 # one analysis branch (e.g. "All_Clusters")       Featuremaps/            # (and any other `output_subdirs`)

title: Create a standard project directory tree — GenerateDirectories
text: base_dir Character. Project root directory. Created absent. subset Character. Name analysis branch Output/ (e.g. "All_Clusters", "Tcells"). Default "All_Clusters". object_name Character NULL. Project label. Stored returned list (used downstream functions file prefixes); affect folder structure. Default NULL. resolution Numeric NULL. Clustering resolution. Stored returned list reference . Default NULL. output_subdirs Character vector. Subfolders create inside Output/<subset>/ branch. Default "Featuremaps". Pass character(0) none, e.g. c("Featuremaps", "DotPlots", "Pathways"). extra_top Character vector NULL. Additional top-level folders create directly base_dir (beyond R_Objects, R_Code, Output). Default NULL. set_wd Logical. Set working directory base_dir? Default FALSE. verbose Logical. Message folder created? Default TRUE.

title: Create a standard project directory tree — GenerateDirectories

text: seurat_object Seurat object. assay Character. assay use expression values. Default "RNA". reduction Character. Dimensional reduction UMAP. Default "umap". features Character vector gene names plot. layout_method Character. "auto" (default) "metadata". "metadata" mode row.must specified. split.Character. Metadata column split UMAP panels . row.Character NULL. "metadata" layout, metadata column whose levels define rows grid. colors Character vector. Color gradient low high expression. output_dir Character NULL. Directory write PDFs. NULL, plot returned instead saved. object_name Character. Label appended PDF filenames. Default "". subset_name Character. Second label appended PDF filenames. Default "". order Logical. Plot higher-expression cells top. Default TRUE. label Logical. Overlay cell-type / cluster labels UMAP panels. Default FALSE. label.Character. Metadata column use labels.  NULL (default) label = TRUE, labels drawn using Seurat::Idents(). Accepts metadata column - avoids Seurat's ident-order problem error occurs column literally named "idents". label_colors Named character vector mapping label value hex color (e.g. c("Excitatory" = "#E41A1C", "Inhibitory" = "#377EB8")). Labels present vector default "black".  NULL = black. repel Logical. Use ggrepel::geom_text_repel avoid overlapping labels (default FALSE).  repel fails due output-size constraints message printed panel skipped rather crashing. label.size Numeric. Label font size. Default 2. pt.size Numeric. Point size. Default 0.001. add_boxplot Logical. Append expression violin/box plot column. Default FALSE. Requires split.set. plot_type Character. Type expression plot: "violin" (default), "box", "" (violin thin white box overlay). alpha Numeric. Fill transparency violin box geoms. Default 0.7. ref_group Character NULL. set, group compared reference level. NULL (default) performs pairwise tests. comparisons List character vectors NULL. Custom pairs test, e.g. list(c("NCI","AD"), c("MCI","AD")). Overrides ref_group. group_colors Named character vector NULL. Fill colors split.level. NULL auto-assigns Nour18. label_format Character. "p.signif" (default, shows */**/ns) "p.format" (shows p = 0.023). hide_ns Logical. TRUE, omit ns brackets entirely. Default FALSE (shows ns gray).

text: seurat_object Seurat object spatial images. group_by_vars Character vector. Metadata columns plot (one PDF per variable). layout_method Character. "auto" "metadata". Default "auto". row.Character NULL. Slide-level metadata column row grouping (required "metadata" layout). images_to_plot Character vector NULL. Images include. remove_outliers Logical. CellType"). Useful many categories. Default FALSE. output_dir Character NULL. PDF output directory. Returns last plot NULL. rowannsize Numeric. Font size row-group annotation label drawn left tissue row "metadata" layout. Default 16. imgalpha Numeric. Transparency tissue background image (0 = fully transparent, 1 = fully opaque). Default 1. alpha Numeric. Transparency colored spot points (0 = fully transparent, 1 = fully opaque). Default 1. object_name Character. Label appended PDF filenames. Default "". group.Character vector. Preferred alias group_by_vars. supplied, group_by_vars ignored. cluster_colors Named character vector. Deprecated alias colors. Kept backward compatibility.

text: Iterates list samples, reads 10X count data, optionally adds per-cell annotation CSVs, attaches per-sample metadata, runs DoubletFinder miQC quality control, saves per-sample QC plots, finally merges objects single Seurat object.

title: Load, QC, and merge 10X scRNA-seq samples — LoadSamplesRNA
code: LoadSamplesRNA(   sample_ids,   run_ids = NULL,   run_ids_x = NULL,   input_type = c("h5", "10x_dir"),   data_paths = NULL,   metadata = NULL,   sample_id_col = "sample_id",   base_dir = NULL,   h5_template = paste0("{base_dir}/{run_id}/per_sample_outs/", "{run_id_x}/count/",     "sample_filtered_feature_bc_matrix.h5"),   matrix_dir_template = "{base_dir}/{run_id}/filtered_feature_bc_matrix",   cellanno_template = paste0("{base_dir}/{run_id}/per_sample_outs/", "{run_id_x}/count/",     "cell_types/cell_types.csv"),   output_dir,   robj_dir = NULL,   merged_filename = "merged_object.rds",   save_individual = FALSE,   resume = FALSE,   individual_dir = NULL,   min.features = 100L,   mt_pattern = "^MT-",   run_doublet = TRUE,   doublet_rate = NULL,   run_miqc = TRUE,   miqc_posterior = 0.95,   force_assay_v3 = TRUE,   merge_samples = TRUE,   add_cell_ids = NULL,   modality = c("scRNA-seq", "snRNA-seq") )

title: Load, QC, and merge 10X scRNA-seq samples — LoadSamplesRNA
text: sample_ids Character vector human-readable sample IDs (e.g. c("HC1","HC2","AD1")). Used Seurat project names key column matching metadata. run_ids Character vector Cellranger run IDs corresponding sample_ids (e.g. paste0("MYSC", 220:223)). Used build h5 / matrix paths cell-id prefixes merge. NULL (allowed data_paths supplied) falls back sample_ids. run_ids_x Character vector NULL. "underscored" form run_ids used per_sample_outs/ subdirectory names (e.g. paste0("MYSC_", 220:223)). NULL auto-generates inserting _ trailing digit block: "MYSC220" → "MYSC_220". input_type Character. read sample's counts: "h5" (default) uses Seurat::Read10X_h5(); "10x_dir" uses Seurat::Read10X() matrix folder. Ignored data_paths already points right kind source (type still used pick reader, set match data_paths). data_paths Character vector NULL. Exact path sample's data source (h5 file input_type = "h5", matrix folder input_type = "10x_dir"), one entry per sample_ids. supplied, base_dir path templates bypassed entirely. Default NULL. metadata Data frame NULL. One row per sample; must contain column matching sample_id_col. columns attached @meta.data. NULL skips step (metadata must added manually afterwards). sample_id_col Character. Name column metadata matches sample_ids. Default "sample_id". base_dir Character NULL. Root directory containing Cellranger run folders (one subfolder per run_id). Required unless data_paths supplied. h5_template Character. Path template h5 file (input_type = "h5"). Available placeholders: {base_dir}, {run_id}, {run_id_x}. Default matches Cellranger-multi per-sample output layout. matrix_dir_template Character. Path template 10X matrix folder (input_type = "10x_dir"). placeholders h5_template. Default "{base_dir}/{run_id}/filtered_feature_bc_matrix". cellanno_template Character NULL. Path template per-cell annotation CSV (e.g. Cellranger cell-type calling). NULL disables cell annotation loading. Files exist silently skipped per sample - safe provide template even samples file. output_dir Character. Directory per-sample QC PDF plots. robj_dir Character NULL. Directory saving final merged Seurat object. NULL skips saving. merged_filename Character. Filename saved merged object. Default "merged_object.rds". save_individual Logical. Save fully-processed sample <sample_id>.rds individual_dir finishes. Combined resume = TRUE, lets long load survive interruptions. Default FALSE. resume Logical. processing sample, check individual_dir existing <sample_id>.rds; found, load skip re-processing (read, QC, doublets, miQC) sample. Default FALSE. individual_dir Character NULL. Directory per-sample .rds files used save_individual / resume. NULL defaults <robj_dir output_dir>/Individual_Samples. min.features Integer. Minimum features per cell CreateSeuratObject(). Default 100. mt_pattern Character. Regex pattern mitochondrial genes. Default "^MT-" (human). Use "^mt-" mouse. run_doublet Logical. Run SCP::db_scDblFinder() doublet detection? Default TRUE. doublet_rate Numeric NULL. Expected doublet rate per 1 000 cells loaded. NULL auto-computes ncol(seurat_object) / 1000 * 0.01. run_miqc Logical. Run SeuratWrappers::RunMiQC()? Default TRUE. miqc_posterior Numeric. miQC posterior probability cutoff. Cells flagged low quality. Default 0.95. force_assay_v3 Logical. Convert RNA assay Seurat v3 format ((seurat_object[["RNA"]], "Assay")) creation? Needed compatibility older packages. Default TRUE. merge_samples Logical. Merge sample objects one Seurat object end? Default TRUE. add_cell_ids Character vector NULL. Prefix added cell barcode merge. NULL uses run_ids. modality Character. Sequencing modality label stored merged object's metadata @misc$scSidekick_params. One "scRNA-seq" (default) "snRNA-seq".

title: Load, QC, and merge 10X scRNA-seq samples — LoadSamplesRNA
text: merge_samples = TRUE, merged Seurat object (invisibly). merge_samples = FALSE, named list individual Seurat objects.

title: Load, QC, and merge 10X scRNA-seq samples — LoadSamplesRNA
text: Three ways point function data: h5 files via templates (default, input_type = "h5"): paths built base_dir + h5_template using Read10X_h5(). Matches Cellranger-multi output. 10X matrix folders via templates (input_type = "10x_dir"): paths built base_dir + matrix_dir_template read Read10X(). folder must contain barcodes.tsv(.gz), features.tsv(.gz), matrix.mtx(.gz) (Cellranger filtered_feature_bc_matrix/ directory). Explicit paths (data_paths = ...): character vector exact h5 files matrix folders, one per sample, used verbatim. Use samples organized consistent layout. base_dir templates ignored path building.

title: Load, QC, and merge 10X scRNA-seq samples — LoadSamplesRNA
text: Pass data.frame one row per sample.  column named sample_id_col must match sample_ids exactly.  Every column added @meta.data automatically.
code: sample_meta <- data.frame(   sample_id = c("HC1","HC2","AD1","FTD1"),   run_id    = c("MYSC220","MYSC221","MYSC222","MYSC223"),   patient   = c("Firstable","Cododge","Transferism","Promiseable"),   group     = c("HC","HC","AD","FTD"),   sex       = c("M","F","M","F"),   batch     = c(1L, 1L, 1L, 1L) )

title: Load, QC, and merge 10X scRNA-seq samples — LoadSamplesRNA
text: Paths built base_dir, run_id, run_id_x using {placeholder} substitution.  defaults match Cellranger-multi output:   Override h5_template cellanno_template layouts.
code: {base_dir}/{run_id}/per_sample_outs/{run_id_x}/count/   sample_filtered_feature_bc_matrix.h5

what: Download and cache a TCGA or TARGET expression dataset — LoadTCGA
title: Download and cache a TCGA or TARGET expression dataset — LoadTCGA
text: Downloads consolidated expression matrix clinical metadata TCGA TARGET project caches result locally .rds file.  Subsequent calls arguments load cache instantly without re-downloading.

title: Download and cache a TCGA or TARGET expression dataset — LoadTCGA
code: LoadTCGA(   project,   data_type = c("rnaseq", "microarray"),   backend = c("recount3", "TCGAbiolinks"),   cache_dir = NULL,   force_reload = FALSE )

title: Download and cache a TCGA or TARGET expression dataset — LoadTCGA
text: project Character scalar.  Project ID, e.g. "TCGA-GBM", "TCGA-LGG", "TARGET-NBL".  See SurvPlot full list available projects. data_type "rnaseq" (default) "microarray". "microarray" forces backend = "TCGAbiolinks" automatically. backend Download engine.  "recount3" (default) fetches single pre-consolidated matrix — fast reliable. "TCGAbiolinks" downloads one file per patient GDC API — use recount3 carry project microarray data. cache_dir Directory cached .rds file.  Defaults OS user cache directory scSidekick. force_reload Logical (default FALSE).  TRUE, deletes existing .rds cache file re-downloads dataset scratch.  Useful adding TCGAbiolinks upstream data updated.  new download cached usual.

title: Download and cache a TCGA or TARGET expression dataset — LoadTCGA
text: named list : expr_mat Numeric matrix, genes × samples, log2(TPM + 1) values. meta Data frame clinical metadata, one row per sample. project Project ID string. data_type Data modality used. backend Download engine used.

title: Download and cache a TCGA or TARGET expression dataset — LoadTCGA

title: Download and cache a TCGA or TARGET expression dataset — LoadTCGA

what: Prepend or append sequential numbers to factor levels — NumberLabels
title: Prepend or append sequential numbers to factor levels — NumberLabels
text: Two modes: Mode 1 (id_by) Numbers levels sequentially: "01.B cell", "02.CD4 T", …  Useful shortening long names UMAP centroid labels keeping full name legend. Mode 2 (id_by) Creates group × id interaction label.  unique value id_by within group gets sequential number: "Male_1", "Male_2", "Female_1", …

title: Prepend or append sequential numbers to factor levels — NumberLabels
code: NumberLabels(   x,   id_by = NULL,   position = c("before", "after"),   sep = ".",   pad_width = NULL )

title: Prepend or append sequential numbers to factor levels — NumberLabels
text: x vector (factor, character, numeric). id_by Optional vector length x.  supplied, enables Mode 2: one sequential number per unique id_by value within level x. position "" (default) places number label; "" places . sep Separator number label.  Default ".". pad_width Integer.  Width zero-padding.  NULL (default) auto-detects based maximum number.

title: Prepend or append sequential numbers to factor levels — NumberLabels
text: factor relabeled levels.

title: Prepend or append sequential numbers to factor levels — NumberLabels
text: x already factor converted automatically (message).  Run SetLevels() first need specific level order.

title: Prepend or append sequential numbers to factor levels — NumberLabels
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Mode 1: number long SingleR labels for UMAP SeuratObj$SingleRAssignment <- NumberLabels(SeuratObj$SingleRAssignment) # levels: "01.B cell", "02.CD4 T", ...  # Mode 2: one number per patient within each Sex group SeuratObj$SexByPatient <- NumberLabels(SeuratObj$Sex, id_by = SeuratObj$patient) # levels: "Female_1", "Female_2", "Male_1", "Male_2", ...  # Append number after, custom separator NumberLabels(SeuratObj$CellType, position = "after", sep = " - ") } # }

title: Parallel Sets (Alluvial) Plot — PlotAlluvial
text: Creates parallel sets (alluvial) chart - clean alternative SCP's alluvials preserves color palettes theme. Supports two axes via axes parameter.  Passing three columns (e.g. axes = c("BraakStage", "ThalPhase", "CERAD")) produces multi-axis diagram ideal multiparametric comparisons. Use donor.aggregate donor / patient level computing counts - streams show donor flows rather cell flows. recommended approach neuropathology scores, clinical metadata, variable defined per-individual rather per-cell. facet.supplied, one alluvial panel drawn per level assembled patchwork.  Plot margins auto-expanded based longest axis label text never clipped.

title: Parallel Sets (Alluvial) Plot — PlotAlluvial
code: PlotAlluvial(   data,   axes = NULL,   group.by = NULL,   split.by = NULL,   donor.by = NULL,   facet.by = NULL,   ncol_facet = NULL,   alpha = 0.75,   axis_width = 0.1,   label = TRUE,   label_size = 4,   label_nudge = 0.1,   group_colors = NULL,   split_colors = NULL,   axes_colors = list(),   output_dir = NULL,   object_name = "",   pdf_width = 7,   pdf_height = 5.5 )

title: Parallel Sets (Alluvial) Plot — PlotAlluvial
text: data Seurat object plain data.frame/tibble containing metadata columns. axes Character vector 2 column names use axes, left right (e.g. c("BraakStage", "ThalPhase", "CERAD")). Stream color determined first axis. group.Character. Left-axis column (retained backwards compatibility).  Ignored axes supplied. split.Character. Right-axis column (retained backwards compatibility).  Ignored axes supplied. donor.Character NULL.  Metadata column identifying donor / patient (e.g. "Donor.ID").  supplied, metadata first deduplicated one row per donor stream widths reflect donor counts rather cell counts - right choice patient-level pathological scores (BraakStage, Thal phase, CERAD, etc.). Default NULL. facet.Character NULL.  Metadata column facet - one alluvial panel per level (e.g. "Diagnosis").  Default NULL. ncol_facet Integer NULL.  Number panel columns facet.used.  Auto-computed (max 3) NULL. alpha Numeric. Stream transparency. Default 0.75. axis_width Numeric. Width axis rectangles.  stream width 1.5 × axis_width.  Default 0.1. label Logical. Annotate axis segment level name. Default TRUE. label_size Numeric. Label font size. Default 4. label_nudge Numeric. Horizontal gap axis bar label. Default 0.1. group_colors Named character vector colors first axis (streams + left axis).  Auto-assigned NULL. split_colors Named character vector colors last axis. Auto-assigned NULL. axes_colors Named list color vectors, keyed column name (e.g. list(BraakStage = BraakColors, ThalPhase = ThalColors)). Takes precedence group_colors/split_colors. output_dir Directory save PDF.  NULL = save. object_name Label prefix output filename. pdf_width, pdf_height PDF dimensions per panel inches.  Default 7 x 5.5.

title: Parallel Sets (Alluvial) Plot — PlotAlluvial
text: ggplot2 patchwork object (invisibly).

title: Dataset Overview Wheel — PlotAtlasWheel
text: Creates circular "atlas wheel" summarises large multi-cohort dataset.  wedge represents one level group.(e.g. cancer type, tissue, condition) annotated names contributing studies.  Summary statistics (total cells, donors, groups, optionally normal-tissue types) placed center. Works Seurat objects plain data.frames. facet.supplied, one wheel built per level, assembled side--side patchwork.  filename includes "by_{facet.}".  Outer label margins auto-expanded based longest group-level name.

title: Dataset Overview Wheel — PlotAtlasWheel
code: PlotAtlasWheel(   meta,   group.by,   study_by = NULL,   patient_by = NULL,   facet.by = NULL,   ncol_facet = NULL,   exclude_groups = NULL,   normal_col = NULL,   normal_val = NULL,   normal_count_by = NULL,   title = "Dataset Overview",   cell_label = "Cells",   show_stats = TRUE,   colors = NULL,   show_circles = TRUE,   circle_size = 18,   wedge_alpha = 0.5,   y_inner = -1,   y_outer = 10,   y_text = 7,   y_label = 11,   y_circle = 1.5,   y_min = -15,   y_center_title = -5.5,   y_center_stats = -13,   label_size = 4,   study_text_size = 2.2,   study_lineheight = 0.9,   title_size = 7,   stats_size = 5,   title_color = "#1e4e5f",   output_dir = NULL,   object_name = "",   pdf_width = 9,   pdf_height = 9 )

title: Dataset Overview Wheel — PlotAtlasWheel
text: meta Seurat object plain data.frame/tibble per-cell (per-sample) metadata. group.Character.  Primary grouping column - one wedge per level (e.g. "CancerType"). study_by Character NULL.  Column whose values pasted inside wedge multi-line study label (e.g. "Study"). NULL, inner text drawn. patient_by Character NULL.  Column identifying individual donors / patients - used count unique donors summary statistics. facet.Character NULL.  Metadata column facet - one wheel per level (e.g. "Tissue").  Default NULL. ncol_facet Integer NULL.  Number wheel columns facet.used.  Auto-computed (max 2) NULL. exclude_groups Character vector NULL.  Levels group.exclude wheel , whose cells still counted center statistics.  Typical use: exclude_groups = "Healthy". normal_col, normal_val Character NULL.  Column value used identify normal (non-disease) samples (e.g. normal_col = "Health", normal_val = "Healthy"). normal_count_by Character NULL.  Column count distinct normal tissue types (e.g. "Organ"). title Character.  Main title text center wheel. Supports "\n" line breaks. cell_label Character.  Descriptor appended cell count (e.g. "Myeloid Cells").  Default "Cells". show_stats Logical.  Show cell / donor / group counts center. Default TRUE. colors Named character vector mapping group.levels colors.  Auto-assigned NULL. show_circles Logical.  Draw white placeholder circle inner base wedge.  Default TRUE. circle_size Numeric.  size placeholder circles. Default 18. wedge_alpha Numeric.  Transparency wedge fill.  Default 0.5. y_inner Numeric.  Inner y edge wedge ring (top center hole).  Default -1. y_outer Numeric.  Outer y edge wedge ring.  Default 10. y_text Numeric.  y position study-name text inside wedge.  Default 7. y_label Numeric.  y position outer group-name labels. Default 11. y_circle Numeric.  y position inner placeholder circles. Default 1.5. y_min Numeric.  Bottom y-axis range.  Default -15. y_center_title Numeric.  y position center title annotation. Default -5.5. y_center_stats Numeric.  y position center statistics. Default -13. label_size Numeric.  Font size outer group-name labels. Default 4. study_text_size Numeric.  Font size study-name text inside wedges.  Default 2.2. study_lineheight Numeric.  Line height multi-line study labels. Default 0.9. title_size Numeric.  Font size center title.  Default 7. stats_size Numeric.  Font size center statistics text. Default 5. title_color Character.  Color center title circle borders.  Default "#1e4e5f". output_dir Directory save PDF.  NULL = save. object_name Label prefix output filename. pdf_width, pdf_height PDF dimensions per wheel inches.  Default 9 x 9.

what: Chord Diagram Between Two Categorical Variables — PlotChord
title: Chord Diagram Between Two Categorical Variables — PlotChord
text: Draws chord diagram showing connectivity two metadata variables (e.g. cell-type assignment sample).  arc represents number cells shared level var1 level var2.  Uses circlize. circlize draws active graphics device (base R graphics), function returns contingency matrix invisibly rather ggplot2 object. facet.supplied, chord diagrams arranged side--side grid single PDF page using par(fig = ..., new = TRUE).  PDF width/height scale automatically number panels.  filename includes "by_{facet.}". canvas_padding expands circlize canvas long sector labels never clipped - increase still see clipping.

title: Chord Diagram Between Two Categorical Variables — PlotChord
code: PlotChord(   data,   var1,   var2,   donor.by = NULL,   facet.by = NULL,   ncol_facet = NULL,   canvas_padding = 0.3,   var1_colors = NULL,   var2_colors = NULL,   alpha = 0.8,   label_cex = 0.8,   directional = FALSE,   output_dir = NULL,   object_name = "",   pdf_width = 7,   pdf_height = 7 )

title: Chord Diagram Between Two Categorical Variables — PlotChord
text: data Seurat object plain data.frame/tibble. var1 Character. First variable (e.g. "BraakStage").  Sectors var1 levels appear first diagram. var2 Character. Second variable (e.g. "ThalPhase"). donor.Character NULL.  Column identifying donor / patient.  supplied, metadata deduplicated one row per donor arc widths reflect donor counts - right choice patient-level pathological clinical variable.  Default NULL. facet.Character NULL.  Metadata column facet - one chord diagram per level, arranged side--side grid.  Default NULL. ncol_facet Integer NULL.  Number columns chord grid facet.used.  Auto-computed (max 3) NULL. canvas_padding Numeric.  Extra space added around chord circle (canvas units) prevent sector labels clipped.  canvas runs -1 - padding 1 + padding axis. Default 0.3.  Increase e.g. 0.5 long labels. var1_colors Named character vector var1 levels. Auto-assigned NULL. var2_colors Named character vector var2 levels. Auto-assigned NULL. alpha Numeric. Chord fill opacity (0-1).  Default 0.8. label_cex Numeric. Sector label font size multiplier.  Default 0.8. directional Logical.  Draw directional arrows var1 var2.  Default FALSE. output_dir Directory save PDF.  NULL = save. object_name Label prefix output filename. pdf_width, pdf_height PDF dimensions per panel inches. Total PDF width = pdf_width × ncol; total height = pdf_height   × nrow.  Default 7 x 7.
