title: UMAP grid with optional composition trend panel — PlotDimPlots
text: seurat_object Seurat object. group.Character. Metadata column point color. split.Character. Metadata column columns (samples/conditions). row.Character NULL. Metadata column rows. number_labels Logical. TRUE, prefix legend entry centroid label zero-padded index (e.g., "01. CellType"). Useful many groups alphanumeric ordering aids interpretation. Default FALSE. show_trend Logical. Add stacked composition trend panel left. Default FALSE. show_labels Logical. Overlay numbered cluster labels. Default FALSE. label.Character NULL. Column label centroids. NULL → group.. colors Named color vector. NULL auto-resolves PrepObject Nour_pal. reduction Character. Reduction name (case-insensitive). pt.size Numeric. Point size. Default 0.1. label.size Numeric. Label text size. Default 3. bar.width Numeric. Bar width trend plot. Default 0.6. legendnrow Integer. Legend rows. Default 2. legendtitle Character NA. Legend title. trend_width Numeric. Relative width trend panel vs. one UMAP column. Default 1. split.col.levels Character vector. Explicit ordering split.levels. width Numeric NULL. Saved PDF width inches. NULL (default) auto-sizes fit panel grid legend. height Numeric NULL. Saved PDF height inches. NULL (default) auto-sizes number rows plus legend strip. join_plots Logical. group.one variable, combine per-variable plots single figure / PDF instead one file . Default FALSE. join_nrow, join_ncol Integer NULL. Grid layout join_plots. NULL auto-arranges near-square grid. output_dir Character NULL. Directory save PDF. object_name Character. Output file-name prefix. subset_name Character. Optional second prefix. file_name Character NULL. Base name (extension) saved PDF. NULL (default) auto-deduces object_name, subset_name, group.. ... Ignored (forward compatibility).

title: UMAP grid with optional composition trend panel — PlotDimPlots
text: patchwork combined plot.  multiple group.join_plots = FALSE, named list plots (one per variable).

title: UMAP grid with optional composition trend panel — PlotDimPlots
text: PlotGridUMAP(): Deprecated - use PlotDimPlots(show_trend=FALSE). PlotTrendAndUMAP(): Deprecated - use PlotDimPlots(show_trend=TRUE).

title: Over-Representation Analysis Visualization — PlotEnrichment
text: Produces bar, dot, lollipop plots ORA enrichment results (enrichR, clusterProfiler, gprofiler2, table term names p-values). Column names auto-detected common output formats; override *_column parameters needed.

title: Over-Representation Analysis Visualization — PlotEnrichment
code: PlotEnrichment(   enrichment_df,   term_column = NULL,   padj_column = NULL,   gene_count_column = NULL,   gene_ratio_column = NULL,   group_column = NULL,   top_n = 20,   plot_type = "dot",   x_by = "gene_ratio",   color_by = "padj",   colors = c("#B40426", "#FDDBC7", "#D1E5F0", "#3B4CC0"),   max_term_length = 50,   output_dir = NULL,   object_name = "",   subset_name = "" )

title: Over-Representation Analysis Visualization — PlotEnrichment
text: enrichment_df Data frame ORA results, character path CSV file. term_column Column term/pathway names. Auto-detected common names ("term", "Term", "Description", etc.). padj_column Adjusted p-value column. Auto-detected. gene_count_column Column overlap gene count. Auto-detected. gene_ratio_column Column gene ratio fold enrichment. Auto-detected; computed gene_count_column / background absent. group_column Optional column facet (e.g. cluster condition). Produces one panel per group. top_n Maximum number terms show per group. Default 20. plot_type One "dot" (default), "bar", "lollipop". x_by drives x-axis (bar length). One "gene_ratio" (default), "gene_count", "-log10padj". color_by drives fill/color. One "padj" (default) "gene_count". colors Character vector 2-5 colors gradient (low padj → high padj, .e. significant → ). Default diverging red-blue. max_term_length Maximum characters term label wrapping. Default 50. output_dir Directory save PDF. NULL = save. object_name Label prefix output file names. subset_name Optional subset label.

title: Over-Representation Analysis Visualization — PlotEnrichment
text: ggplot2 patchwork object.

what: Violin / box / bar plots for gene expression and numeric metadata — PlotFeature
title: Violin / box / bar plots for gene expression and numeric metadata — PlotFeature
text: Plots one panel per feature - either gene expression value pulled specified assay layer numeric metadata column - grouped categorical metadata variable x-axis.  Multiple features assembled patchwork.

title: Violin / box / bar plots for gene expression and numeric metadata — PlotFeature
code: PlotFeature(   data,   features,   group.by,   split.by = NULL,   row.by = NULL,   exclude = NULL,   plot_type = c("violin", "box", "both", "bar"),   add_points = FALSE,   point_size = 0.3,   alpha = 0.7,   add_stats = FALSE,   comparisons = NULL,   ref_group = NULL,   hide_ns = FALSE,   stat_alpha = 0.05,   label_format = c("stars", "p.format"),   ncol = NULL,   downsample = NULL,   colors = NULL,   assay = "RNA",   layer = "data",   output_dir = NULL,   object_name = "",   pdf_width = NULL,   pdf_height = NULL )

title: Violin / box / bar plots for gene expression and numeric metadata — PlotFeature
text: data Seurat object plain data frame. features Character vector gene names /numeric metadata column names.  Mixed vectors supported. group.Character.  Categorical metadata column x-axis (groups / conditions). split.Character NULL.  Creates column facets within feature panel - one panel per level split.. row.Character NULL.  Creates row facets within feature panel.  Combined split.produces two-way facet_grid. exclude Named list values exclude plotting. name column name value character vector levels drop.  Can target group., split., row., metadata column. Example: list(Cognitive.Status = "Reference", Sex = "Unknown"). plot_type One "violin" (default), "box", "" (violin thin white box overlay), "bar" (mean ± SE bar chart). add_points Logical.  Overlay jittered individual points. Default FALSE. point_size Numeric.  Size jittered points.  Default 0.3. alpha Numeric.  Fill transparency violins / bars.  Default 0.7. add_stats Logical.  Add Wilcoxon significance brackets via ggsignif.  Default FALSE. comparisons List length-2 character vectors specifying pairs compare.  NULL (default) tests pairwise combinations, vs ref_group set. ref_group Character NULL.  Reference group comparisons (others tested ). hide_ns Logical.  Suppress "ns" brackets.  Default FALSE. stat_alpha Numeric.  Significance threshold used bracket labeling determining star levels (* stat_alpha, ** stat_alpha / 5, *** stat_alpha / 50).  Default 0.05. label_format One "stars" (default: *, **, ***) "p.format" (numeric p-value shown). ncol Integer NULL.  Columns patchwork assembly. NULL = min(n_features, 3). downsample Integer NULL.  supplied, downsample many cells total (balanced across group.levels) plotting.  NULL (default) uses cells. colors Named character vector fill colors group.levels.  NULL auto-resolves PrepObject (Seurat object passed) SelectColors(). assay Character.  Seurat assay pull gene expression . Default "RNA". layer Character.  Assay layer / slot.  Default "data" (log-normalized counts). output_dir Character NULL.  Save directory.  Walks PrepObject Seurat object passed. object_name Character.  Filename prefix.  Walks PrepObject Seurat object passed. pdf_width Numeric NULL.  Override auto-calculated PDF width inches.  NULL (default) sizes automatically number groups, splits, features. pdf_height Numeric NULL.  Override auto-calculated PDF height inches.  NULL (default) sizes automatically number row facets features.

title: Violin / box / bar plots for gene expression and numeric metadata — PlotFeature
text: one feature requested, single ggplot2 object. multiple features requested, patchwork combined plot. plot also saved PDF output_dir available.

title: Violin / box / bar plots for gene expression and numeric metadata — PlotFeature
text: Feature type auto-detected: gene names found assay extracted log-normalized expression; numeric metadata columns used directly. Passing categorical metadata column raises helpful error pointing appropriate scSidekick visualization function instead.

title: Violin / box / bar plots for gene expression and numeric metadata — PlotFeature
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Gene expression violin plot PlotFeature(SeuratObj, features = c("CD3E", "CD79A", "LYZ"),   group.by = "CellType")  # Numeric metadata with split + stats, excluding a reference group PlotFeature(SeuratObj, features = "Age.at.Death",   group.by  = "Dementia.AD",   split.by  = "Sex",   exclude   = list(Cognitive.Status = "Reference"),   add_stats = TRUE,   plot_type = "box")  # Two-way facet: split.by columns × row.by rows PlotFeature(SeuratObj, features = c("CD3E", "LYZ"),   group.by = "Group",   split.by = "Timepoint",   row.by   = "Sex") } # }

what: GSEA Running Score Mountain Plots - Direct or from RunGSEA Output Directory — PlotGSEAEnrichment
title: GSEA Running Score Mountain Plots - Direct or from RunGSEA Output Directory — PlotGSEAEnrichment
text: Two operating modes: Direct mode - pass fgsea_result ranked_stats directly (e.g. ad-hoc fgsea run single CSV). RunGSEA directory mode - point output_dir completed RunGSEA run via gsea_dir.  function auto-discovers CSV files, searches pathway names keyword search (/logic RunSCssGSEA), loads ranked AUC vectors cached DE RDS files, produces grid layout: One PDF per split.level (cell type) - PDF may multiple pages groups max_cols Columns = group.levels (comparison groups, e.g. Male / Female) - max_cols per page; additional groups wrap new pages Rows = matched pathways (one 3-panel mountain stack per pathway)

title: GSEA Running Score Mountain Plots - Direct or from RunGSEA Output Directory — PlotGSEAEnrichment
code: PlotGSEAEnrichment(   fgsea_result = NULL,   ranked_stats = NULL,   gsea_dir = NULL,   database = NULL,   search_terms = NULL,   pathways = NULL,   top_n = 5,   label_levels = NULL,   group_levels = NULL,   split_levels = NULL,   max_cols = 6,   colors = c(pos = "#B40426", neg = "#3B4CC0"),   ncol = 1,   output_dir = NULL,   object_name = "",   subset_name = "" )

title: GSEA Running Score Mountain Plots - Direct or from RunGSEA Output Directory — PlotGSEAEnrichment
text: fgsea_result Direct mode . Data frame fgsea output path single RunGSEA CSV.  Must pathway, NES, leadingEdge, size columns. ranked_stats Direct mode . Named numeric vector (gene → AUC logFC), sorted descending. gsea_dir RunGSEA directory mode. Path output_dir used RunGSEA. database Database subfolder name (e.g. "Hallmark", "C2"). NULL uses first database found. search_terms Keyword search pathways - /logic RunSCssGSEA: character vector applies ; list character vectors applies within element, across elements. Case-insensitive. pathways Exact pathway names plot (alternative search_terms). top_n Direct mode . neither pathways search_terms given, plot top N |NES|.  Default 5. label_levels RunGSEA directory mode. label.levels include. NULL = . group_levels RunGSEA directory mode. group.(cluster) levels include pages. NULL = . split_levels RunGSEA directory mode. split.(cell type) levels include pages. NULL = . max_cols RunGSEA directory mode. Maximum number group.levels (columns) per page. total number groups exceeds value groups chunked across multiple pages within PDF. Default 6. colors Named vector "pos" (positive NES) "neg" (negative NES) color entries. Default red / blue. ncol Direct mode . Number columns output layout. Default 1. output_dir Directory save PDFs. NULL = save. object_name Label prefix output file names. subset_name Optional subset label.

title: GSEA Running Score Mountain Plots - Direct or from RunGSEA Output Directory — PlotGSEAEnrichment
text: Direct mode: single patchwork object. RunGSEA directory mode: invisibly, nested list [[lab]][[cl]][[pw]][[sp]] mountain-plot patchwork objects; PDFs written output_dir (one per lab×cl combination).

what: Faceted bar chart summarizing metadata distributions across patients — PlotMetaSummary
title: Faceted bar chart summarizing metadata distributions across patients — PlotMetaSummary
text: Deduplicates cell-level metadata donor / patient level plots multi-panel bar chart: one column per metadata variable, one row per level optional row-splitting variable (e.g. Sex).  Bars stacked fill_variable segment optionally annotated count.

title: Faceted bar chart summarizing metadata distributions across patients — PlotMetaSummary
code: PlotMetaSummary(   data,   id_column = NULL,   variables,   fill_variable = NULL,   row_variable = NULL,   column_variable = NULL,   exclude = NULL,   percent = FALSE,   count_unit = c("auto", "donors", "cells"),   show_counts = TRUE,   min_count_to_label = 1L,   label_size = 3,   alpha = 0.85,   colors = NULL,   y_label = NULL,   output_dir = NULL,   object_name = "",   file_name = NULL,   return_data = FALSE,   return_flextable = FALSE )

title: Faceted bar chart summarizing metadata distributions across patients — PlotMetaSummary
text: data Seurat object plain data frame. id_column Character.  column uniquely identifies donor patient (e.g. "Donor.ID").  Set NULL count cells directly without deduplication. variables Character vector.  Metadata columns display column facets x-axis (e.g. c("Braak", "APOE.Genotype", "CERAD.score")). fill_variable Character.  Metadata column used color-fill stacked bars (e.g. "Dementia.AD").  NULL, bars shown single neutral color legend. row_variable Character.  Metadata column used create row facets (e.g. "Sex").  NULL = row splitting. column_variable Character NULL.  Metadata column used create column facets within panel.  supplied, one plot produced per entry variables (multi-page PDF), plot faceted variable.  NULL = column faceting (default). exclude Named list values exclude plotting. name column name value character vector levels drop.  Example: list(Cognitive.Status = "Reference"). percent Logical.  TRUE, convert counts percentages plotting.  fill_variable set, bar sums 100\ NULL, bars show percentage panel total.  Segment labels automatically switch "X%" format.  Default FALSE. count_unit One "auto" (default), "donors", "cells".  "auto" counts donors id_column provided one unique donor exists per group; otherwise counts cells. show_counts Logical.  Annotate bar segment count. Default TRUE. min_count_to_label Integer.  Suppress count label segments fewer many observations.  Default 1. label_size Numeric.  Text size count labels (passed geom_text).  Default 3. alpha Numeric.  Fill transparency bars (0-1).  Default 0.85. colors Named character vector colors fill_variable levels.  NULL auto-resolves PrepObject (Seurat object passed) SelectColors(). y_label Character NULL.  Override y-axis label. NULL derives count_unit. output_dir Character NULL.  Directory save PDF. NULL returns plot without saving; Seurat object first walks output_dir stored PrepObject (unless AutoSavePlots = FALSE). object_name Character.  Prefix auto-generated file name. Falls back object_name stored PrepObject. file_name Character NULL.  Base name (extension) saved PDF.  NULL (default) auto-deduces object_name, fill_variable, variables. return_data Logical.  Also return deduplicated donor-level data frame.  Default FALSE. return_flextable Logical.  Also return list formatted flextable objects: $donor_table (wide donor-level data) $crosstab (cross-tabulation per variable, rows separated variable).  Requires flextable package.  Default FALSE.

title: Faceted bar chart summarizing metadata distributions across patients — PlotMetaSummary
text: plot requested (default), ggplot2 object. return_data return_flextable TRUE, named list elements $plot, optionally $data, optionally $flextable.

title: Faceted bar chart summarizing metadata distributions across patients — PlotMetaSummary
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Basic usage PlotMetaSummary(SeuratObj,   id_column     = "Donor.ID",   variables     = c("Braak", "APOE.Genotype", "CERAD.score"),   fill_variable = "Dementia.AD",   row_variable  = "Sex",   exclude       = list(Cognitive.Status = "Reference"))  # Also return the donor table and a cross-tab flextable out <- PlotMetaSummary(SeuratObj,   id_column        = "Donor.ID",   variables        = c("Braak", "CERAD.score"),   fill_variable    = "Dementia.AD",   return_data      = TRUE,   return_flextable = TRUE) out$plot out$data out$flextable$crosstab } # }

what: Plot multiple features on a single embedding — PlotMultiFeature
title: Plot multiple features on a single embedding — PlotMultiFeature
text: Overlays several genes onto one UMAP / tSNE / PCA plot, coloring cells gene express threshold.  Three modes available:

title: Plot multiple features on a single embedding — PlotMultiFeature
code: PlotMultiFeature(   seurat_object,   features,   colors = NULL,   null_color = "gray70",   threshold = 0.0125,   plot_mode = c("gene", "intersection", "scale"),   pct_cutoff = 0.3,   intersection_label = "Co-expressing",   assay = "RNA",   layer = "data",   reduction = "umap",   pt.size = 0.5,   alpha = 0.9,   shape = 16,   title = NULL,   subtitle = NULL,   legend_title = "Gene",   show_legend = TRUE,   output_dir = NULL,   object_name = "",   subset_name = "" )

title: Plot multiple features on a single embedding — PlotMultiFeature
text: seurat_object Seurat object (v3 v5). features Character vector gene names visualize. colors Named unnamed character vector colors (one per gene). Auto-filled Nour_pal("") missing entries. NULL, full Nour_pal("") palette used. null_color Color cells express gene threshold. Default "gray70". threshold Numeric scalar, named numeric vector (names = gene names), giving minimum expression value cell considered positive.  Default 0.0125. plot_mode One "gene" (default), "intersection", "scale". pct_cutoff plot_mode = "intersection": fraction gene list cell must express count co-expressing.  Default 0.3 (.e. >= 30% genes). intersection_label Label shown legend co-expressing cells. Default "Co-expressing". assay Assay pull expression .  Default "RNA". layer Layer / slot pull (Seurat v5 layer name v3 slot name). Default "data". reduction Dimensional reduction use coordinates.  Default "umap". pt.size Point size.  Default 0.5. alpha Point transparency [0, 1].  Default 0.9. shape ggplot2 point shape integer.  Default 16 (filled circle). title Plot title.  Default NULL. subtitle Plot subtitle.  Default NULL. legend_title Legend title.  Default "Gene". show_legend Logical; show color legend?  Default TRUE. output_dir Directory save PDF.  NULL (default) plot printed active graphics device. object_name Optional prefix used building output file name. subset_name Optional second prefix output file name.

title: Plot multiple features on a single embedding — PlotMultiFeature
text: ggplot object (invisibly output_dir set).

title: Plot multiple features on a single embedding — PlotMultiFeature
text: "gene" (default) cell colored last gene features expresses threshold (later genes overwrite earlier ones multi-expressing cells).  Cells thresholds drawn null_color. "intersection" Cells express least pct_cutoff × n_genes genes highlighted co-expressing. others null_color. "scale" Continuous color gradient showing expression level single gene (features[1] ).

what: Pathway state hierarchy butterfly plot — PlotPathwayButterfly
title: Pathway state hierarchy butterfly plot — PlotPathwayButterfly
text: Scores cells four pathways, maps 2-D hierarchy coordinate system, produces butterfly layout: one large overview panel (cells colored group.) plus one small panel per group level (group highlighted color; remaining cells gray).  split.supplied, separate figure produced split level.

title: Pathway state hierarchy butterfly plot — PlotPathwayButterfly
code: PlotPathwayButterfly(   seurat_object,   group.by,   quadrant_order,   split.by = NULL,   gene_sets = NULL,   pathway_sets = list(Hallmark = list(category = "H")),   species = "Homo sapiens",   quadrant_labels = NULL,   group_colors = NULL,   assay = "RNA",   layer = "counts",   subsample = FALSE,   n_cells = 20000L,   subsample_warn_at = 50000L,   center = TRUE,   log_scale = TRUE,   overview_title = NULL,   overview_size = 2,   panel_ncol = NULL,   point_size = 0.3,   point_alpha = 0.6,   label_size_large = 5,   label_size_small = 2.5,   title_size_large = 12,   title_size_small = 8,   width = NULL,   height = NULL,   file_name = NULL,   output_dir = NULL,   caffeinate = FALSE )

title: Pathway state hierarchy butterfly plot — PlotPathwayButterfly
text: seurat_object Seurat object. group.Character. Metadata column cell grouping coloring. quadrant_order Character vector exactly four pathway names, order: bottom-left, bottom-right, top-left, top-right.  Names must match either names(gene_sets) (custom mode) pathway names requested MSigDB collection. split.Character NULL. Metadata column whose levels generate separate butterfly figure.  Default: NULL. gene_sets Named list character vectors — one element per pathway. NULL (default), gene sets fetched MSigDB via msigdbr using pathway_sets filtered quadrant_order. pathway_sets Named list specifying MSigDB collection(s) search. element list category optional subcategory.  used gene_sets = NULL. Default: list(Hallmark = list(category = "H")). species Species string msigdbr. Default: "Homo sapiens". quadrant_labels Character vector four display labels corner boxes (order matches quadrant_order: bl, br, tl, tr). Defaults auto-shortened pathway names. group_colors Named character vector mapping group levels colors. Defaults PrepObject colors auto-generated palette. assay Assay extract counts .  Default: "RNA". layer Layer / slot name.  Default: "counts". subsample Logical.  TRUE, randomly sample n_cells cells scoring.  Default: FALSE. n_cells Integer.  Number cells sample subsample = TRUE.  Default: 20000L. subsample_warn_at Integer NULL.  cell count exceeds value subsample = FALSE, message emitted. Set NULL silence.  Default: 50000L. center Logical.  Column-center scores hierarchy transform. Default: TRUE. log_scale Logical.  Apply log2 scaling hierarchy coordinates. Default: TRUE. overview_title Character.  Title overview panel.  Defaults object name stored PrepObject. overview_size Numeric.  Width overview panel relative one small panel column.  Default: 2. panel_ncol Integer NULL.  Number columns small- panel grid.  Defaults ceiling(n_groups / 2). point_size Numeric.  Point size.  Default: 0.3. point_alpha Numeric.  Point alpha.  Default: 0.6. label_size_large Numeric.  Corner label size overview panel. Default: 5. label_size_small Numeric.  Corner label size small panels. Default: 2.5. title_size_large Numeric.  Panel title font size overview. Default: 12. title_size_small Numeric.  Panel title font size small panels. Default: 8. width Numeric NULL.  PDF width inches.  NULL (default), width auto-sized (overview_size + panel_ncol) * 1.5. height Numeric NULL.  PDF height inches.  NULL (default), height auto-sized panel_nrow * 2.8 + 0.4. file_name Character NULL.  Output file name (without directory).  Defaults "<object_name> pathway butterfly.pdf". output_dir Character NULL.  Directory saving PDF. Falls back PrepObject output directory.  NULL, plot printed active device. caffeinate Logical.  Keep machine awake computation. Default: FALSE.

title: Pathway state hierarchy butterfly plot — PlotPathwayButterfly
text: Invisibly, named list (one element per split level) containing hierarchy (X/Y coordinate data frame) scores (raw pathway score matrix, cells × pathways).

title: Pathway state hierarchy butterfly plot — PlotPathwayButterfly
text: quadrant_order specifies four pathway names order bottom-left, bottom-right, top-left, top-right.  Y-axis separates top bottom; X-axis gives left-right balance within whichever axis dominates cell.

title: Pathway state hierarchy butterfly plot — PlotPathwayButterfly
text: Pathway scores computed mean expression across pathway's genes per cell (adapted scrabble R package).  Scores column-centered center = TRUE (default) mean score pathway across cells zero hierarchy transform.

what: UMAP with Pie Charts at Cluster Centroids — PlotPieUMAP
title: UMAP with Pie Charts at Cluster Centroids — PlotPieUMAP

title: UMAP with Pie Charts at Cluster Centroids — PlotPieUMAP
code: PlotPieUMAP(   seurat_object,   group.by,   split.by,   reduction = "umap",   pie_scale = 1,   bg_color = "lightgray",   bg_alpha = 0.3,   pt.size = 0.4,   split_colors = NULL,   label = TRUE,   label_size = 3,   output_dir = NULL,   object_name = "",   subset_name = "" )

title: UMAP with Pie Charts at Cluster Centroids — PlotPieUMAP
text: seurat_object Seurat object. group.Metadata column defining groups/clusters. One pie chart placed group's centroid reduction space. split.Metadata column whose level proportions define pie slices (e.g. "Sample" shows much cluster sample). reduction Dimensionality reduction use. Default "umap". pie_scale Scaling multiplier pie radius (1 = auto-sized). Increase larger pies, decrease pies overlap. Default 1. bg_color Background scatter point color. Default "lightgray". bg_alpha Background scatter point transparency. Default 0.3. pt.size Background scatter point size. Default 0.4. split_colors Named character vector colors split.levels. Auto-assigned PrepObject Nour_pal NULL. label Logical. Add cluster labels centroids. Default TRUE. label_size Font size cluster labels. Default 3. output_dir Directory save PDF. NULL = save. object_name Label prefix output file names. subset_name Optional subset label.

title: UMAP with Pie Charts at Cluster Centroids — PlotPieUMAP

Auto-palette selection: "auto": Nour_pal("") variables \(\leq 8\) levels (warm, distinct), Nour_pal("spectrum") \(> 8\) levels (broader color range). title: Prepare a Seurat Object for Uniform Downstream Analysis — PrepObject
text: seurat_object Seurat object. variables Character vector metadata column names color prepare.  Can single name; re-run PrepObject time add . palettes Palette name(s).  One : single string ("auto", "", "spectrum", ...) applied every variable. named character vector keyed variable (c(Cluster = "", Sample = "spectrum")); names listed fall back "auto". unnamed character vector length variables, matched position (c("spectrum", "", "")). Default "auto". reverse Logical, named logical vector, unnamed logical vector matched position variables.  TRUE reverses palette variables; named vector reverses selectively (e.g. c(Cluster = TRUE, Sample = FALSE)).  Default FALSE. custom_colors Named list named character vectors override automatic palette generation entirely specific variables. E.g. list(Sample = c(Control = "#3B4CC0", NRF1_mRNA = "#B40426")). var_levels Named list character vectors set factor level ordering variable, controlling sort order heatmap columns, violin plots, ggplot2 facets. E.g. list(Sample = c("Control", "NRF1_mRNA")). group.Character.  Store preferred primary grouping column. Downstream scSidekick functions use default group.none specified call.  Pass NA explicitly clear previously stored value. split.Character.  Store preferred secondary split column. Downstream functions use default split..  Pass NA explicitly disable stored default (even one exists). output_dir Character.  Store preferred output directory saving PDFs plots.  downstream scSidekick functions walk value output_dir argument NULL. Pass NA explicitly clear stored value. object_name Character.  Label used prefix output file names. subset_name Character.  Optional subset label appended output file names (e.g. "NoNeurons"). AutoSavePlots Logical NULL.  Controls whether plotting functions automatically save PDFs stored output_dir output_dir passed explicitly function call. TRUE (default set): plotting functions walk use stored output_dir automatically — existing behavior. FALSE: walk-suppressed; functions return plot object without saving unless caller explicitly passes output_dir = "..." plotting call. Pass NULL leave current setting unchanged. force Logical.  FALSE (default), variables already stored colors left unchanged.  TRUE, existing colors regenerated every variable listed variables.

Sender receiver tile plots: faceted top pathway, showing much cell type sends/receives per condition. top changed links heatmap: top_links sender→receiver pairs highest variance across conditions, showing exactly cell-type interactions drive pathway-level changes. CSV underlying metrics (flow, sender, receiver, link tables).

Number top sender→receiver links (ranked variance flow across conditions) show links heatmap. Default 15. rank_by Character. title: Rename a cell type label inside a CellChat object without re-running analysis — RenameCellTypeInCC
text: communication probability matrices recomputed - labels change. safe underlying values invariant cell type called. Typical workflow renaming:
code: # 1. Patch and re-save all RDS files rds_files <- list.files("CellChatObjects/", "CellChat\\.rds$", full.names = TRUE) for (f in rds_files) {   cc <- RenameCellTypeInCC(readRDS(f), "Immune_contam", "NK_TCells")   saveRDS(cc, f) }  # 2. Re-run comparison figures (fast - pure plotting) cclist <- setNames(lapply(rds_files, readRDS),                    sub("CellChat\\.rds$", "", basename(rds_files))) CompareCellChat(cclist, output_dir = ...) RankCellChatPathways(cclist, output_dir = ...)  # 3. Per-group RunCellChat PDFs: delete old PDFs and re-run RunCellChat with #    resume = TRUE - the pipeline checkpoint means only plots are regenerated.

what: Run consensus NMF (cNMF) to discover gene expression programs — RunCNMF
title: Run consensus NMF (cNMF) to discover gene expression programs — RunCNMF
text: Wraps dylkot/cNMF pipeline (Kotliar et al. 2019) reticulate: prepare (high-variance gene selection + normalization), factorize (heavy NMF replication step), combine, k_selection_plot diagnostic. cNMF inherently two-stage, human---loop method: run function, inspect k-selection plot writes, choose number programs k, call GetCNMFPrograms extract consensus result K. cNMF Python package, R dependency. Install (pip install cnmf) point function environment conda_env python.

title: Run consensus NMF (cNMF) to discover gene expression programs — RunCNMF
code: RunCNMF(   seurat_object,   assay = NULL,   name = "cnmf",   output_dir = NULL,   k_range = 5:15,   n_iter = 100L,   num_highvar_genes = 2000L,   genes_use = NULL,   batch.by = NULL,   seed = 14L,   n_workers = 1L,   conda_env = NULL,   python = NULL,   resume = FALSE,   caffeinate = FALSE,   show_plot = TRUE )

title: Run consensus NMF (cNMF) to discover gene expression programs — RunCNMF
text: seurat_object Seurat object containing raw counts. assay Character NULL. Assay pull counts . NULL uses default assay. name Character. Run name; cNMF writes output output_dir/name/. Default "cnmf". output_dir Character NULL. Output directory. Walks output_dir stored PrepObject NULL. k_range Integer vector. Candidate numbers programs (K) factorize across. Default 5:15. n_iter Integer. NMF replicates per K. Default 100L. Higher robust much slower. num_highvar_genes Integer. Number overdispersed genes cNMF selects factorization. Default 2000L. Ignored genes_use supplied. genes_use Character vector NULL. Optional precomputed gene set restrict input (bypasses cNMF's high-variance selection). Ignored batch.set. batch.Character vector NULL. One meta.data columns correct batch effects factorization. supplied, cNMF's Preprocess Harmony-corrects counts variables, learns program usages corrected data, re-fits spectra uncorrected TP10K data (Kotliar batch-correction workflow). Requires Python package harmonypy environment. NULL (default) runs without batch correction. seed Integer. Random seed reproducibility. Default 14L. n_workers Integer. Number cNMF worker processes run factorize step parallel. Default 1L (single, -process). > 1, env's cnmf command launched per worker (K x iteration) jobs split across ; set number cores can spare. worker writes <name>.factorize.worker_<>.log output_dir. conda_env Character NULL. Name conda environment cnmf installed. python Character NULL. Path Python interpreter cnmf installed (alternative conda_env). resume Logical. TRUE, reuse work already disk: completed factorization (skip prepare/factorize/combine), batch-correction path existing Harmony-corrected files (skip expensive correction step, e.g. recover run crashed Harmony). Default FALSE. caffeinate Logical. Prevent machine sleeping run (macOS). Default FALSE. show_plot Logical. Display k-selection plot active graphics device (e.g. RStudio Plots pane) finished. Default TRUE. Requires png (preferred) magick package.

title: Run consensus NMF (cNMF) to discover gene expression programs — RunCNMF
text: input Seurat object run metadata stored seurat_object@misc$cnmf. k-selection plot written output_dir/name/ .legend sidecar, analysis_params.json (parameters plus draft methods paragraph) written output_dir create_analysis_pptx.

title: Run consensus NMF (cNMF) to discover gene expression programs — RunCNMF
text: Parallelism. Factorization runtime scales n_iter * length(k_range) (e.g. defaults 100 x 11 = 1100 NMF fits), object size. Set n_workers > 1 split fits across parallel cNMF worker processes multi-core machine. cluster-scale runs, cNMF's command-line workflow can distribute workers across jobs; see cNMF documentation.

title: Run consensus NMF (cNMF) to discover gene expression programs — RunCNMF
text: Kotliar D, et al. (2019) Identifying gene expression programs cell-type identity cellular activity single-cell RNA-Seq. eLife 8:e43803.

what: Run the full CellChat analysis pipeline per group — RunCellChat
title: Run the full CellChat analysis pipeline per group — RunCellChat
text: level group.col Seurat object, subsets data, builds CellChat object, runs standard pipeline (overexpressed genes → interactions → communication probability → pathway probability → aggregate network → communication patterns), writes visualizations + CellChat RDS file output_dir.

title: Run the full CellChat analysis pipeline per group — RunCellChat
code: RunCellChat(   seurat_object = NULL,   cell.by,   group.by,   groups = NULL,   identity_column = NULL,   group.col = NULL,   species = "human",   colors = NULL,   output_dir,   robj_dir = NULL,   min.cells = 0L,   thresh.p = 0.05,   run.patterns = TRUE,   pattern_k_range = 3:10,   vertex.receiver = seq(1, 4),   save.rds = TRUE,   resume = FALSE,   redo_plots = FALSE,   assay_type = NULL,   caffeinate = FALSE )

title: Run the full CellChat analysis pipeline per group — RunCellChat
text: seurat_object Seurat object. Ignored redo_plots = TRUE. cell.Character. Metadata column cell identities (y-axis chord/circle diagrams, e.g., "subAssignment"). identity_column accepted deprecated alias. group.Character. Metadata column whose levels get CellChat run (e.g., "Group"). group.col accepted deprecated alias. groups Character vector NULL. Specific levels group.process. NULL uses levels. species Character. Determines CellChatDB ligand-receptor database PPI network loaded. One : "human" (default) - uses CellChatDB.human PPI.human "mouse" - uses CellChatDB.mouse PPI.mouse colors Named character vector mapping cell.levels colors. NULL uses CellChat's default palette. Tip: use color vector Seurat DimPlots figures consistent. output_dir Character. Root directory output. Per-group sub-folders created automatically. robj_dir Character NULL. Directory RDS checkpoint files. Defaults file.path(output_dir, "CellChatObjects"). min.cells Integer. Minimum number cells cell type must given condition interactions retained CellChat::filterCommunication(). Increase reduce noise rare populations. Default 0 (keep ). thresh.p Numeric. P-value threshold used CellChat::identifyOverExpressedGenes() CellChat::computeCommunProbPathway(). Lowering (e.g. 0.01) makes analysis conservative; raising (e.g. 0.1) recovers interactions. Default 0.05. Note: pipeline significance threshold. thresh parameter CompareCellChat() independently controls L-R interactions shown comparison plots. run.patterns Logical. Run communication pattern analysis outgoing incoming signals across k values pattern_k_range? Adds substantially run time. Default TRUE. pattern_k_range Integer vector. Range latent pattern numbers (k) tested CellChat::identifyCommunicationPatterns(). k×direction combination produces one PDF. Default 3:10. Reduce (e.g. 3:6) run time concern. vertex.receiver Integer vector. Indices receiver cell types hierarchy layout per-pathway plots. Default seq(1, 4) (first four cell types). Adjust match biological receiver populations. save.rds Logical. Save CellChat object RDS file? Default TRUE. resume Logical. Skip groups already completed recover groups interrupted mid-run. Default FALSE. Requires save.rds = TRUE output_dir set. Two checkpoints used: Pipeline checkpoint ({grp}CellChat_ckpt.rds): written immediately netAnalysis_computeCentrality (plotting). run interrupted plotting pattern analysis, file lets next run skip expensive pipeline steps jump straight visualizations. Final RDS ({grp}CellChat.rds): written end successful group run replaces checkpoint. file exists, group considered fully complete skipped. redo_plots Logical. Re-generate per-group visualization PDFs existing RDS files without re-running communication probability pipeline. Default FALSE. TRUE: group, {grp}CellChat.rds loaded robj_dir. plotting code (circle, pathway, bubble, scatter, patterns) re-executed using loaded object. pipeline (computeCommunProb, aggregateNet, etc.) completely skipped - seurat_object argument ignored. Use RenameCellTypeInCC() + saveRDS() regenerate figures corrected cell-type labels. assay_type Character NULL. Seurat assay use ligand-receptor expression. NULL (default) auto-detects: uses "RNA" Seurat v4 objects "RNA" / "Spatial" v5 objects depending available assays. caffeinate Logical. TRUE, prevents Mac sleeping run using caffeinate system command. Default FALSE.

what: Run GSEA pathway analysis on a Seurat object — RunGSEA
title: Run GSEA pathway analysis on a Seurat object — RunGSEA
text: Performs following steps combination label.(outer loop) split.(inner loop) levels: Subset object current level. Run Wilcoxon rank-sum DE presto::wilcoxauc(). pathway database pathway_sets, run fgsea::fgsea() using AUC scores ranking statistics. Generate lollipop NES plot (top/bottom 10 pathways per cluster). Build NES heatmaps showing top top_n pathways per cluster.

title: Run GSEA pathway analysis on a Seurat object — RunGSEA
code: RunGSEA(   seurat_object,   group.by = "Assignment",   split.by = "GlobalAssignment",   label.by = NULL,   gene_sets = NULL,   deg_df = NULL,   deg_gene_column = "feature",   deg_group_column = "group",   deg_fc_column = "logFC",   deg_padj_column = "padj",   deg_padj_cutoff = 0.05,   deg_top_n = 20L,   pathway_sets = list(Hallmark = list(category = "H"), KEGG = list(category = "C2",     subcategory = "CP:KEGG"), Reactome = list(category = "C2", subcategory =     "CP:REACTOME"), WP = list(category = "C2", subcategory = "CP:WIKIPATHWAYS")),   species = "Homo sapiens",   assay = "RNA",   min_cells = 20L,   padj_thresh = 0.05,   logfc_thresh = 0.1,   top_n = 5L,   nes.cutoff = 1,   output_dir = NULL,   resume = FALSE,   heatmap_params = list(row_names_side = "left", show_row_dend = FALSE,     row_names_max_width = grid::unit(15, "cm")),   heatmap_colors = NULL,   caffeinate = FALSE )

title: Run GSEA pathway analysis on a Seurat object — RunGSEA
text: seurat_object Seurat object. group.Character. Metadata column whose levels compared within subset (used DE groups). Default "Assignment". split.Character NULL. Metadata column whose levels get independent GSEA (e.g., run GSEA separately per broad cell type "GlobalAssignment"). NULL runs single GSEA whole object (label.subset), comparing group.levels without cell-type subsetting. Default "GlobalAssignment". label.Character NULL. Outer metadata column subsetting (e.g., condition). NULL treats whole object one group. gene_sets Named list character vectors, name gene set label vector contains gene symbols. supplied, MSigDB queried pathway_sets ignored. Takes priority deg_df. Default NULL. deg_df Data frame differential expression results. supplied (gene_sets NULL), gene sets built taking top deg_top_n - -regulated genes per group table. Columns identified deg_gene_column, deg_group_column, deg_fc_column, deg_padj_column. Default NULL. deg_gene_column Character. Column deg_df containing gene symbols. Default "feature". deg_group_column Character. Column deg_df containing group label used split genes per-group sets. Default "group". deg_fc_column Character. Column deg_df containing log-fold change values used rank genes within group. Default "logFC". deg_padj_column Character. Column deg_df containing adjusted p-values used filter genes ranking. Default "padj". deg_padj_cutoff Numeric. Adjusted p-value threshold applied deg_df rows building gene sets. Default 0.05. deg_top_n Integer. Maximum number genes per group taken deg_df filtering ranking. Default 20. pathway_sets Named list MSigDB gene-set databases test. element list category field optional subcategory field; element name becomes short label used output filenames return list. default covers four curated collections:   useful collections (pass additional list elements): list(category = "C2", subcategory = "CP:BIOCARTA") - BioCarta list(category = "C2", subcategory = "CP:PID") - NCI Pathway Interaction Database list(category = "C5", subcategory = "GO:BP") - Gene Ontology Biological Process list(category = "C5", subcategory = "GO:MF") - GO Molecular Function list(category = "C5", subcategory = "GO:CC") - GO Cellular Component list(category = "C7", subcategory = "IMMUNESIGDB") - ImmuneSigDB (immune gene sets; recommended immune datasets) list(category = "C8") - Cell-type signature gene sets See msigdbr::msigdbr_collections() full catalogue. species Character. Species passed msigdbr::msigdbr(). Must match species names recognized function. Common values: "Homo sapiens" - human (default) "Mus musculus" - mouse "Rattus norvegicus" - rat "Danio rerio" - zebrafish Run msigdbr::msigdbr_species() complete list. assay Character. Seurat assay DE via presto::wilcoxauc(). Default "RNA". BPCells sketch workflows use "sketch". min_cells Integer. Skip subsets fewer cells. Default 20. separate guard also skips (label, split) combination fewer 2 levels group.subsetting - prevents presto::wilcoxauc crashing e.g. one label.level contains one group.category (e.g., Cognitive.Status group single Sex). message emitted skipped combination. padj_thresh Numeric. Adjusted p-value threshold DE fgsea filtering. Default 0.05. logfc_thresh Numeric. Minimum log-fold-change DE filtering. Default 0.1. top_n Integer. Number top bottom pathways per cluster shown summary heatmaps. Default 5. nes.cutoff Numeric. selecting union top/bottom top_n pathways per cluster, rows whose maximum |NES| across clusters falls threshold removed summary heatmap. Set 0 skip filter. Default 1.0. output_dir Character NULL. provided, CSVs PDFs written structured subdirectory tree. NULL, results returned R list. resume Logical. TRUE output_dir set, skip work already completed: DE cache: Wilcoxon DE results (label, split) pair saved output_dir/label/de_cache_<split>_<label>.rds reloaded subsequent runs instead re-running presto::wilcoxauc. DB-level skip: full NES heatmap PDF (label, split, db_name) triple already exists, database skipped entirely (slot return list set NULL). Default FALSE. heatmap_params Named list additional arguments forwarded ComplexHeatmap::Heatmap() full summary NES heatmaps. default set scSidekick can overridden - e.g. clustering distance, font size, row ordering. heatmap_colors circlize::colorRamp2 color function heatmap fill scale. NULL (default) uses 5-stop blue-white-red diverging palette limits auto-scaled data range (capped +/-4): Supply colorRamp2 object override. breaks set custom function used -(auto-scaling). Examples: Classic RdBu (3-stop): circlize::colorRamp2(c(-3, 0, 3), c("#2166ac", "white", "#b2182b")) Viridis plasma (sequential, one-sided scores): circlize::colorRamp2(seq(0, 3, length.= 9), viridis::plasma(9)) Purple-green (PRGn, cooler look): circlize::colorRamp2(c(-3, 0, 3), RColorBrewer::brewer.pal(3, "PRGn")) palette, tighter breaks (good subtle signals): circlize::colorRamp2(c(-1.5, -0.75, 0, 0.75, 1.5), c("#007dd1", "#b3d9f5", "white", "#f5c08a", "#ab3000")) Note: NES heatmaps, diverging palettes (centered 0) semantically correct 0 means "enrichment". caffeinate Logical. TRUE, prevents Mac sleeping run via caffeinate. Useful long multi-database runs overnight. Default FALSE.
code: list(   Hallmark = list(category = "H"),   KEGG     = list(category = "C2", subcategory = "CP:KEGG"),   Reactome = list(category = "C2", subcategory = "CP:REACTOME"),   WP       = list(category = "C2", subcategory = "CP:WIKIPATHWAYS") ) circlize::colorRamp2(     c(-lim, -lim/2, 0, lim/2, lim),     c("#007dd1", "#b3d9f5", "white", "#f5c08a", "#ab3000")   )

what: Pseudobulk GSEA with limma contrasts and per-patient ssGSEA — RunGSEA_pseudobulk
title: Pseudobulk GSEA with limma contrasts and per-patient ssGSEA — RunGSEA_pseudobulk
text: statistically rigorous alternative RunGSEA(). Instead treating individual cells replicates, function aggregates counts donor/sample level (pseudobulk), fits limma-voom linear model, ranks genes moderated t-statistic running fgsea. respects true unit replication avoids inflated degrees freedom arise cell-level comparisons. Optionally also computes per-patient pathway activity scores (ssGSEA via GSVA) tests across groups, producing boxplots heatmaps alongside GSEA results.

title: Pseudobulk GSEA with limma contrasts and per-patient ssGSEA — RunGSEA_pseudobulk
code: RunGSEA_pseudobulk(   seurat_object = NULL,   group.by = NULL,   sample.by = NULL,   contrast.by = NULL,   split.by = NULL,   subset.by = NULL,   run_label = NULL,   label.by = NULL,   pseudobulk = NULL,   covariates = NULL,   contrasts = NULL,   gene_sets = NULL,   deg_df = NULL,   deg_gene_column = "feature",   deg_group_column = "group",   deg_fc_column = "logFC",   deg_padj_column = "padj",   deg_padj_cutoff = 0.05,   deg_top_n = 20L,   identity_column = NULL,   sample_column = NULL,   group_column = NULL,   pathway_sets = list(Hallmark = list(category = "H"), KEGG = list(category = "C2",     subcategory = "CP:KEGG"), Reactome = list(category = "C2", subcategory =     "CP:REACTOME"), WP = list(category = "C2", subcategory = "CP:WIKIPATHWAYS")),   species = "human",   output_dir,   assay = NULL,   min.cells = 10L,   min.samples.per.group = 3L,   run.ssgsea = TRUE,   ssgsea.use.padj = TRUE,   top_n_heatmap = 30L,   top_n_ssgsea = 30L,   nes.cutoff = 1,   group_colors = NULL,   heatmap_params = list(row_names_side = "left", show_row_dend = FALSE,     row_names_max_width = grid::unit(15, "cm")),   heatmap_colors = NULL,   save.rds = TRUE,   redo_plots = FALSE,   caffeinate = FALSE )

title: Pseudobulk GSEA with limma contrasts and per-patient ssGSEA — RunGSEA_pseudobulk
text: seurat_object Seurat object. Either seurat_object precomputed pseudobulk list must supplied. group.Character NULL. Metadata column holding cell-type labels. One independent GSEA run per level (e.g. "Assignment"). Pass NULL object already contains one cell type (e.g. Microglia-object); cells treated single group called "" single GSEA run. sample.Character. Metadata column identifying biological replicate (donor/patient ID). statistical unit; unique value becomes one pseudobulk sample limma-voom model (e.g. "DonorID"). contrast.Character. Metadata column holding comparison group label (e.g. "Disease" levels "AD" "Control"). Cells NA column excluded aggregation. split.Character NULL. Optional metadata column used create interaction groups fitting model. supplied, contrast groups become <contrast.>.<split.> combinations (e.g. split.= "Sex" turns "AD" "Control" "AD.Female", "AD.Male", "Control.Female", "Control.Male"), allowing sex-stratified condition-stratified contrasts. Default NULL. subset.Character NULL. Optional metadata column used run full pipeline separately level. level gets subdirectory output_dir. Use produce independent analyses per broad cell class, brain region, grouping variable without calling function multiple times. Default NULL. run_label Character NULL. Short label appended output filenames plot titles distinguish run others saved output_dir. Set automatically subset.used; otherwise leave NULL unless need custom tag. pseudobulk Optional precomputed pseudobulk. named list elements $counts (genes x samples matrix) $samples (sample-level metadata data frame). Supply value returned previous RunGSEA_pseudobulk() call skip aggregation step reruns. Default NULL (aggregation performed seurat_object). covariates Character vector sample-level metadata columns include covariates limma-voom model (e.g. c("Age", "PMI")). Numeric covariates median-imputed NA; factor/character covariates receive "unknown" level. Default NULL. contrasts Named list limma contrast expressions. Every element must single string built model coefficient names form make.names(paste0("group", level)). small helper makes concise: Positive NES = enrichment first term. NULL standard set generated automatically levels contrast.. redo_plots = TRUE, user-supplied list takes priority list saved RDS. Default NULL. gene_sets Named list character vectors. element one gene set (e.g. list(MySet = c("GENE1", "GENE2", ...))). supplied, pathway_sets deg_df ignored msigdbr required. Default NULL. deg_df Data frame differential expression results. Top DEGs per group extracted used gene sets. supplied, pathway_sets ignored msigdbr required. Use deg_* parameters map data frame's column names. Default NULL. deg_gene_column Character. Column deg_df containing gene names. Default "feature". deg_group_column Character (length 1 2). Column(s) deg_df defining group row belongs . Two columns create two-level label used heatmap row splitting. Default "group". deg_fc_column Character. Column deg_df containing fold change used rank genes within group. Default "logFC". deg_padj_column Character NULL. Column deg_df containing adjusted p-values pre-filtering. Set NULL skip filtering. Default "padj". deg_padj_cutoff Numeric. Adjusted p-value threshold applied deg_df selecting top genes. Default 0.05. deg_top_n Integer. Number top genes per group (ranked deg_fc_column, descending) include gene set. Default 20. pathway_sets Named list MSigDB collections test. element must category field optional subcategory field (see msigdbr::msigdbr_collections()). element name becomes label used output filenames. Default: Hallmark, KEGG, Reactome, WikiPathways. Ignored gene_sets deg_df supplied. species Character. Species name passed msigdbr::msigdbr(). Use "human" "mouse" (shorthand accepted) full name returned msigdbr::msigdbr_species(). Default "human". output_dir Character. Root directory CSV, PDF, RDS output. Per-contrast per-database subdirectories created automatically. assay Character NULL. Seurat assay containing raw counts aggregate. NULL uses default assay. BPCells sketch workflows, always pass "RNA" explicitly since pseudobulk aggregation requires raw counts cells. Default NULL. min.cells Integer. Minimum number cells sample must contribute given cell type included pseudobulk cell type. Samples threshold excluded avoid noisy pseudo-replicates. Default 10. min.samples.per.group Integer. Minimum number samples required per group level within cell type. Cell types group fewer many samples skipped entirely. Default 3. run.ssgsea Logical. Also compute per-patient pathway activity via single-sample GSEA (ssGSEA, using GSVA::gsva())? Produces score heatmaps contrast boxplots per cell type per database. Requires GSVA Bioconductor package. Default TRUE. ssgsea.use.padj Logical. TRUE (default), pathway significance ssGSEA judged BH-adjusted p-value (padj < 0.05). Set FALSE use raw p-value instead – useful many contrasts make BH correction aggressive real signals drop . Affects pathway selection heatmaps, boxplot panel selection, significance bracket thresholds. Default TRUE. top_n_heatmap Integer. Number pathways shown NES heatmap per contrast/database combination, selected maximum |NES| across cell types. Default 30. top_n_ssgsea Integer. Number pathways shown ssGSEA score heatmap per cell type, selected significance variance. Default 30. nes.cutoff Numeric. Minimum absolute NES required pathway appear summary NES heatmap (pathways x contrasts). candidate row set first built union top top_n_heatmap pathways individual per-contrast heatmap; nes.cutoff removes rows whose maximum |NES| across contrasts falls threshold. Default 1.0. Set 0 skip cutoff show union rows. group_colors Named character vector mapping contrast.levels colors. Used consistently across boxplots heatmap annotations. NULL auto-generates colors Nour palette. Default NULL. heatmap_params Named list additional arguments forwarded ComplexHeatmap::Heatmap(). default set internally can overridden (e.g. list(clustering_distance_rows = "pearson")). heatmap_colors circlize::colorRamp2 color function NES ssGSEA score heatmaps. NULL (default) uses 5-stop blue-white-red diverging palette centered 0. Breaks fixed c(-2, -1, 0, 1, 2) ssGSEA row-z-score heatmaps auto-scaled data NES heatmaps. Supply colorRamp2 object override heatmaps . Examples: Classic RdBu: circlize::colorRamp2(c(-3, 0, 3), c("#2166ac", "white", "#b2182b")) Purple-green (PRGn): circlize::colorRamp2(c(-3, 0, 3), RColorBrewer::brewer.pal(3, "PRGn")) Viridis plasma (one-sided scores): circlize::colorRamp2(seq(0, 3, length.= 9), viridis::plasma(9)) Tighter scale subtle signals: circlize::colorRamp2(c(-1.5, -0.75, 0, 0.75, 1.5), c("#007dd1", "#b3d9f5", "white", "#f5c08a", "#ab3000")) save.rds Logical. Save pseudobulk counts, sample metadata, GSEA results, ssGSEA results RunGSEA_pseudobulk_results.rds output_dir. Required redo_plots = TRUE. Default TRUE. redo_plots Logical. Reload previously saved RDS regenerate plots without rerunning full pipeline. Default FALSE. TRUE: RunGSEA_pseudobulk_results.rds loaded output_dir. NES heatmaps lollipop plots regenerated saved GSEA data frame. ssGSEA score CSVs disk reread used regenerate score heatmaps boxplots. seurat_object, group., sample., contrast.needed can omitted. Useful changing group_colors, heatmap_params, plot parameter without rerunning hours-long pipeline. caffeinate Logical. Prevent system sleeping run (macOS , uses built-caffeinate utility). Recommended overnight runs. Default FALSE.
code: cf <- function(l) make.names(paste0("group", l))   contrasts <- list(     AD_vs_Ctrl   = paste(cf("AD"),     "-", cf("Control")),     Interaction  = paste0("(", cf("AD.F"), "-", cf("Ctrl.F"), ")",                           " - ",                           "(", cf("AD.M"), "-", cf("Ctrl.M"), ")")   )

title: Pseudobulk GSEA with limma contrasts and per-patient ssGSEA — RunGSEA_pseudobulk
text: Invisibly returns named list: pseudobulk Genes x samples aggregated count matrix. samples Sample-level metadata data frame used model. gsea Long-format data frame fgsea results columns pathway, NES, padj, cell_type, contrast, db. ssgsea ssGSEA contrast results (one row per pathway/cell type/ contrast), NULL run.ssgsea = FALSE. contrasts named contrast list actually used model (useful contrasts auto-generated).

title: Pseudobulk GSEA with limma contrasts and per-patient ssGSEA — RunGSEA_pseudobulk
text: Three metadata columns drive analysis: group.Cell type column. GSEA run independently level (e.g. "Assignment"). Cells aggregated within level. sample.Donor/sample ID column. statistical unit. unique value becomes one pseudobulk replicate limma model (e.g. "DonorID", "SampleID"). contrast.Comparison group column. Levels define groups contrasted (e.g. "Disease" levels "AD" "Control"). Cells NA column dropped aggregation.

title: Pseudobulk GSEA with limma contrasts and per-patient ssGSEA — RunGSEA_pseudobulk
text: Supply gene sets using exactly one following (priority order): gene_sets - named list character vectors, one vector per gene set. deg_df - differential expression data frame; top DEGs per group extracted used gene sets (see deg_* parameters). pathway_sets (default) - one MSigDB collections fetched via msigdbr. require gene_sets deg_df.

title: Run inferCNV copy-number inference — RunInferCNV
text: Wraps full inferCNV workflow: gene-order table generation (via AnnotateFeatures() / GetGeneOrder()), downsampling, object creation, inference, post-processing map_metadata_from_infercnv.txt, standard figure output. Returns Seurat object new metadata columns: CNV_score, CNV_Prediction, infercnv_subcluster, one proportion_cnv_chrN column per chromosome.

title: Run inferCNV copy-number inference — RunInferCNV
code: RunInferCNV(   seurat_object,   cell_type_col = "Assignment",   ref_group_names,   output_dir = NULL,   species = NULL,   chromosomes = NULL,   gene_biotype = "protein_coding",   annotation_file = NULL,   downsample = 200L,   cutoff = 0.1,   HMM = FALSE,   denoise = TRUE,   scale_data = TRUE,   cluster_by_groups = TRUE,   num_threads = 4L,   cnv_score_threshold = "auto",   cnv_k = 2,   resume = FALSE,   caffeinate = FALSE )

title: Run inferCNV copy-number inference — RunInferCNV
text: seurat_object Seurat object. cell_type_col Character. Metadata column containing cell-type labels used split cells define reference groups. Default "Assignment". ref_group_names Character vector. Cell-type labels treated normal (diploid) reference — typically immune stromal populations. output_dir Character NULL. Directory inferCNV output saved figures. Walks PrepObject() settings NULL. species Character. "mouse", "human", "zebrafish". Used auto-generate gene order via AnnotateFeatures() yet annotated. chromosomes Character numeric vector chromosomes analyze. NULL uses species-appropriate autosomes (mouse 1–19, human 1–22, zebrafish 1–25). gene_biotype Character. Biotype filter gene order table. Default "protein_coding". annotation_file Character NULL. Passed AnnotateFeatures() manual gene annotation preferred automatic sources. downsample Integer. Maximum cells per identity class. NULL uses cells (recommended large objects). Default 200L. cutoff Numeric. inferCNV expression cutoff. Use 0.1 10x Genomics 1 Smart-seq2. Default 0.1. HMM Logical. Run inferCNV's HMM subclonal CNV calls? Dramatically increases runtime. Default FALSE. denoise Logical. Apply inferCNV denoising. Default TRUE. scale_data Logical. Scale expression data inference. Default TRUE. cluster_by_groups Logical. Cluster cells within annotation groups. Default TRUE. num_threads Integer. Threads inferCNV. Default 4L. cnv_score_threshold Numeric "auto". "auto" (default), threshold set mean(ref_scores) + cnv_k × sd(ref_scores) using reference cells' CNV score distribution — data-driven approach Tirosh et al. 2016. Pass numeric value override. cnv_k Numeric. Number standard deviations reference mean used auto threshold. Higher values = conservative (fewer malignant calls). Default 2. resume Logical. TRUE output files already exist, skip re-running inferCNV go straight post-processing. Default FALSE. caffeinate Logical. Prevent machine sleeping run. Default FALSE.

title: Run inferCNV copy-number inference — RunInferCNV
text: input Seurat object additional metadata columns.

what: Single-cell ssGSEA with significance testing and heatmap visualization — RunSCssGSEA
title: Single-cell ssGSEA with significance testing and heatmap visualization — RunSCssGSEA
text: Runs per-cell single-sample Gene Set Enrichment Analysis (ssGSEA) Seurat object using GSVA (compatible GSVA v1 v2 APIs), computes group-level significance (ANOVA Kruskal-Wallis), saves enrichment scores significance tables CSVs, produces ComplexHeatmap PDF mean z-scored enrichment per group.

title: Single-cell ssGSEA with significance testing and heatmap visualization — RunSCssGSEA
code: RunSCssGSEA(   seurat_object,   group.by,   split.by = NULL,   subset_cells = FALSE,   subset_by = NULL,   subset_values = NULL,   gene_sets = NULL,   deg_df = NULL,   deg_gene_column = "feature",   deg_group_column = "group",   deg_fc_column = "logFC",   deg_padj_column = "padj",   deg_padj_cutoff = 0.05,   deg_top_n = 20,   gene_set_library = NULL,   gene_set_subcategory = NULL,   search_terms = NULL,   species = "Homo sapiens",   method = "gsva",   downsample = NULL,   min.size = 5,   ssgsea.norm = TRUE,   ucell_max_rank = 1500L,   cores = 1,   chunk_size = 1000L,   resume = FALSE,   fit = "ANOVA",   sig_group_by = NULL,   p_cutoff = 0.05,   show_only_significant = TRUE,   group_colors = NULL,   split_colors = NULL,   heatmap_params = list(row_names_side = "left", show_row_dend = FALSE,     row_names_max_width = grid::unit(15, "cm")),   heatmap_colors = NULL,   add_boxplots = TRUE,   add_pvalues = TRUE,   add_to_object = FALSE,   output_dir,   object_name = "Analysis",   subset_name = "",   caffeinate = FALSE )

title: Single-cell ssGSEA with significance testing and heatmap visualization — RunSCssGSEA
text: seurat_object Seurat object. group.Character. Metadata column used primary grouping variable heatmap columns significance testing (e.g. "Assignment", "celltype"). split.Character NULL. Optional secondary metadata column splitting heatmap columns (e.g. "Health", "Condition"). set, significance also tested within split level addition pooled test. subset_cells Logical. TRUE, subset Seurat object subset_values scoring. Default FALSE. subset_by Character NULL. Metadata column used subsetting (required subset_cells = TRUE). NULL treats subset_values cell barcodes directly. subset_values Character vector. Values retain subset_by, cell barcodes subset_by = NULL. gene_sets Named list gene vectors (Mode 1). NULL using another mode. deg_df Data frame DEG results (Mode 2). NULL otherwise. Compatible standard outputs presto::wilcoxauc(), edgeR, table gene, group, log-fold-change columns. deg_gene_column Character. Column deg_df holding gene symbols. Default "feature". deg_group_column Character scalar length-2 character vector. Column(s) deg_df defining gene-set groups. One column → one gene set per unique value. Two columns → gene sets named "col1__NKSEP__col2"; heatmap rows split col1 automatically. Default "group". deg_fc_column Character. Log-fold-change column selecting top genes. Default "logFC". deg_padj_column Character NULL. Adjusted p-value column pre- filtering. NULL skips filtering. Default "padj". deg_padj_cutoff Numeric. Adjusted p-value threshold DEG pre-filtering. Default 0.05. deg_top_n Integer. Top N genes per group (deg_fc_column) include gene set. Default 20. gene_set_library Character NULL. MSigDB collection code (Mode 3 4). Common options: "H" - Hallmark (50 coherent biological processes) "C2" - Curated: canonical pathways (combine gene_set_subcategory = "CP:KEGG", "CP:REACTOME", "CP:WIKIPATHWAYS", "CP:BIOCARTA", "CP:PID") "C5" - Gene Ontology (subcategory = "GO:BP", "GO:MF", "GO:CC") "C6" - Oncogenic signatures "C7" - Immunologic signatures (IMMUNESIGDB) "C8" - Cell-type signatures NULL fetches collections (slow; use search_terms). gene_set_subcategory Character NULL. MSigDB sub-collection code. Default NULL (entire collection). search_terms Search filter applied MSigDB gene-set names (case- insensitive, regex). Accepts two forms: Character vector - search. gene set whose name matches least one term kept: search_terms = c("APOPTOSIS", "CELL_DEATH"). List character vectors - /search. list element -group: terms element must appear pathway name (order separation matter). Results union () across -groups. Examples: list(c("T_CELL", "ACTIVATION")) - words name list(c("T_CELL", "ACTIVATION"), "APOPTOSIS") - previous APOPTOSIS list(c("T_CELL","ACTIVATION"), c("B_CELL","DIFFERENTIATION")) - two independent -groups, union results NULL (default) keeps gene sets fetched collection. species Character. Species MSigDB fetching. Passed msigdbr::msigdbr(). Default "Homo sapiens". Use "Mus musculus" mouse datasets. See msigdbr::msigdbr_species() supported species. method Character. Scoring backend. "gsva" (default) uses GSVA package's ssGSEA algorithm; "ucell" uses UCell package's rank-based U statistic, faster memory-efficient large datasets produce normalized enrichment scores. downsample Integer NULL. Maximum cells per group.identity use scoring. ssGSEA \(O(n_{\text{cells}})\) - downsampling 500-2000 cells per group dramatically reduces runtime minimal loss group-level signal. NULL uses cells. Default NULL. min.size Integer. Minimum gene-set size intersecting expression matrix. Gene sets threshold silently dropped. Default 5. ssgsea.norm Logical. Normalize ssGSEA enrichment scores [0, 1] range? Default TRUE (matches escape::enrichIt default). Ignored method = "ucell". ucell_max_rank Integer. UCell : top ucell_max_rank genes per cell (expression rank) considered computing U statistic. Lower values reduce memory use. Default 1500L. cores Integer. Number parallel cores. method = "gsva", requires BiocParallel; falls back serial unavailable. method = "ucell", sets number UCell threads. Default 1. chunk_size Integer. UCell : number cells processed per parallel chunk. Tune balance memory speed. Default 1000L. resume Logical. TRUE combined-scores CSV checkpoint previous run exists output_dir, load skip scoring. Useful re-running plots adjusting significance thresholds. Default FALSE. fit Character. Significance test applied per pathway: "ANOVA" (default, parametric F-test) "Kruskal" (non-parametric Kruskal-Wallis). use BH adjustment multiple testing. sig_group_by Character NULL. Metadata column use grouping factor significance testing. NULL (default) uses group.. Override display grouping (group.) differs statistical grouping (e.g. display fine cell type, test broad disease category). p_cutoff Numeric. BH-adjusted p-value threshold selecting pathways display heatmap. Default 0.05. show_only_significant Logical. TRUE, heatmap shows pathways p_adj < p_cutoff. Falls back top 30 statistic pathways pass threshold. FALSE, pathways shown. Default TRUE. group_colors Named character vector colors group.levels. NULL (default) auto-generates colors Nour palette. split_colors Named character vector colors split.levels. NULL (default) auto-generates colors Nour palette. Ignored split.= NULL. heatmap_params Named list additional arguments forwarded ComplexHeatmap::Heatmap(). Overrides internal defaults row label placement, dendrogram display, row-name width. heatmap_colors circlize::colorRamp2 color function heatmap fill scale. NULL (default) uses 5-stop blue-white-red diverging palette breaks c(-2, -1, 0, 1, 2) (row z-scores): Supply colorRamp2 object override. breaks set used -- row z-score heatmaps, symmetric breaks around 0 recommended. Examples: Classic RdBu (3-stop): circlize::colorRamp2(c(-2, 0, 2), c("#2166ac", "white", "#b2182b")) Purple-green (PRGn): circlize::colorRamp2(c(-2, 0, 2), RColorBrewer::brewer.pal(3, "PRGn")) Viridis plasma (raw non-z-scored scores): circlize::colorRamp2(seq(0, 1, length.= 9), viridis::plasma(9)) Tighter scale subtle signals: circlize::colorRamp2(c(-1, -0.5, 0, 0.5, 1), c("#007dd1", "#b3d9f5", "white", "#f5c08a", "#ab3000")) add_boxplots Logical. TRUE (default), generate per-pathway boxplot pages showing per-cell score distributions across group.groups (split split.set). add_pvalues Logical. TRUE (default), add BH-adjusted p-value brackets boxplots using ggpubr::stat_compare_means(). Requires add_boxplots = TRUE. add_to_object Logical. FALSE (default), returns enrichment scores data frame invisibly modify Seurat object (scores saved CSV - recommended large datasets avoid metadata bloat). TRUE, adds one metadata column per gene set seurat_object@meta.data returns modified Seurat object. downsample also set, cells included downsampled scoring set receive NA. output_dir Character. Directory output files (created absent). object_name Character. First component output file name prefix. Default "Analysis". subset_name Character. Second component output file name prefix. Default "". caffeinate Logical. TRUE, prevents Mac sleeping run using caffeinate system command. Default FALSE.

title: Single-cell ssGSEA with significance testing and heatmap visualization — RunSCssGSEA
text: add_to_object = FALSE (default): invisibly returns data frame enrichment scores (cells × gene sets) additional columns group.split.identity. add_to_object = TRUE: invisibly returns Seurat object one metadata column per gene set. Files saved output_dir: <prefix> ssGSEA_scores.csv - per-cell enrichment scores + group labels <prefix> ssGSEA_significance.csv - per-pathway significance (pooled across cells) <prefix> ssGSEA_significance_by_<split.>.csv - per-pathway significance within split.level (split.set) <prefix> ssGSEA heatmap.pdf - ComplexHeatmap mean z-scored enrichment per group

title: Single-cell ssGSEA with significance testing and heatmap visualization — RunSCssGSEA
text: Unlike pseudobulk ssGSEA (see RunGSEA_pseudobulk()), function scores every cell individually rather aggregating sample first. appropriate meaningful biological replicates (sample IDs), want use single-cell resolution visualization. datasets proper replicates, pseudobulk ssGSEA statistically rigorous.

title: Single-cell ssGSEA with significance testing and heatmap visualization — RunSCssGSEA
text: Mode 1 - pre-built named list   Mode 2 - DEG data frame Converts marker/DEG table one gene set per group. deg_group_column argument can name one column (simple group, e.g. "celltype") two columns (e.g. c("celltype", "cytokine")). two columns supplied, gene set names built "celltype__NKSEP__cytokine" internally heatmap rows automatically split first column (celltype). Mode 3 - MSigDB collection   Mode 4 - MSigDB term search (fetch gene sets matching pattern)
code: gene_sets = list(SetA = c("GENE1", "GENE2"), SetB = c("GENE3")) gene_set_library = "H"                        # Hallmark gene_set_library = "C2", gene_set_subcategory = "CP:KEGG" search_terms = c("CELL_DEATH", "APOPTOSIS") # optionally restrict to a collection: gene_set_library = "C2", search_terms = "CELL_DEATH"

what: Upstream Regulator Analysis via decoupleR + CollecTRI — RunURA
title: Upstream Regulator Analysis via decoupleR + CollecTRI — RunURA
text: Infers transcription factor activity pseudobulk gene expression using decoupleR CollecTRI TF-target database.  Optionally tests differential TF activity one condition pairs (limma \(\geq 3\) donors per side; t-test otherwise).

title: Upstream Regulator Analysis via decoupleR + CollecTRI — RunURA
code: RunURA(   seurat_object,   group.by = NULL,   donor.by = NULL,   split.by = NULL,   contrast.by = NULL,   contrast = NULL,   group.levels = NULL,   assay = "RNA",   layer = "data",   use_pseudobulk = TRUE,   force = FALSE,   network = NULL,   method = "ulm",   organism = "human",   n_top = 25L,   min_cells = 10L,   output_dir = NULL,   object_name = "",   subset_name = "",   plot = TRUE,   caffeinate = FALSE,   max_cells = NULL )

title: Upstream Regulator Analysis via decoupleR + CollecTRI — RunURA
text: seurat_object Seurat object. group.Character NULL.  Metadata column defines primary grouping (e.g. "CellType", "Cluster"). NULL treats cells one group. donor.Character NULL.  Replicates column (e.g. "Donor.ID").  Enables limma-based differential testing \(\geq 3\) donors per contrast arm.  NULL falls back t-test. split.Character NULL.  Additional stratification column (e.g. "Sex").  Included pseudobulk grouping, generates group × split columns activity matrix, facets bubble plot. contrast.Character NULL.  Condition column DE (e.g. "Diagnosis"). contrast single c("test", "ref") pair list pairs, e.g. list(c("AD","Control"), c("MCI","Control")). Required contrast.set. group.levels Character vector NULL.  Restrict specific levels group..  NULL = levels. assay Character.  Seurat assay.  Default "RNA". layer Character.  Assay layer.  Default "data". use_pseudobulk Logical.  TRUE (default) TF activity inferred pseudobulk expression averages — statistically rigorous DE testing.  FALSE, decoupleR runs directly full single-cell matrix (cells × genes) activity scores averaged per group; simpler, works without donor., pseudoreplication means DE results interpreted caution. force Logical.  TRUE, recomputes pseudobulk even cached result already stored seurat_object@misc$pseudobulk. Default FALSE. network data.frame columns source, target, mor.  Fetched CollecTRI NULL. method One "ulm" (default), "viper", "mlm". organism "human" (default) "mouse". n_top Top N TFs include plots.  Default 25L. min_cells Minimum cells per pseudobulk group.  Default 10L. output_dir Directory PDFs.  NULL = files saved. object_name Character.  Label prefix output file names. subset_name Character.  Optional subset label appended output file names. plot Logical.  Save plots.  Default TRUE. caffeinate Logical.  Prevent machine sleeping run (macOS ; uses caffeinate -).  Default FALSE. max_cells Integer NULL.  use_pseudobulk = FALSE object cells value, stratified random sample max_cells cells drawn running decoupleR.  Preserves group proportions.  NULL (default) uses cells.

title: Upstream Regulator Analysis via decoupleR + CollecTRI — RunURA
text: Invisibly, list: activity TF × group mean-activity matrix. scores Long-format decoupleR output (pseudobulk samples). de Differential table, NULL.  Columns: TF, cell_type, split_level, mean_a, mean_b, activity_diff, t, p.value, p.adj, contrast. network TF-target network used.

what: Fast Wilcoxon Rank-Sum + AUC Marker Test (BPCells / Seurat-v5 Compatible) — RunWilcoxAUC
title: Fast Wilcoxon Rank-Sum + AUC Marker Test (BPCells / Seurat-v5 Compatible) — RunWilcoxAUC
text: drop-replacement presto::wilcoxauc(seurat_object, ...) works reliably BPCells-backed Seurat v5 multi-layer objects. standard presto::wilcoxauc.Seurat method extracts expression matrix internally way can error lazy matrices v5 layer syntax.  RunWilcoxAUC bypasses path: pulls matrix .get_layer_data() (handles BPCells lazy subsetting, Seurat v3 slot= vs v5 layer=, sparse → dgCMatrix coercion) calls presto::wilcoxauc(matrix, y) S3 dispatch lands wilcoxauc.default directly - internal ::: access needed.

title: Fast Wilcoxon Rank-Sum + AUC Marker Test (BPCells / Seurat-v5 Compatible) — RunWilcoxAUC
code: RunWilcoxAUC(   seurat_object,   group.by = NULL,   groups_use = NULL,   assay = "RNA",   layer = "data",   caffeinate = FALSE,   ... )

title: Fast Wilcoxon Rank-Sum + AUC Marker Test (BPCells / Seurat-v5 Compatible) — RunWilcoxAUC
text: seurat_object Seurat object. group.Character. Metadata column whose levels define comparison groups (e.g. "Cluster", "Assignment"). NULL, Idents used. groups_use Character vector NULL. Restrict test subset group.levels.  NULL = levels. assay Character. Assay extract. Default "RNA". layer Character. Layer / slot extract. Default "data" (log-normalized counts, recommended AUC statistics). "counts" uses raw counts; "scale.data" uses scaled values (note: negative values make AUC less interpretable test still valid). caffeinate Logical. TRUE, prevents machine sleeping run (macOS ; uses caffeinate -). Default FALSE. ... Additional arguments forwarded wilcoxauc.

title: Fast Wilcoxon Rank-Sum + AUC Marker Test (BPCells / Seurat-v5 Compatible) — RunWilcoxAUC
text: data.frame one row per (feature × group) combination containing auc, pval, padj, logFC, pct_in, pct_out - standard presto output.

title: Fast Wilcoxon Rank-Sum + AUC Marker Test (BPCells / Seurat-v5 Compatible) — RunWilcoxAUC
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Basic usage - use active identity markers <- RunWilcoxAUC(SeuratObj)  # Specify a metadata column markers <- RunWilcoxAUC(SeuratObj, group.by = "Cluster")  # Only compare specific groups markers <- RunWilcoxAUC(SeuratObj, group.by = "Cluster",                          groups_use = c("Microglia", "Astrocytes"))  # Top markers per cluster library(dplyr) top_markers <- markers |>   filter(padj < 0.05, logFC > 0.5, auc > 0.6) |>   group.by(group) |>   slice_max(auc, n = 20) } # }

title: Split cells into expression groups per cell type — SplitByGene
code: SplitByGene(   seurat_object,   gene,   group_by,   split = c("zero_vs_expressed", "quantile", "expressed_quantile"),   quantile_range = c(0.25, 0.75),   assay = NULL,   layer = "data",   col_name = NULL )

title: Split cells into expression groups per cell type — SplitByGene
text: seurat_object Seurat object. gene Character. Gene split . group_by Character. Cell-type identity column. split Character. "zero_vs_expressed" (default), "quantile", "expressed_quantile". quantile_range Numeric length-2. Quantile bounds two threshold modes. Default c(0.25, 0.75). assay Character NULL. Assay use. layer Character. Layer/slot. Default "data". col_name Character NULL. New metadata column name. Defaults "<gene>_split".

title: Split cells into expression groups per cell type — SplitByGene
text: Seurat object new metadata column. Prints count table.

title: Split cells into expression groups per cell type — SplitByGene
text: Split modes "zero_vs_expressed" GOI = 0 → "zero"; GOI > 0 → "expressed". Cleanest pseudobulk: maps donor-level proportion. "quantile" Bottom quantile_range[1] → "low"; top 1 - quantile_range[2] → "high"; middle → NA (excluded). "expressed_quantile" "quantile" among GOI > 0 cells . Zeros → NA. Avoids dropout low group can confound library size - regress nCount_RNA downstream.

Columns fraction values NA flagged high-missingness. show_all Logical (default FALSE).  TRUE, prints every column including > max_levels unique values. Useful large data frames want see numeric columns .
