what: Kaplan-Meier survival plots for bulk expression data — SurvPlot
title: Kaplan-Meier survival plots for bulk expression data — SurvPlot
text: Generates publication-ready Kaplan-Meier survival plots one genes gene-set signatures bulk RNA-seq microarray expression data.  function two input modes: TCGA mode — supply tcga_project function downloads, processes, caches expression matrix clinical metadata automatically via TCGAbiolinks.  Subsequent calls project load local cache instantly. Custom matrix mode — supply expr_mat (genes × samples) meta (samples × variables) data frames. path non-TCGA cohorts Cavalli medulloblastoma dataset, PBTA, -house bulk RNA-seq study. Samples classified High / Low (Q1–Q4) using chosen split_method, KM curves drawn log-rank p-values. Results can faceted across two clinical variables simultaneously (e.g. sex rows, tumour subtype columns) producing one grid page per gene/signature.  sidecar CSV records every cutoff, group size, p-value full reproducibility.

title: Kaplan-Meier survival plots for bulk expression data — SurvPlot
code: SurvPlot(   expr_mat = NULL,   meta = NULL,   tcga_project = NULL,   tcga_data_type = c("rnaseq", "microarray"),   tcga_backend = c("recount3", "TCGAbiolinks"),   tcga_cache_dir = NULL,   genes = NULL,   gene_lists = NULL,   time_col = "OS",   status_col = "status",   split_method = c("median", "quartile", "value", "optimal"),   split_value = NULL,   split_per_group = TRUE,   facet_row = NULL,   facet_col = NULL,   output_file = NULL,   pdf_width = NULL,   pdf_height = NULL,   palette = c("Red", "Blue"),   quartile_palette = c("#2166AC", "#92C5DE", "#F4A582", "#D6604D"),   show_pval = TRUE,   show_n = TRUE,   write_sidecar = TRUE,   min_group_n = 5L,   multivariate_covariates = NULL )

title: Kaplan-Meier survival plots for bulk expression data — SurvPlot
text: expr_mat Numeric matrix, genes × samples.  Rownames must gene symbols; colnames must sample identifiers matching rownames(meta). Values log scale (e.g. log2 TPM + 1 log2 FPKM + 1). Supply either + meta, tcga_project. meta Data frame sample-level clinical metadata.  Rownames must match colnames(expr_mat).  Must contain minimum columns named time_col status_col. tcga_project Character scalar.  TCGA (TARGET) project identifier. See TCGA projects section full list available IDs.  Requires TCGAbiolinks installed: BiocManager::install("TCGAbiolinks").  Data downloaded cached .rds file; subsequent calls load cache. tcga_data_type Data modality download GDC.  One : "rnaseq" (default) STAR-aligned RNA-seq counts (workflow.type = "STAR - Counts").  TPM values extracted log2-transformed (log2(TPM + 1)).  Falls back FPKM TPM unavailable. "microarray" Legacy microarray data via GDC legacy archive.  Use older TCGA accessions pre-date RNA-seq, external cohorts processed Affymetrix / Illumina arrays (e.g. Cavalli medulloblastoma dataset). tcga_backend Download engine TCGA / TARGET data.  One : "recount3" (default) Downloads one pre-consolidated genes × samples count matrix per project — single file, per-patient downloads.  Counts converted log2(TPM + 1).  Requires recount3: BiocManager::install("recount3").  Automatically switches "TCGAbiolinks" tcga_data_type = "microarray". "TCGAbiolinks" Downloads one file per patient GDC API (e.g. 391 files TCGA-GBM).  Much slower prone partial-download crashes.  Use recount3 carry project, microarray data. tcga_cache_dir Path directory downloaded data cached .rds files.  Defaults OS user cache directory scSidekick (tools::R_user_dir("scSidekick", "cache")).  Set shared network path allow multiple users reuse cache. Delete .rds file force fresh download. genes Character vector gene symbols analyse single-gene mode.  gene produces one set KM panels.  Genes absent expr_mat trigger warning skipped. gene_lists Named list character vectors signature-score mode. element gene set; name becomes panel title.  Scores mean log2 expression genes present matrix.  Example: list(YAP_up = c("PRC1","HPCA"), YAP_dn = c("ZIC1","CHD7")). time_col Name survival time column meta (standardised TCGA metadata).  TCGA downloads "OS" (overall survival days).  disease-specific survival use column produced preprocessing pipeline. status_col Name event indicator column.  Must numeric: 1 = event occurred (death / recurrence), 0 = censored. TCGA downloads "status" (standardised vital_status SurvPlot automatically). split_method Strategy classifying samples expression groups. One "median" (default), "quartile", "value", "optimal".  See Splitting strategies section full details option. split_value Numeric cutoff used split_method = "value". Samples expression / score \(\geq\) split_value labelled High; others Low.  Commonly set 0 gene-set enrichment scores. split_per_group Logical (default TRUE).  TRUE, threshold (median / quantiles) computed independently within facet group.  ensures 50/50 split within every subtype sex strata, preventing globally-low subgroup assigned entirely "Low".  Set FALSE use single global threshold across samples (equivalent splitting per group). facet_row Name column meta whose levels define rows output grid.  Common choices: "gender", "race", "age_group".  Set NULL (default) row faceting. facet_col Name column meta whose levels define columns output grid.  Common choices: "Subgroup", "paper_IDH.status", "tumor_stage".  Set NULL (default) column faceting. output_file Output path.  Two modes depending path: Ends .pdf genes written pages single multi-page PDF. end .pdf Treated directory.  One PDF per gene/signature written (<gene>_survival.pdf), plus one shared survival_sidecar.csv.  directory created exist. Set NULL (default) suppress file output return plots invisibly manipulation. pdf_width, pdf_height Numeric.  PDF dimensions inches.  NULL (default) dimensions auto-calculated number genes facet levels: approximately 4.5 per column 4.5 per row. palette Character vector length \(\geq 2\).  Colours High Low groups respectively.  Default: c("Red", "Blue").  R colour name hex code accepted. quartile_palette Character vector length \(\geq 4\).  Colours Q1 Q4 split_method = "quartile".  Default blue--red diverging scale: c("#2166AC", "#92C5DE", "#F4A582", "#D6604D"). show_pval Logical (default TRUE).  Display log-rank p-value KM panel. show_n Logical (default TRUE).  Append group sample size legend label, e.g. "High (n = 47)". write_sidecar Logical (default TRUE).  Write companion CSV file recording cutoff, group sizes, split method, log-rank p-value every panel.  Ignored output_file = NULL.  See Sidecar CSV section column definitions. min_group_n Integer (default 5).  Minimum number samples required group splitting.  Panels group falls threshold skipped recorded NA sidecar. multivariate_covariates Character vector meta column names include covariates multivariate Cox proportional hazards model. supplied, forest plot (survminer::ggforest) printed additional page KM panels gene / signature.  Cox model uses continuous expression values (High/Low labels) feature term, powerful treating binary predictor.  samples complete data across expression feature covariates used (faceting Cox).  Example: c("gender", "age_at_index", "paper_IDH.status").  Set NULL (default) skip multivariate analysis.

title: Kaplan-Meier survival plots for bulk expression data — SurvPlot
text: Invisibly returns named list two elements: plots Named list, one entry per gene/signature.  entry list ggsurvplot objects (one per facet combination), NULL skipped panels. sidecar Data frame one row per KM panel documenting feature name, facet levels, cutoff, group sizes, log-rank p-value.

text: Pass following strings tcga_project.  IDs follow format "TCGA-<CODE>". Brain / CNS "TCGA-GBM" Glioblastoma multiforme (n ≈ 173) "TCGA-LGG" Brain Lower Grade Glioma (n ≈ 516) Breast "TCGA-BRCA" Breast invasive carcinoma (n ≈ 1 098) Lung "TCGA-LUAD" Lung adenocarcinoma (n ≈ 585) "TCGA-LUSC" Lung squamous cell carcinoma (n ≈ 504) Colorectal "TCGA-COAD" Colon adenocarcinoma (n ≈ 521) "TCGA-READ" Rectum adenocarcinoma (n ≈ 177) Liver / Biliary "TCGA-LIHC" Liver hepatocellular carcinoma (n ≈ 377) "TCGA-CHOL" Cholangiocarcinoma (n ≈ 51) Kidney "TCGA-KIRC" Kidney renal clear cell carcinoma (n ≈ 611) "TCGA-KIRP" Kidney renal papillary cell carcinoma (n ≈ 321) "TCGA-KICH" Kidney chromophobe (n ≈ 113) Gynaecological "TCGA-OV" Ovarian serous cystadenocarcinoma (n ≈ 608) "TCGA-UCEC" Uterine corpus endometrial carcinoma (n ≈ 587) "TCGA-UCS" Uterine carcinosarcoma (n ≈ 57) "TCGA-CESC" Cervical squamous cell carcinoma (n ≈ 309) Skin "TCGA-SKCM" Skin cutaneous melanoma (n ≈ 473) "TCGA-UVM" Uveal melanoma (n ≈ 80) Gastrointestinal / Pancreatic "TCGA-STAD" Stomach adenocarcinoma (n ≈ 443) "TCGA-ESCA" Esophageal carcinoma (n ≈ 185) "TCGA-PAAD" Pancreatic adenocarcinoma (n ≈ 185) Thoracic "TCGA-MESO" Mesothelioma (n ≈ 87) "TCGA-THYM" Thymoma (n ≈ 124) Head Neck / Thyroid "TCGA-HNSC" Head neck squamous cell carcinoma (n ≈ 528) "TCGA-THCA" Thyroid carcinoma (n ≈ 507) Haematological "TCGA-LAML" Acute myeloid leukaemia (n ≈ 200) "TCGA-DLBC" Diffuse large B-cell lymphoma (n ≈ 48) Sarcoma / Mesenchymal "TCGA-SARC" Sarcoma (n ≈ 265) Urological "TCGA-BLCA" Bladder urothelial carcinoma (n ≈ 433) "TCGA-PRAD" Prostate adenocarcinoma (n ≈ 551) "TCGA-TGCT" Testicular germ cell tumours (n ≈ 156) Endocrine "TCGA-ACC" Adrenocortical carcinoma (n ≈ 92) "TCGA-PCPG" Pheochromocytoma paraganglioma (n ≈ 187) Paediatric (TARGET programme) "TARGET-AML" Acute myeloid leukaemia "TARGET-NBL" Neuroblastoma "TARGET-OS" Osteosarcoma "TARGET-WT" Wilms tumour "TARGET-RT" Rhabdoid tumour "TARGET-CCSK" Clear cell sarcoma kidney

title: Kaplan-Meier survival plots for bulk expression data — SurvPlot
text: download, SurvPlot standardises OS (overall survival days, combining days_to_death days_to_last_follow_up) status (1 = deceased, 0 = living/censored).  Common columns available facet_row / facet_col include: gender Reported sex ("male" / "female") race Self-reported race age_at_index Age diagnosis (years) tumor_stage / pathologic_stage Pathological stage paper_* TCGA marker-paper columns, e.g. paper_IDH.status, paper_Histology, paper_CIMP.status, paper_Pan.Glioma.RNA.Expression.Cluster Available columns vary cancer type.  inspect available first download run: colnames(result$sidecar) examine meta returned list.

title: Kaplan-Meier survival plots for bulk expression data — SurvPlot
text: "median" (default) Splits samples median expression / score value High Low groups.  split_per_group = TRUE (recommended), median computed independently within facet cell, subgroup uniformly low expression still split 50/50 — matching approach used published survival analyses. "quartile" Divides samples four equally-sized groups (Q1 = lowest 25\ four KM curves per panel coloured quartile_palette.  Useful detecting non-linear dose–response relationships. "value" Splits user-supplied fixed numeric threshold (split_value).  Applies cutoff across groups facets.  Set split_value = 0 gene-set enrichment scores computed gene_lists mode (positive score = enriched). "optimal" Uses survminer::surv_cutpoint() find threshold maximises log-rank statistic.  Computationally intensive prone overfitting small cohorts; recommended discovery.  Falls back "median" optimisation fails.  Requires survminer \(\geq\) 0.4.9.

title: Kaplan-Meier survival plots for bulk expression data — SurvPlot
text: gene_lists supplied instead genes, function computes per-sample score gene set mean log2 expression across genes set present matrix.  mean-expression scorer used internally PlotPathwayButterfly; additional packages required.  Missing genes silently dropped warning issued 50\ enrichment-style interpretation, use split_method = "value" split_value = 0 (score \(\geq\) 0 = enriched) split_method = "median" (top half = enriched).

title: Kaplan-Meier survival plots for bulk expression data — SurvPlot
text: faceting genes / signatures appear single row one page (one PDF file per gene directory mode). facet_row Panels stacked vertically, one row per level row variable. facet_row + facet_col One page per gene/signature. Panels arranged (n\_row\_levels × n\_col\_levels) grid.  Cells fewer 2 × min_group_n samples left blank preserve alignment.  PDF dimensions auto-scaled grid size unless overridden pdf_width / pdf_height.

title: Kaplan-Meier survival plots for bulk expression data — SurvPlot
text: Single PDF Set output_file path ending .pdf (e.g. "~/results/survival.pdf").  genes appear separate pages one file. Directory Set output_file folder path (existing new; must end .pdf).  One PDF per gene/signature written, named <gene>_survival.pdf.  single survival_sidecar.csv covering genes written folder.

title: Kaplan-Meier survival plots for bulk expression data — SurvPlot
text: Written alongside every PDF output, sidecar records one row per KM panel following columns: feature Gene name signature name facet_row, facet_col Level values ("" faceting) split_method Method actually used (may differ request fallback occurred) cutoff Threshold applied; "quartile" semicolon-separated list five quantile boundaries n_total Total samples subset n_High, n_Low (n_Q1–n_Q4) Samples per group pval Log-rank p-value

title: Kaplan-Meier survival plots for bulk expression data — SurvPlot
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # ── Example 1: TCGA-GBM, two genes, sex × IDH status grid ──────────────── SurvPlot(   tcga_project = "TCGA-GBM",   genes        = c("YAP1", "S100A4"),   time_col     = "OS",   status_col   = "status",   facet_row    = "gender",   facet_col    = "paper_IDH.status",   output_file  = "~/Desktop/GBM_survival/"   # directory → one PDF per gene )  # ── Example 2: TCGA-GBM, quartile split, all in one PDF ────────────────── SurvPlot(   tcga_project = "TCGA-GBM",   genes        = c("YAP1", "CD276", "CD47"),   split_method = "quartile",   output_file  = "~/Desktop/GBM_quartiles.pdf" )  # ── Example 3: Gene-set signature scores, value split at 0 ─────────────── SurvPlot(   tcga_project = "TCGA-LGG",   gene_lists   = list(     YAP_Activated = c("PRC1", "HPCA", "SRSF7", "TIMM17A", "PIM1"),     YAP_Repressed = c("ZIC1", "ZIC2", "CHD7", "KIF5C", "DPYSL2")   ),   split_method  = "value",   split_value   = 0,       # >= 0 = enriched   facet_row     = "gender",   facet_col     = "paper_Pan.Glioma.RNA.Expression.Cluster",   output_file   = "~/Desktop/LGG_signatures/" )  # ── Example 4: Custom matrix (e.g. Cavalli medulloblastoma) ────────────── Cav.EXP  <- t(read.table("Cavalli_expression.txt", header = TRUE,                            row.names = 1)) Cav.meta <- read.table("Cavalli_pheno.txt", header = TRUE, row.names = 1) # Cavalli metadata uses "survival" and "status" already res <- SurvPlot(   expr_mat     = Cav.EXP,   meta         = Cav.meta,   genes        = c("YAP1", "CD276", "CD47", "CCL5"),   time_col     = "survival",   status_col   = "status",   facet_row    = "Gender",   facet_col    = "Subgroup",   split_method = "median",   output_file  = "~/Desktop/Cavalli_survival/" ) # Inspect the sidecar head(res$sidecar)  # ── Example 5: Paediatric cancer via TARGET ─────────────────────────────── SurvPlot(   tcga_project  = "TARGET-NBL",   genes         = c("MYCN", "ALK"),   time_col      = "OS",   status_col    = "status",   split_method  = "optimal",   output_file   = "~/Desktop/NBL_survival.pdf" )  # ── Example 6: No faceting, return plots for manual arrangement ─────────── res <- SurvPlot(   tcga_project = "TCGA-SKCM",   genes        = "S100A4",   split_method = "median" ) # Access the ggsurvplot object for the first panel print(res$plots[["S100A4"]][[1]]) } # }

what: Visualize Leading-Edge Genes from GSEA Results — VisualizeLeadingEdge
title: Visualize Leading-Edge Genes from GSEA Results — VisualizeLeadingEdge
text: Loads one GSEA result CSV files (RunGSEA RunGSEA_pseudobulk), searches pathways keyword exact name, extracts union leading-edge genes, produces per-cell expression heatmap. Optionally, DEG data frame supplied, leading-edge genes intersected significant DEGs additional diagnostic plots produced (second heatmap, dotplot, stacked violins, boxplots).

title: Visualize Leading-Edge Genes from GSEA Results — VisualizeLeadingEdge
code: VisualizeLeadingEdge(   seurat_object,   gsea_files,   search_terms = NULL,   pathways = NULL,   group.by,   split.by = NULL,   assay = "RNA",   layer = "data",   min_expr = 0.05,   subset_cells = FALSE,   subset_by = NULL,   subset_values = NULL,   deg_df = NULL,   deg_gene_column = "feature",   deg_group_column = "group",   deg_fc_column = "logFC",   deg_padj_column = "padj",   deg_padj_cutoff = 0.05,   deg_top_n = 20,   add_dotplot = TRUE,   add_violin = TRUE,   add_boxplots = TRUE,   add_pvalues = TRUE,   add_feature_maps = FALSE,   heatmap_type = c("cell", "group"),   heatmap_column_split = NULL,   show_pathway_annotation = TRUE,   group_colors = NULL,   split_colors = NULL,   heatmap_params = list(row_names_side = "left", show_row_dend = FALSE,     row_names_max_width = grid::unit(15, "cm")),   output_dir,   object_name = "Analysis",   subset_name = "" )

title: Visualize Leading-Edge Genes from GSEA Results — VisualizeLeadingEdge
text: seurat_object Seurat object. gsea_files Character vector CSV file paths (output RunGSEA RunGSEA_pseudobulk). Must contain pathway leadingEdge columns. search_terms Keyword(s) filter pathways. See Pathway search . pathways Exact pathway names select (alternative search_terms). group.Metadata column primary grouping (required). Used column annotation, dotplot, violin grouping. split.Optional second metadata column column annotation violin splitting. assay Assay pull expression (default "RNA"). layer Layer pull expression (default "data"). min_expr Genes whose row-mean expression threshold excluded heatmap (default 0.05). subset_cells Logical; TRUE, subset seurat_object subset_values subset_by analysis. subset_by Metadata column used subsetting (requires subset_cells = TRUE). subset_values Character vector values retain subset_by. deg_df Optional DEG data frame (e.g. presto FindMarkers). provided, leading-edge genes intersected significant DEGs additional plots generated. deg_gene_column Column deg_df gene names (default "feature"). deg_group_column Column deg_df group labels (default "group"). deg_fc_column Column deg_df log-fold change (default "logFC"). deg_padj_column Column deg_df adjusted p-value (default "padj"). deg_padj_cutoff Adjusted p-value cut-(default 0.05). deg_top_n Top N DEGs per group (logFC) bonus plots (default 20). add_dotplot Logical; produce Seurat::DotPlot top DEGs (default TRUE; requires deg_df). add_violin Logical; produce stacked violin plots top DEGs (default TRUE; requires deg_df). add_boxplots Logical; produce boxplots top DEGs (default TRUE; requires deg_df). add_pvalues Logical; overlay Kruskal-Wallis p-value boxplots via ggpubr::stat_compare_means (default TRUE). add_feature_maps Logical; produce per-gene FeaturePlot PDFs top DEG genes (default FALSE; can slow large gene sets). heatmap_type Character. Either "cell" (default) per-cell heatmap cells ordered group.(split.), "group" compact group-mean heatmap one column per group.x split.combination. heatmap_column_split Character NULL. Metadata column used split heatmap columns labelled sections (e.g. diagnosis timepoint variable). applies heatmap_type = "cell". Default NULL. show_pathway_annotation Logical. TRUE (default), colored annotation bar drawn right heatmap showing source pathway gene belongs . Suppressed automatically one pathway matched. group_colors Named character vector colors group.levels. Auto-assigned Nour_pal("") NULL. split_colors Named character vector colors split.levels. Auto-assigned Nour_pal("spectrum") NULL. heatmap_params Named list additional arguments forwarded ComplexHeatmap::Heatmap(). key supplied overrides default. output_dir Directory output files saved. object_name Label used output file names subfolder (default "Analysis"). subset_name Optional label appended output file names (default "").

title: Visualize Leading-Edge Genes from GSEA Results — VisualizeLeadingEdge
text: Invisibly returns named list: genes_all leading-edge genes present Seurat object (min_expr filtering). genes_top Top DEG genes within leading edge, NULL deg_df provided. top_degs Filtered DEG data frame, NULL.

title: Visualize Leading-Edge Genes from GSEA Results — VisualizeLeadingEdge
text: search_terms character vector logic - term matches, case-insensitive. search_terms list character vectors element -group (terms must appear pathway name); results 'd across elements. pathways Exact pathway names (alternative search_terms).

what: Build a PowerPoint summary from an analysis output folder — create_analysis_pptx
title: Build a PowerPoint summary from an analysis output folder — create_analysis_pptx
text: Collects PDFs output_dir, converts images magick::image_read_pdf(), groups labelled sections based filename patterns, assembles polished .pptx via officer package.

title: Build a PowerPoint summary from an analysis output folder — create_analysis_pptx
code: create_analysis_pptx(   output_dir,   params_json = NULL,   out_pptx = NULL,   slide_format = "standard",   slide_width = NULL,   slide_height = NULL,   pdf_density = 150,   include_legends = TRUE,   legend_font_size = 8,   include = NULL,   exclude = NULL )

title: Build a PowerPoint summary from an analysis output folder — create_analysis_pptx
text: output_dir Character. Folder containing PDF figures. params_json Character NULL. Path analysis_params.json. NULL (default) function walks directory tree output_dir finds file - lets call create_analysis_pptx sub-folder (e.g., CellChat GSEA output directory) still get shared analysis parameters parent folder. out_pptx Character NULL.  save .pptx.  NULL (default) filename auto-generated dataset/subset labels current date, saved inside output_dir. slide_format Character. Slide aspect ratio preset: "standard" (4:3, 10 × 7.5 , default) "widescreen" (16:9, 13.33 × 7.5 ). Override explicit slide_width / slide_height needed. slide_width, slide_height Numeric. Explicit slide dimensions inches. provided override slide_format. pdf_density Integer. DPI used rasterising PDFs.  Increase (e.g. 200) sharper figures cost speed. Default 150. include_legends Logical. TRUE (default), legend sentence placed figure slide, sourced .legend sidecar file beside PDF auto-generated fallback templates. legend_font_size Numeric. Font size points legend text box slide. Default 8. include Character vector NULL. Section names include; sections dropped. NULL (default) sections included. PDFs assigned sections filename pattern matching - see .pptx_section_rules rules. Valid section names: "Quality Control" - QC density plots, doublet scores, filtration barplots "Dimensionality Reduction" - PCA elbow, UMAP /Harmony, RNA UMAPs "Cluster Markers" - ComplexHeatmap heatmaps, presto marker tables "Dotplots" - PDF "dotplot" filename "Cell Type Annotation" - AutoAssignment UMAPs, SingleR, scType, featureheatmaps, assignment helper "UMAPs" - combined multi-panel UMAP PDF "Composition" - barplots, chord diagrams, rose/trend plots, iteration--bar figures "Feature Maps" - GenerateFeatureMaps per-gene UMAPs "Pathway Analysis" - GSEA heatmaps lollipop PDFs "CellChat" - RunCellChat, CompareCellChat, RankCellChatPathways output PDFs "" - anything matched rules exclude Character vector NULL. Section names drop (applied include). NULL excludes nothing. valid values include. Typical use: exclude = c("Feature Maps", "CellChat") keep main analysis compact running separate sub-folder PPTXs heavy sections.

title: Build a PowerPoint summary from an analysis output folder — create_analysis_pptx
text: Invisibly returns path saved .pptx file.

Subset label (e.g. "All_Clusters"). assay_type Character. Sequencing modality. Controls word used individual observations auto-generated legends methods text. Recognized values (case-sensitive): "scRNAseq" → "single-cell RNA-seq" / "cells" (default) "snRNAseq" → "single-nucleus RNA-seq" / "nuclei" "scATACseq" → "single-cell ATAC-seq" / "cells" "scMultiome" → "single-cell multiome" / "cells" "Spatial" → "spatial transcriptomics" / "spots" "Visium" → "Visium spatial transcriptomics" / "spots" "VisiumHD" → "Visium HD spatial transcriptomics" / "bins" "Xenium" → "Xenium situ transcriptomics" / "cells" string passed -(long name) "cells" observation word. params_json Character. Full path JSON file.  Defaults file.path(output_dir, "analysis_params.json").

what: Start the scSidekick passive session logger — start_session_logger
title: Start the scSidekick passive session logger — start_session_logger
text: Attaches R task callback silently logs every top-level expression evaluated session, together status (OK / ERR), timestamp, working directory.  ggplot2 figures auto-saved PNGs whenever new plot detected; base-R graphics captured plot.new.

title: Start the scSidekick passive session logger — start_session_logger
code: start_session_logger(log_root = NULL, quiet = FALSE)

title: Start the scSidekick passive session logger — start_session_logger
text: log_root Character.  Parent directory log files. Defaults ~/.R/session_logs. quiet Logical.  FALSE (default) prints startup message showing log file path.

title: Start the scSidekick passive session logger — start_session_logger
text: Invisibly returns path log file.

title: Start the scSidekick passive session logger — start_session_logger
text: Logs written <log_root>/<session_id>_session.log plots <log_root>/<session_id>_plots/.  Call summarize_r_session() afterwards generate markdown report clean reconstructed R script. Recommended usage: add following line ~/.Rprofile logger starts automatically every new R session:
code: if (requireNamespace("scSidekick", quietly = TRUE)) scSidekick::start_session_logger()

what: Summarize an R session log — summarize_r_session
title: Summarize an R session log — summarize_r_session
text: Parses structured session log created start_session_logger() (plain .Rhistory file) writes two files output_dir:

title: Summarize an R session log — summarize_r_session
code: summarize_r_session(   log_file = NULL,   rhistory = NULL,   output_dir = NULL,   open = TRUE )

title: Summarize an R session log — summarize_r_session
text: log_file Character NULL.  Path .log file written start_session_logger().  NULL (default) recent log ~/.R/session_logs/ used. rhistory Character NULL.  Path .Rhistory file summarize instead structured log.  Takes precedence log_file. output_dir Character NULL.  Directory output files. Defaults directory containing log_file (rhistory), current working directory. open Logical.  TRUE (default) session interactive, opens markdown file editor writing.

title: Summarize an R session log — summarize_r_session
text: Invisibly returns named list $md, $r, $data.

title: Summarize an R session log — summarize_r_session
text: session_YYYYMMDD_summary.md - markdown report phase--phase summaries, error counts, plot inventory. session_YYYYMMDD_clean.R   - deduplicated, phase-annotated R script containing successful commands.

title: scSidekick minimal ggplot2 theme — theme_NourMin
text: clean, publication-ready theme built ggplot2::theme_light(). Features: gridlines, light gray panel borders, centered titles, transparent strip backgrounds, compact margins. Use instead theme_min2() analysis scripts - identical.
